It is headed by Rashad Muhammad Al-Alimi and has a membership of eight members after having Abdrabbuh Mansur Hadi's resignation.

motions.m: B: That Western states should support local rebel groups (e.g. by funding them and giving them intel) as opposed to direct military intervention in other states
C: That the IMF's bail-out of Sri Lanka should be contingent on political reform
A: That we would prefer a world where all people believe in Afterlife A to Afterlife B
motions.i: A: 'Afterlife A': After a life well lived, the 'ethereal soul' is separated from the 'physical body' and joins an eternal, blissful community of souls and 'God' (no physical body) 'Afterlife B': After a life well lived the 'ethereal soul' is separated from the 'physical body' and leads a second life with rewards from the previous life carrying into the next

motions.m: B: That it is in the interests of the Catholic church for an 'extrovert' pope to succeed Pope Francis.
motions.i: B: Introvert' Catholics believe that the Church should remain discrete from the modern, secular world, keeping as close to tradition as possible, and being actively-countercultural. 'Extrovert' Catholics see the Church as responding to the needs of the world, and are more tolerant of making doctrinal change to align with changing social values. Pope Francis is very much an 'extrovert'.

motions.m: C: That secular democracies with substantial religious communities should allow a separate legal system for those communities (e.g. Syariah Law, Canon Law)
B: That western liberal democracies should allow the experimentation and creation of animal-human hybrids
motions.i: B: Japan has lifted a ban on the creation of animal-human hybrids by allowing scientists to conduct stem-cell experiments to grow human organs inside animals. China has also been experimenting with the transplant of human brain cells into animal subjects to achieve advanced cognition.

motions.m: C: That we believe people are more moral today than in the past
A: That we would allow doctors to override advance care directives
motions.i: A: An Advance Care Directive (ACD) is a legal document in which a person of sound mind specifies what actions should be taken for their health if they are no longer able to make decisions for themselves (because of illness or incapacity). Doctors are legally required to uphold the instructions of an ACD, irrespective of their professional medical opinions.

motions.m: B: That we support the Campbelltown value system
motions.i: B: Traditionally in Japan, virtuous losses tend to gain significantly more popular support than victories. This value system manifests in various contexts including sports, popular culture, education, the veneration of historical figures, and… just about anywhere else. We decided to call this the Campbelltown value system.

motions.m: A: That we regret the prevalence of malevolent AI in popular culture (eg-Skynet in Terminator, Ultron in the Avengers, etc)
C: Assuming the technology exists, that it is immoral to create simulated high-fidelity alternate universes.
motions.i: C: "In the beginning the Universe was created. This has made a lot of people very angry and been widely regarded as a bad move." - Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

motions.m: That the South Korean government should redirect resources currently used to help Koryo-in settle in their respective countries of residence, to efforts to resettle them in South Korea.
motions.i: Koryo-in (고려인) are Korean diasporas residing in post-Soviet countries, mainly in Central Asia. Koryo-ins moved to the Far East and Primorsky regions during the 1910s to organise resistance against Japanese colonisation, until the Soviet Union forcefully relocated them to other territories in 1937. There are around 500,000 Koryo-ins, most of whom still identify as Korean descendants. The South Korean government helps Koryo-ins abroad to settle in their respective countries of residence by providing foreign aid in education, job training, etc.

motions.m: That we oppose Australia's 501 policy.
motions.i: Australia's 501 policy refers to section 501 of the Migration Act 1958, which deals with the cancellation of visas on character grounds. Under the policy, the Australian government has the authority to cancel the visas of and deport non-citizens who fail the character test, regardless of how long they have lived in Australia. The policy is typically applied to individuals who have been sentenced to at least one year imprisonment.

motions.m: That we oppose countries adopting 'pull-back' measures to respond to the influx of irregular migrants.
motions.i: Irregular migrants are those who enter another country without official permission. In response to border crossings by irregular migrants, several countries adopt 'pull-back' measures, cooperating with migrants' countries of origin to stop them from exiting (e.g. providing support for border controls).

motions.m: That we regret self-denial in religion (e.g. celibacy, fasting, giving up material or sensory pleasures).
That, in multireligious states, schools should teach integrated religious education instead of separated religious education.
motions.i: In 'integrated religious education', all students are taught material encompassing many major religions. In 'separated religious education', students study different religions in distinct classrooms (i.e. Catholic students attend Catholic class, Buddhist students attend Buddhist class, etc.).

motions.m: That we regret the influence of the Filipino Catholic Church in the People Power Revolution.
motions.i: The 1986 People Power Revolution was a nonviolent protest that led to the end of Ferdinand Marcos Sr.'s 20-year dictatorship and the inauguration of Corazon Aquino as President of the Philippines. An estimated two million people participated in the revolution. Religious groups played a significant role in the protest, alongside opposition political parties, military defectors who opposed Marcos, and militant groups. Religious groups were led by then-Archbishop of Manila, Cardinal Jaime Sin, who called on Filipinos to surround the police and military headquarters to protect military defectors. Additionally, the Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) wrote a statement opposing Marcos, calling out the government's systematic voter disenfranchisement, widespread vote-buying, tampering of election results, and political violence.

motions.m: That the National Semiconductor Strategy is in the interests of Malaysia.
motions.i: Malaysia's recently announced National Semiconductor Strategy (NSS) aims to secure at least 500 billion ringgit (US$106 billion) in fresh semiconductor investments. To support the industry's expansion, the government will develop a global research and development hub, train 60,000 local engineers and set aside 25 billion ringgit (US$5.3 billion) for related incentives.

motions.m: That we would discontinue the Jakarta capital city relocation project.
motions.i: In 2019, the Indonesian government ratified a plan to move the capital of Indonesia from Jakarta, a city on the northwest coast of Java, to Nusantara, a planned city in Borneo.

motions.m: That it is in China's interests to abandon the CPEC project.
motions.i: The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) project is an infrastructure network connecting China to the Arabian Sea through Kashmir, which was first inaugurated in 2015. In March 2024, the project was faced with militant attacks targeting Chinese workers, investments, and sites of geostrategic significance. The attacks occurred amidst a surge in jihadist militant activity in the CPEC region of Pakistan bordering Afghanistan.

motions.m: That we prefer all human genome editing experiments to be approved by the WHO rather than domestic regulatory bodies.
motions.i: In 2018, the World Health Organization (WHO) constructed strict recommendations on institutional, national, regional and global governance mechanisms for human genome editing (e.g. the establishment of a database of all ongoing clinical trials involving human genome editing). The WHO recommendations were a consequence of the He Jiankui controversy, involving the unprecedented and unregulated birth of twin "designer babies". Currently, experiments on human genome editing do not require WHO approval, and are instead overseen by domestic laws and regulators (e.g. the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Authority (HFEA) in the UK).

motions.m: That we support a requirement for US state medical boards to be made up of a majority of non-doctors.
motions.i: US state medical boards are responsible for licencing doctors and investigating complaints made against them. Where a doctor is found to have committed malpractice, the board is empowered to discipline them, including through the suspension or revocation of their medical licences.

motions.m: That we support Microsoft's investment into OpenAI.
motions.i: Microsoft has made a series of investments in OpenAI totalling around US$13billion. Instead of receiving equity in OpenAI, the investment was structured as a "strategic partnership" in which Microsoft receives the exclusive right to integrate some of OpenAI's developments into Microsoft products.

motions.m: That we would not grant legal protections to the wishes of the dead.
That we support funding orders in class action litigation.
motions.i: In a class action lawsuit, one or more plaintiffs bring a lawsuit on behalf of a larger "class". Typically, plaintiffs sign a litigation funding agreement that sets out their legal representation's fees if successful. Plaintiffs may apply to the Court for a funding order, which requires all members of the class to contribute to these fees out of the proceeds of a judgment or settlement, regardless of whether they have signed the litigation funding agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, "funding order" refers to common fund orders, funding equalisation orders, group costs orders and all similar orders.

motions.m: That Māori should pursue redress for colonisation through novel legal claims rather than through the Waitangi Tribunal.
motions.i: Māori can seek redress for the injustices of colonisation in one of two ways. Since 1975, Māori have sought redress through the Waitangi Tribunal, a permanent Commission of Inquiry that is empowered to investigate alleged breaches of the Treaty of Waitangi (an agreement that protects Māori's right to self-determination). The Tribunal can make non-binding recommendations which form the basis of negotiations and a potential settlement between tribes (iwi) and the New Zealand Government. Settlements typically include an apology, financial compensation and the return of some land. Recently, some Māori have successfully achieved similar outcomes through novel legal claims, e.g. on the basis that the Government had a duty to protect Māori which they had not fulfilled. These claims have been successfully argued in overseas jurisdictions without similar tribunals.

motions.m: That we support Indonesia's revision of their green investment rulebook.
motions.i: Indonesia's green investment rulebook categorises types of projects based on their alignment with Indonesia's net-zero emissions goal by 2060. It is used to decide the level of government support (e.g. tax breaks, subsidies) that projects receive. In February 2024, Indonesia revised the rulebook to categorise coal-fired power plants for nickel facilities as "green" (i.e. aligned with net-zero 2060) and captive coal power plants and mining of critical minerals as "amber" (i.e. could support the transition to net-zero if they meet specific criteria). Previously, these projects were not considered to contribute to the net-zero transition.

motions.m: That we support the proposed expansion of the public interest test.
motions.i: When a business in the UK is subject to a foreign takeover, the takeover must be approved by the government using the 'public interest test'. The current public interest test takes into account national security, diversity of media ownership, and the stability of the financial sector. There has been a proposal to expand the public interest test to also take into account effects on sustainability, jobs and the broader health of the economy.

motions.m: That governments should enter into offtake agreements with emerging manufacturers of green energy technology.
motions.i: An offtake agreement is an agreement to purchase a vendor's goods before they actually produce them.

motions.m: That renters of public housing should be allowed to purchase that housing at a discounted rate.
That, in cities facing housing crises, we would remove all heritage protections in building codes.
motions.i: Heritage protections are regulations that preserve buildings of historical, cultural or architectural significance. These regulations prevent demolition and require any renovation to be done in particular ways that keep a protected building's special qualities intact.

motions.m: That we oppose the attempts of civil organisations in South Korea to inform North Korean citizens about their regime's wrongdoings (e.g. disseminating leaflets and ad balloons, smuggling USBs, transmitting cross-border radio waves).
That we oppose Ukraine expanding military operations into Sudan and Syria.
motions.i: Ukraine has been engaging in a series of military operations in Sudan and Syria, sending their special forces units to engage Russian military and mercenary forces in both countries.

motions.m: That the EU should give all legislative power to a single body.
motions.i: Legislative power in the EU is divided between the European Commission, the European Parliament and the European Council of Ministers. The Commission may propose bills, while both the Parliament and the Council of Ministers must pass them for them to become law.

motions.m: That we should break up the three big chocolate traders.
motions.i: The largest three chocolate traders are Barry Callebaut, Cargill, and Olam International. They control a majority of the cocoa market, and are vertically integrated (i.e. they are involved in the sourcing, processing and distribution of cocoa products).

motions.m: That regulators should be granted divestiture powers.
motions.i: Divestiture powers allow regulators to force companies to sell particular holdings.

motions.m: That we should ban personalised pricing.
motions.i: Personalised pricing refers to firms pricing goods and services differently for each consumer based on the information the firm has about them.

motions.m: That we regret the rise of open-audition programs in the K-Pop industry.
motions.i: For the purposes of the debate, 'open-audition program' refers to TV shows where individuals who aspire to be K-pop idols publicly compete to be selected as the final members of a new idol group (e.g. ILLIT, TWICE, IZ*ONE). The selection criteria mainly depend on viewers' votes.

motions.m: That, in states with a low fertility rate, we prefer policies with a familising effect rather than a de-familising effect.
motions.i: Welfare policies related to childcare have a 'familising effect' when they make it easier for parents to look after their child by decreasing material and logistical constraints on childcare. Examples include tax benefits for families with children, parent allowances and paid parental leave. On the contrary, policies have a 'de-familising effect' when they fully or partially replace the parent's role in childrearing by offering alternative sources of care services. Examples include publicly funded or subsidised daycare facilities and helping families hire a paid caretaker.

motions.m: That we oppose the portrayal of vengeance in media as morally justified (e.g. John Wick, Kill Bill, Taken).
That we regret the cooption of indigenous and minority languages, art and culture for fantasy and sci-fi worldbuilding.
That military leaders should be directly elected by citizens.
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, military leaders would still be subject to the authority of elected civilian leaders.

motions.m: That in the aftermath of the repeal of Section 377A, the LGBTQ+ movement in Singapore should actively prioritise fighting for reforms that impact the Trans community, at the exclusion of fighting for gay marriage.
motions.i: The repeal of Section 377A was a landmark victory for the LGBTQ+ movement in Singapore, marking the culmination of a decade of advocacy. Section 377A had criminalised consensual same-sex relations between men. The success of its repeal, while celebrated, was not a complete triumph - the government also amended the constitution to prevent legal challenges to the definition of marriage as between a man and a woman.

motions.m: That the Philippines should disallow split-ticket voting.
motions.i: Currently, the Philippines allows 'split-ticket' voting where the President and Vice President are elected separately, rather than together (such as in the United States and Indonesia). Since the 1987 Constitution of the Philippines, only two elections have resulted in the President and Vice Presidents being elected from the same political party: in 2004, President Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo with running mate VP Noli De Castro, and in 2022, President Bongbong Marcos with running mate VP Sara Duterte. Traditionally, Vice Presidents in the Philippines are appointed as secretaries of departments (e.g. Department of Social Welfare and Development, Education, and Agriculture) under the executive.

motions.m: That the US should abolish state by state incorporation and have businesses incorporated at the federal level.
motions.i: In the US, corporate law varies substantially from state to state. Corporations can incorporate themselves in whatever state they choose and are then subject to the corporate law of that state. Under a national incorporation system, businesses would be incorporated at the federal level and be subject to one consistent set of federal corporate law.

motions.m: That we oppose risk retention requirements.
motions.i: Risk retention requirements require financial institutions which create and sell securities to investors to retain some portion of the credit risk associated with the securities they sell. Banks will continue to hold a portion of the assets they have sold to investors in order to expose themselves to that credit risk.

motions.m: That China should require shadow banks to obtain formal bank licenses in order to continue operating.
motions.i: Formal Chinese banks, most of which are state owned, operate under heavy regulations. As a result, a 'shadow banking' sector worth approximately US$3 trillion has emerged. 'Shadow banks' include trusts, asset managers and microfinance firms. These shadow banks partake in simple banking activities without formal banking licences (e.g. providing loans distinct from the regulated loans given by formal Chinese banks). For the purposes of this debate, forcing shadow banks to obtain a formal banking licence would require them to obtain the same licence as the formal Chinese banks.

motions.m: That sports clubs should adopt a blanket 'no fault stand-down' policy.
motions.i: Under a 'no fault stand-down' policy, any player charged with a serious criminal offence would be automatically suspended with full pay, pending the trial's outcome. The policy makes no judgement about the player's guilt, but operates as a precautionary blanket policy. It contrasts to a discretionary system, where each time a player is charged with a crime, the club makes a decision on whether to stand them down based on the circumstances.

motions.m: That, as an artist, we would prioritise making our art comprehensible to audiences, at the expense of authentic self-expression.
That artists should always refuse requests to do unpaid work.
That we oppose the establishment of "prohibited art" museums.
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, a "prohibited art" museum is a museum specially curated to display works of art that were denounced or censored by the mainstream for political, social, or religious reasons. One example is the Museum of Prohibited Art that opened in Barcelona in October 2023, which houses exhibits such as "Piss Christ" (a blood-red photograph of the crucifixion immersed in the artist's urine; Andres Serrano), "Silence" (an exhibit of 30 pairs of stiletto heels on the same number of Islamic prayer rugs; Zoulikha Bouabdellah) and "Shark" (a sculpture of a tied up, nearly- naked Saddam Hussein submerged in formaldehyde; David Cerny).

motions.m: That we oppose the use of video refereeing to make on-field decisions.
That, as a reigning F2 champion with no promised F1 seat, we would choose to race in a different series over taking a F1 reserve seat.
motions.i: Formula 2 is a 'spec series' (where drivers race identical cars) and is widely considered a training ground for Formula 1. Under current regulations, the F2 drivers' champion is not permitted to participate in another season of F2. Seats in F1 are increasingly hard to come by, with prior experience and sponsorship backing being a large factor in hiring decisions. Consequently, the three most recent F2 champions had to choose between a reserve seat with an F1 team (a role that entails working in the team's driving simulator to develop the car, occasional test driving, and replacing a driver if they are sick or injured) or racing in a different series.

motions.m: That we believe that the morally righteous choice is for the man to save the baby
motions.i: A man enters a burning building on the verge of collapse. He sees a 25 year old mother and her only child, a newborn baby. Both are strangers to him. He can certainly rescue either the mother or her baby, but not both.

motions.m: That we prefer a standard of strict liability to a standard of negligence in workplace harassment cases
motions.i: In workplace harassment cases, strict liability holds employers accountable for harassment between employees, regardless of what steps were taken to prevent or address the harassment, while negligence requires proving the employer failed to take reasonable steps.

motions.m: That we prefer a Tournament of Judges model for appointing judges to higher courts to a system of appointments by executives
motions.i: A "Tournament of Judges" model is where judges compete for promotion to higher courts. This is based on objective metrics such as publication rates, citations by other courts and judges, speed of dispositions, and successful appeal rates.

motions.m: That courts should impose adverse cost orders on parties who refuse reasonable settlement offers and subsequently fail to obtain a better outcome at trial
That India should aggressively pursue the development of SEZs
motions.i: Special Economic Zones (SEZs) are geographically designated areas within a country that operate under reduced economic regulations. These include tax incentives, lower tariff rates and special customs procedures.

motions.m: That we believe artificial intelligence will do more harm than good to the average person
That it is in the interests of the Asian Tigers to aggressively crackdown on unpaid overtime
That it is in the interests of the United States to significantly raise personal income and corporate taxes to reduce the budget deficit, rather than significantly reducing government spending
That Zohran Mamdani's victory in the New York mayoral primary is in the interest of the Democratic Party
That we oppose court-mandated Men's Behaviour Change programs
motions.i: Men's Behaviour Change Programs are government-run initiatives designed to combat gendered violence. Men in these programs meet regularly with a trained facilitator or counsellor in a group setting. The sessions focus on addressing issues such as unresolved childhood trauma, emotional regulation, substance abuse and the impact of past experiences on present behaviour. Men can attend voluntarily, or men convicted of an offence can be compelled to attend by court order.

motions.m: That we oppose bans on sex-selective abortions
That it is in girls' interests to be sent to single-sex schools
That we should incentivise the construction of build-to-rent housing
motions.i: Build-to-Rent (BTR) refers to residential developments that are built primarily to be rented out, rather than sold to individual buyers. Typically, BTR properties are owned and managed by a single institutional landlord, such as a property fund, superannuation fund, or real estate investment trust.

motions.m: That, in developed states, we support governments significantly investing in housing construction under the Singaporean model
motions.i: In Singapore, Housing & Development Board (HDB) units are public housing units built and managed by the government. Under this scheme, residents purchase the lease, not the land. After 99 years, the unit reverts to the state.

motions.m: That, in cities facing housing crises, we would ban individuals from purchasing more than two existing (i.e. not newly built) dwellings
That states which have recently formed through religious secession should establish Religious Councils rather than implementing secularism
motions.i: Religious Councils are councils of religious leaders from the dominant faith in a country which advise governments on whether their laws comply with religious scripture before they are passed. Their advice is non-binding.

motions.m: That we support states employing surrendered militants for counter-insurgency measures
That the Indian feminist movement should actively encourage women to participate in the Agniveer scheme
motions.i: The Agniveer scheme, launched by the Indian government, is a short-term military recruitment program for youth aged 17.5 to 21. Recruits serve in the armed forces for four years, after which 25% are selected on merit for long- term service. The remaining receive a one-time severance package but no pension or permanent benefits.

motions.m: That MERCOSUR countries should allow the free movement of labour amongst member states
motions.i: MERCOSUR is a trade bloc in South America including Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay.

motions.m: That, as a West African nation, we would offer diaspora (including descendants of enslaved persons) citizenship and tax incentives to return to their home nation
That it is in the interest of the al-Sharaa Syrian government to sign the Abraham Accords
That, when a loved one disappears for months without explanation, we would rather believe they chose to leave than believe they were dead
That parents from low socioeconomic backgrounds should teach their children to believe happiness is the only thing in life that really matters
That US universities should comply with the Trump administration
That it is in PGA Tour's interests to make a large investment in virtual golf arenas and include virtual golf tournaments on the PGA Tour
motions.i: PGA Tour is a non-profit organisation that runs 'The PGA Tour', its flagship series of North American Golf Tournaments. It is largely player run, with roughly half of its board being current players, advised by a player-elected Player Advisory Council (PAC). Virtual golf arenas are large indoor entertainment centres. In Phase 1, players tee-off towards a virtual golf simulator, and play through the hole virtually until reaching the green. They then complete the hole on an in-arena putting green.

motions.m: That, as an English Premier League football club outside of the Big Six, we would prioritise signing young talents on long-term deals over buying established stars
That we support referees and refereeing bodies having a bias toward star sports players
That we prefer the New Zealand UE system to a system where entry to university degrees is granted on high school marks and university entrance exam results
motions.i: In New Zealand, University Entrance (UE) is the minimum grade required for admission to university and does not change year on year. UE allows students entry into any university course and there is no limit on the amount of spaces offered. Entrance into specialised courses with limited spaces (e.g. medicine, law, engineering majors) is determined by results in your first year at university (e.g. first-year Health Sciences papers for entry into Medicine). If you do not get into the specialised course you apply for, the first-year papers will be cross-credited to another relevant degree (e.g. Bachelor of Health Sciences).

motions.m: That the United States National Institutes of Health (NIH) should limit the amount of grant money universities can use for overhead expenses (e.g. administration, utilities) to no more than 15% of research expenses (e.g. researcher salaries, equipment, facilities)
motions.i: Universities receiving National Institutes of Health (NIH) research grants typically receive approximately 50% of direct research costs as additional funding to cover overhead expenses such as administration and utilities.

motions.m: That universities should be required to cover 50% of the unpaid student loan debt when a student fails to graduate and subsequently defaults on their loan

That, as drug reform advocates in western liberal democracies, we would prioritise progressive public health narratives (e.g. harm minimisation) over libertarian civil rights narratives (e.g. bodily freedom) when discussing party drugs
motions.i: For the purpose of this debate, party drugs are cocaine, ecstasy (MDMA), ketamine, LSD and GHB.

motions.m: That, in countries where cannabis is being legalised, we would do so using a state monopoly model (i.e. state-run cannabis outlets) rather than a commercial model
That we support involuntary inpatient treatment (i.e. long to medium term stays in psychiatric facilities or rehabilitation centres) for drug addicts
That we support the decline of Traditional News outlets in favour of New Media
motions.r: English as a Foreign Language Finals
motions.i: New Media outlets are characterised by their relatively small size and focus on online distribution (e.g. podcasting, social media commentating and blog- style posting). Examples include Breitbart, Rebel News, Turning Point USA, Vox, Hasan Piker, The Daily Aus, etc. Traditional Media refers to established mastheads, which are generally larger in size and tend to distribute in a variety of mediums (e.g. in print, online and on social media). Examples include The New York Times, The Australian Financial Review, Fox News, Al Jazeera, the BBC, Chosun Ilbo, etc.

motions.m: That we would hold political party leadership accountable for crimes committed by candidates during elections and elected members whilst in office
motions.r: English as a Foreign Language Finals

motions.m: That we prefer a world where people are meaningfully less inclined to backlash against protests which turn violent
motions.r: English as a Foreign Language Finals

motions.m: That we would abolish the Price Limit Rule in China's Stock Exchanges
motions.r: English as a Second Language Finals
motions.i: The Price Limit Rule is a stock market regulation in China that restricts the daily price movement of individual stocks within a fixed percentage range. When a stock reaches the upper limit or lower limit, further trading at a higher or lower price is not allowed for the rest of the trading day.

motions.m: That we support Southeast Asian nations privatising state owned banks
motions.r: English as a Second Language Finals

motions.m: That, in times of recession, we should prohibit publicly traded companies engaged in critical sectors (e.g. health, security, energy) from going private
motions.r: English as a Second Language Finals

motions.m: That we support left-wing parties adopting nationalist environmental policy and rhetoric (e.g. economic protectionism for renewable energy projects, portraying environmental damage as a threat to national safety, portraying the green transition as necessary for energy security and independence)

That it is in the interests of Pacific Island nations threatened by sea level rise to prioritise purchasing land from neighbouring developing countries for the purpose of future relocation, over negotiating pathways to permanent residence in developed countries
That we oppose the reopening of the Panguna mine
motions.i: Bougainville is a semi-autonomous region of Papua New Guinea. The Panguna Copper mine was opened in 1972 by Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL). Local concerns about the mine revived a secessionist movement, leading to the mine's sabotage in 1989 and a civil war that killed 20,000 people. A 2001 peace deal granted autonomy and promised a referendum, which in 2019 saw 98% vote for independence. The mine remains uncontained, with an estimated $80 billion in copper and gold. The Autonomous Bougainville Government now owns 72% of BCL, and is considering reopening the mine as part of its 2027 independence plan.

motions.m: THR the decline of Franchise Loyalty in professional team sports
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, "Franchise Loyalty" refers to players prioritizing to stay in the same team for an extended period of time.

motions.m: THW disallow district level courts from issuing nationwide injunctions in the United States of America
motions.i: In the USA, nationwide injunctions are court orders that bind the federal government, even in its relations with non-parties in a lawsuit, to stop enforcing a law or regulation. It stops the government from acting in a particular way nationwide, regardless of the specific geographic location of the lawsuit.

motions.m: THP the university-governance system over the researcher-governance system within universities
motions.i: There are two systems for corporate sponsorship of research in universities. In a university-governance system, the university manages the commercialization process, with companies providing large-scale grants and partnering directly with the institution, which then respectively distributes funding to researchers. In contrast, in a researcher-governance system, companies fund researchers directly through giving grants to specific research or hiring and compensating faculty while they continue to operate within the university.

motions.m: THW mandate real-term (i.e. adjusted for inflation) funding cuts for tertiary education programmes whose graduates have below-median tax contributions five years after graduation
TH, as parents of an academically gifted child, W choose not to grade-skip their child at any grade level
THW ban the use of classified intelligence as a basis to justify the denial or delay of habeas corpus proceedings
motions.i: Habeas corpus proceedings refers to proceedings where a court examines the legality of someone's detention. Primarily, a court order compels the custodian of a detained person to bring them before the court to determine if the detention is lawful.

motions.m: THO litigation finance in class action lawsuits
motions.i: Litigation finance refers to the practice of outside investors funding legal claims in exchange for a share of the proceeds if the case is successful. These funders cover legal costs such as attorney fees, expert witnesses, discovery, etc. Typical funders include hedge funds, private equity firms, and specialized litigation finance companies (e.g., Burford Capital, Omni Bridgeway).

motions.m: THP a single-league playoff system over the East-West conference model in the NBA
motions.i: In the current NBA format, the NBA is structured into two conferences — the Eastern Conference (e.g. Boston Celtics, Philadelphia 76ers, New York Knicks, etc.) and the Western Conference (e.g. Los Angeles Lakers, Golden State Warriors, San Antonio Spurs, etc.) — with 15 teams in each, primarily organized based on geographic location. In the regular season, teams play 82 games where they face teams from the other conference two times and teams from the same conference three or four times. At the end of the season, teams are seeded based on wins in relation to teams within their respective conference. The top teams then proceed to the play-offs. In the current playoff system, teams are seeded within their respective conferences based on regular season performance with the top 6 in each conference qualifying for the playoffs and the 7th-10th teams in each conference entering the Play-In Tournament where teams compete in a single elimination format to determine the lowest playoff seeds at each side of the bracket. In the current format, teams from the East and West can only face each other in the NBA Finals of the play-offs. In a single-league playoff system, the teams are seeded regardless of conference affiliation. Under this model, teams are seeded 1 through 16 based on the total number of wins (e.g., 1 vs. 16, 2 vs. 15, etc.) with the top 12 teams across the league qualifying for the playoffs, and the 13th-20th teams competing in the Play-In tournament. This format abandons the East West conference structure in the play-offs.

motions.m: THP a world where only one dominant boxing promotion company exists
motions.i: Several boxing associations—such as the WBA, WBC, and WBO—rank fighters and award titles. In boxing, fighters sign with a promoter who helps organize fights for them. Promoters are prohibited from creating their own rankings or titles. The industry includes many promotional companies, such as Golden Boy, Mayweather Promotions, Top Rank, and etc. Organizing fights between fighters usually involves negotiations among different promoters.

motions.m: THBT Pakistan should offer the Baloch insurgents full amnesty in exchange for permanently surrendering arms
motions.i: The Baloch movement is a nationalist movement seeking greater autonomy and independence for Balochistan, mainly in Pakistan. It stems from grievances over political marginalization, military repression, and control of natural resources. It is split between groups like the Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA) and Balochistan Liberation Front (BLF), which use violence to seek independence, and parties like the BNP (Balochistan National Party), which pursue autonomy through elections and legal means. The BLA has historically resorted to destroying infrastructure, targeting security personnel, and sabotaging foreign investments. Most recently, in March 2025, they hijacked a passenger train, killed five officers, and injured many others.

motions.m: THBT Dissanayake's Government should return back land previously acquired by the Sri Lankan Military after the 2009 liberation war back to the native Tamils (Areas within the Jaffna Peninsula, Trincomalee, Sampur, etc.)
motions.i: After the Civil War ended in 2009, the Sri Lankan Military acquired vast swathes of land owned by the native minority Tamil population (around 30,000 acres) in the Northern and Eastern Provinces of Sri Lanka. Anura Dissanayake, the newly elected President of Sri Lanka promised to return back land to the Tamils in his electoral campaign. Military bases like Palaly and Kankesanthurai are located in Jaffna, while Trincomalee and Sampur also host major military installations and are declared high-security zones.

motions.m: THP the sequencing-first model as opposed to the elections-first model of post colonial democratisation in South Asia
motions.i: The sequencing-first model of democratization refers to an approach where liberal-democratic institutions are developed by elites who had significant influence during the colonial era before electoral competition is introduced in nascent political systems. In contrast, the elections-first model prioritizes the rapid introduction of multi-party elections even if the necessary institutions to support fair and effective governance are underdeveloped.

motions.m: THO all scientific research efforts to genetically engineer endangered species
motions.i: Genetic engineering is the process of directly manipulating an organism's genetic material (DNA) to alter its traits. This involves introducing, removing, or modifying specific genes to achieve a desired outcome.

motions.m: TH, as the vegan movement, W reject partnerships with lab-grown meat companies in favor of prioritizing investments in whole-food plant-based systems
motions.i: Lab-grown meat companies refer to firms that produce animal meat by cultivating animal cells in bioreactors. Examples include Upside Foods, Mosa Meat, etc. On the other hand, whole-food plant-based (WFPB) systems focus on natural, minimally processed plant foods like vegetables, legumes, grains, nuts, and seeds.

motions.m: THBT the human obligation to alleviate suffering extends equally to wild animals as it does to pets

THW criminalise all cultural practices that obligate women to renounce their inheritance rights (e.g. giving family wealth to the eldest son in some rich European families, taking land from widows in some African communities, Muslim women accepting less inheritance than men, etc.)
THO the portrayal of abortion as 'without consequence' within feminist spaces
THBT the Japanese feminist movement should support the criminalization of Enjo kōsai
motions.i: Enjo kōsai is a type of transactional relationship where older men give money and/or luxury gifts to attractive young women in exchange for sexual favors in Japan. While Enjo kōsai is not explicitly illegal, Japanese laws prohibit paying a person under 18 for obscene acts and juvenile delinquency.

motions.m: THO the European Union's recent anti-trust crackdown on the US Big Tech Giants
motions.i: In March 2025, the European Union intensified its antitrust actions against major U.S. tech giants (eg. Apple, Meta, etc.) for alleged violations of the Digital Markets Act. The DMA prohibits self-preferencing and requires interoperability. The companies face fines of up to 10% of their global revenue (20% for repeated violations) along with multiple lawsuits.

motions.m: THBT all private spaceflight companies (SpaceX, Blue Origin, etc) should prioritize consolidating existing exploration efforts on celestial bodies that have already been explored (Moon, Mars, etc.), as opposed to focusing on unexplored celestial bodies (moons of Jupiter and Saturn, etc.)
THBT Indonesia should make foreign access to its nickel and rare earth minerals conditional on joint development of green technologies, rather than industrial park based capital-only investments
motions.i: The industrial park model for capital-only investments typically refers to a state-coordinated strategy where infrastructure, land, and basic utilities are concentrated within a designated industrial complex - often developed with government or public financing.

motions.m: THS parents intervening in subtle social conflicts among children in primary school
motions.i: Subtle social conflicts refer to non-physical, emotionally ambiguous interactions that involve exclusion, manipulation, or social imbalance rather than overt aggression. Some examples of such conflict include exclusion-based play, mocking/teasing masked as jokes, ignoring one child inside a group, etc.

motions.m: In multi-cultural societies, THBT first-generation immigrant parents should instill home country values in their children, even if they contradict liberal norms (e.g., not questioning elders authority)
THP a world where time-bound family formations (e.g., 25-year co-parenting contracts, renewable spousal units, etc.) is the norm
THBT it is in the interest of South Africa to rescind the Expropriation Act
motions.i: South Africa's latest Expropriation Bill introduces cases where private land can be expropriated by the government without compensation, aiming to address land reform. In response, President Donald Trump signed an executive order to halt all future aid to South Africa until a complete investigation on the issue is completed.

motions.m: THO the medicalization of gender dysphoria among the queer community
motions.i: Medicalization is the process of defining a phenomenon through medical language in order to categorize it as one that can be treated by medical services (eg. gender affirming services).

motions.m: THBT queer advocacy groups should prioritize empowering individuals to pursue non-state-dependent relationships (e.g. chosen families, live-in relationships, co parenting, etc.) over campaigning for state-recognized unions (e.g. marriage, adoption, etc.)

TH, as the South Korean LGBTQIA+ movement, W significantly prioritize provinces outside of Seoul in their advocacy (ie. organizing larger pride parades in provinces, financially supporting provincial queer organizations, etc.)
THBT Egypt should accept all refugees fleeing from Gaza
motions.i: In early 2025, President Donald Trump proposed a plan to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip to Egypt, threatening it with scaling back financial assistance from the USA if it refuses to accept the refugees. Egypt has remained cautious about accepting, while continuing to provide humanitarian aid.

motions.m: THBT the TPLF should have actively attempted to establish internal power sharing within its members before signing the Pretoria Agreement
motions.i: The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), the main force in Tigray's war against Ethiopia's federal government (2020–2022), signed the Pretoria Agreement in 2022 to end the conflict. At the time, it had a centralized leadership with limited internal power-sharing. After the agreement, it began to fragment with new parties and factions (e.g., Tigray Independence Party, Salsay Weyane Tigray, etc.) emerging in Tigray, some calling for Tigrayan independence and others pushing for different styles of governance.

motions.m: THO the Grab-GoTo Merger.
motions.i: As of March 20, 2025, Grab Holdings Ltd., a leading Southeast Asian ride- hailing and delivery company headquartered in Singapore, is actively pursuing the acquisition of Indonesian tech conglomerate GoTo Group, which is a dominant player in Indonesia's digital economy, providing services such as ride-hailing and e-commerce.

motions.m: THW require the GPIF to significantly increase investments in domestic private equity and venture capital funds, even at the expense of reducing investments in its offshore assets
motions.i: The Government Pension Investment Fund (GPIF) is Japan's primary public pension fund and the largest in the world, managing over ¥220 trillion (approximately USD $1.5 trillion). As of recent years, roughly 50% of GPIF's portfolio is invested in foreign assets—split evenly between foreign equities and foreign bonds. These include major holdings primarily in the U.S. stock indexes (such as the S&P 500) and debt instruments like the U.S. Treasury securities.

motions.m: THS the consolidation of fragmented agricultural land holdings through state-directed agrarian restructuring in the Mekong Delta
motions.i: The Mekong Delta—which produces over half of Vietnam's rice and a significant share of its aquaculture exports—has historically been highly fragmented, with most farms averaging less than 0.5 hectares. A significant proportion of land holdings are held by smallholder households from ethnic minority communities, such as the Khmer and Hoa people, who occupy land without proper documentation and rely on informal recognition by local authorities or customary claims. Typically, these households cultivate various crops (e.g., rice, corn, tapioca, etc.) on their land. The region is experiencing rapid climate change and natural calamities. Recently, the Vietnamese government started pursuing state-directed agrarian restructuring, promoting the consolidation of small, scattered plots into larger farms through state-led land auctions and planned rural relocation programs.

motions.m: THP a world where the two-kingdoms doctrine was the dominant Christian worldview
motions.i: The traditional ontological belief in Christianity is predicated by a hierarchical theory of structure, where authority flowed from the top down, and laws—both moral and civil—were seen as expressions of this divinely ordained structure, with the church playing a central role in interpreting God's will and enforcing moral order. In contrast, the Two-Kingdoms doctrine holds that there is a separation between the divine and the earthly. In this view, all people and institutions in the earthly realm are equal in nature and authority. Law in the earthly kingdom is judged by human reason and justice rather than divine hierarchy, and no person or institution mediates another's relationship with God.

motions.m: THP a world where all major religions did not obligate their followers to go on religious pilgrimages
motions.i: For the purpose of this debate, pilgrimages are physical journeys to sacred religious sites. Examples include the Hajj to Mecca in Islam, the Camino de Santiago in Christianity, and the Kumbh Mela in Hinduism.

motions.m: TH, as a sectarian minority leader in an authoritarian Islamic state, would actively encourage their followers to conceal their religious identity even when not under duress
motions.i: Sectarian minorities are groups that differ from the dominant sect of a religion in beliefs, practices, or identity. These typically include religious communities such as Shia Muslims in Sunni-majority countries, or other smaller Islamic sects (e.g., Ismailis, Ahmadis, and etc.).

motions.m: In ethnically-diverse states that have recently decolonised, THBT national militaries should dismantle residual colonial military structures that prioritise selective ethnic recruitment
motions.i: Selective ethnic recruitment refers to colonial-era policies in which certain ethnic groups were deliberately favored for military service based on stereotypical assumptions about their inherent bravery or martial prowess, while other groups were excluded. Popular examples include the British prioritising the Sikhs and the Gurkhas while deprioritising the Bengalis in India, Northerners (e.g., Hausa-Fulani) being prioritised heavily over southern groups in Nigeria, etc.

motions.m: TH, as a recently installed dictatorship, W not employ the divide-and-rule coup proofing strategy after overthrowing the previous government
motions.i: Dictatorships often employ the divide-and-rule strategy by fragmenting the military's power to ensure that no single military group has the power to overthrow them. Some popular examples include Saddam Hussein creating the Republican Guard and the Special Republican Guard separate from the main military in Iraq, Muammar Gaddafi weakening the regular army and creating loyalist militias in Libya, etc.

motions.m: In states facing armed insurgencies, THS the deployment of Private Military and Security Contractors (eg. The Wagner Group, Blackwater, G4S, etc.) at all critical resource extraction sites in exchange for joint control
TH Predicts that Abrahamic religions will be more decentralised over the next 10 years
TH, as a leader of a small gated religious community, would allow closed-community reality TV to cover their community
motions.i: Closed-community reality TV shows follow outsiders or insiders entering closed religious communities (e.g., the Amish community) to show how strict beliefs, rules, and lifestyles shape everyday life.

motions.m: THBT it is in the interest of Christian churches to prioritise the relational approach over the proclamation approach in evangelism
motions.i: Evangelism is the process through which churches recruits followers. Churches typically adopt one of two approaches when first approaching potential members. The Relational Approach seeks to attract and retain followers primarily through emphasizing the friendships, community, and social support that religious participation can provide. The Proclamation Approach seeks to attract and retain followers primarily through establishing affinity between the individual and the divine. Common ways include proclamation of the gospel (e.g., sharing that Jesus atoned for humanity's sins through His sacrifice), Catechism, etc.

motions.m: THBT the popularization of women-made literature (e.g., Heated Rivalry, Fifty Shades of Grey) centered around sexually explicit material has done more harm than good to women
For weaker states in asymmetric warfare, TH prefers a strategy of prioritising economic resilience over bolstering military strength
THBT the feminist movement should strongly emphasise the "manadult" framework as a standard for male partners
motions.i: A "manadult" refers to a male partner who actively practices anti-patriarchal maturity. The 'manadult' framework focuses on 5 charateristics: independent deconstruction of patriarchy rather than relying on women to educate him, seeking and accepting feminist feedback, consuming women's media, proactively taking on care labour (e.g., household chores, emotional support), and emotionally diverse friendships.

motions.m: THBT the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) should lower the threshold for mandatory delisting
motions.i: In China, publicly traded companies may face mandatory delisting for reasons including major financial fraud, sustained losses, insolvency, or low trading volume. Firms identified to have serious financial or compliance risks are often flagged as risky for several years before possible delisting. China's largest equity market historically delisted less than 0.3% of its companies per year. For the purposes of this debate, "lowering the threshold for mandatory delisting" means expanding the circumstances under which companies are required to leave public exchanges and allowing regulators to delist companies more readily.

motions.m: THBT the EU should significantly relax labor regulations (e.g., minimum wage, overtime regulations, severance pay, employee councils' veto power over firing decisions)
THO Project Stargate
motions.i: Project Stargate is an American artificial intelligence infrastructure joint venture created by OpenAI, SoftBank, Oracle, and investment firm MGX, with technology partners including Microsoft, Nvidia, and Arm, announced at the White House on January 21, 2025, by President Trump. The venture plans to invest up to US$500 billion in AI data centers and related infrastructure in the United States by 2029, beginning with an initial US$100 billion phase.

motions.m: THR the rise of celebrity politicians (e.g. Imran Khan, Shakib Al Hasan, Thelapathy Vijay) in South Asia.
THP a world where key political and social activists (e.g: Malala) are viewed as ordinary rather than exceptional
TH, as the anti-AI movement, would prioritize existential risks over near-term harms in its messaging

THBT social movements should engage in restructuring (e.g. limiting expected workloads, rotating leadership and responsibilities, reducing meeting intensity or frequency) in response to activist burnout
THBT college accreditation should focus on output (e.g., learning assessment, graduation rate, earnings) rather than input (e.g., curriculum structure, faculty qualification, governance)
motions.i: Accreditation is the review system that determines whether colleges and programs meet acceptable standards. Receiving accredited status is usually necessary for access to government funding or student loans.

motions.m: TH prefers the grade-specialization model over the cohort-continuity model in secondary education
motions.i: In the grade-specialization model, teachers repeatedly teach the same grade level each year (e.g., always teaching 10th grade students), but teach different cohorts over time. In the cohort-continuity model, teachers move up through school with the same group of students over multiple years (e.g., teaching the same class from 10th to 12th grade).

motions.m: THBT China should adopt a fully standardized national Gaokao system
motions.i: The Gaokao, China's national university entrance examination, is currently administered with substantial regional variation. Students generally take the examination in their home province, and universities allocate admission quotas for each different region. Different regions differ in subjects, difficulty, scoring systems, and examination formats. Students are primarily evaluated relative to others within their own provincial system.

motions.m: THR the use of WLFI as the WLF's primary form of collateral
motions.i: World Liberty Financial (WLF) is a decentralized finance, or DeFi, project that provides crypto-based financial services such as lending, borrowing, and stablecoin-related products. WLFI is WLF's own governance token, which is tied to participation in the ecosystem and allows holders to vote on certain decisions within the platform. At the same time, WLFI is also a financial asset that has increasingly been used as collateral in WLF-linked lending, borrowing, and liquidity arrangements within its DeFi ecosystem.

motions.m: THO the tokenization of financial markets
motions.i: The tokenization of financial markets refers to the use of digital tokens—recorded on blockchain networks—to represent ownership of financial assets such as equity, bonds, or real estate. Financial institutions such as JPMorgan Chase have developed token-based payment and settlement systems.

motions.m: THBT cryptocurrency markets should be regulated under a commodities-market model rather than an investor-protection model
motions.i: U.S. crypto regulation is currently split between the Securities Exchange Commission (SEC) and Commodities Future Trading Commission (CFTC), with many digital assets classified case by case through enforcement, guidance, and litigation. A commodities-market model, enforced by the CFTC, focuses on exchange oversight, custody, market integrity, and anti-manipulation rules (e.g. in gold or oil markets). On the other hand, an investor-protection model, enforced by the SEC, focuses on issuer disclosures, registration, and safeguards for buyers investing in projects that promise returns (e.g. in stock markets).

motions.m: In multi-ethnic post-conflict states, TH prefers the implementation of a Presidential form of government over a Parliamentary form of government
motions.i: In a Presidential system, the President is directly elected by the people, and acts as both Head of State and Head of Government. The executive branch is completely separate from the legislature. In a Parliamentary system, the Prime Minister, who is the Head of Government, is not directly elected by the public but is the leader of the majority party or coalition in Parliament.

motions.m: THBT Singapore should adopt the Kishore approach to foreign policy as opposed to the Bilahari approach
motions.i: Kishore Mahbubani and Bilahari Kausikan are two prominent Singaporean foreign policy thinkers. Kishore argues small states must always be conscious of their limitations, exercising caution and restraint in public positions so as not to unnecessarily antagonise great powers. Bilahari advocates for small states to stand firm on sovereignity and international law, fiercely safeguarding national interests regardless of how great powers may feel.

motions.m: THBT it is in ASEAN's interest to recognize the elections in Myanmar
TH Regrets Cambodia's invoking of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) compulsory conciliation mechanism
motions.i: The Cambodia–Thailand maritime dispute centres on an Overlapping Claims Area (OCA) in the Gulf of Thailand. In 2001, the two countries signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU-44) to provide a framework for resolving the boundary and jointly developing the energy resources. However, in May 2026, Thailand's government unilaterally terminated MOU-44. On June 2, 2026, Prime Minister Hun Manet formally notified both Thailand and the UN Secretary-General that Cambodia was invoking compulsory conciliation under UNCLOS Annex V, Section 2. This mechanism creates a conciliation commission to hear both sides, examine the facts and legal arguments, and issue recommendations.

motions.m: THP a world where Sulu joined the BARMM
motions.i: The Bangsomoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) is a special autonomous region in the southern Philippines created to give Muslim-majority areas stronger self-government. Sulu is an island province with a Muslim- majority population. In the 2019 plebiscite on the law creating BARMM, 54% of Sulu's population voted against the law. In 2024, the Supreme Court ruled that Sulu's "No" vote had to be respected, so the province was removed from BARMM and placed under Region IX (Zamboanga Peninsula), with a dominantly Christian-majority population. Zamboanga Peninsula is one of the nearest regular administrative regions to Sulu, with Zamboanga City serving as a major transport, trade, port, and service hub for the Sulu Archipelago.

motions.m: TH Predicts that the global population living in poverty* will increase, rather than decrease, in the next 10 years *living on less than USD4.20 per day adjusted for inflation and purchasing power
THR the US Shale Boom
motions.i: The US shale boom refers to the massive surge in oil and natural gas production in the United States, driven by advances in horizontal drilling and hydraulic fracturing since 2010.

motions.m: TH Predicts that international relations will become more liberalist in the next 25 years
motions.i: Liberalism in international relations (IR) is a theoretical perspective emphasizing that states can achieve cooperation, mutual benefits, and peace through international institutions, and economic interdependence.

motions.m: TH, as a young adult, would prefer to build our life with a partner who works in the same industry rather than someone with a significantly different career
In Asian countries with low birthrates, THS government-run dating services (e.g., FirstDate in Singapore)
THS the trend of roster dating
motions.i: Roster dating is the modern practice of casually dating or talking to multiple people simultaneously to keep options open, rather than focusing on one person.

motions.m: THW abolish mega auctions in the IPL
motions.i: The Indian Premier League (IPL) is a professional men's cricket league. In this league, teams acquire players mainly through an annual auction. In an annual auction, players are listed with a base price, and teams bid for them, with the highest bidder securing the player's services. Franchises must operate within a salary cap and squad size limits. A Mega Auction is a special form of the annual auction held every 3-4 years. It serves as a complete squad reset where teams can retain only a small core of 4-6 players. In contrast, the regular annual auction does not restrict teams from retaining as many players.

motions.m: TH, as a national team coach, would prioritize intrinsic over extrinsic motivation
motions.i: Coaching models prioritizing extrinsic motivation are models which reward or punish athletes based on their performance. Coaching models which prioritize intrinsic motivation are models which focus on increasing inherent satisfaction when engaging in the sport, e.g. enjoying the sport, serving the team.

motions.m: TH, as a rising player in esports, would prefer to be signed as a support main over a duelist main
motions.i: In hero-shooter games like Valorant and Marvel Rivals, team composition depends on a mixture of roles and characters. A support main usually helps the team through utility like flashes, recon, smokes, healing, site control, or defensive setups rather than being the primary player expected to kill. A Duelist, by contrast, is an aggressive role built to take first contact, enter contested areas, create space, and win fights. Duelists are expected to seek early engagements and get high-value kills, which requires quick reflexes and reaction times.

motions.m: THBT Bangladesh should grant more autonomy to its local governments
THS Pakistan's aggressive security measures against cross-border militancy from Afghanistan
THO the rise of minmaxxing
motions.i: Minmaxxing means optimizing something by maximizing its strengths and minimizing or ignoring its weaknesses. It's often used to describe approaches to life, work, fitness, dating, or productivity.

motions.m: THR the stigmatization against being chronically online
THBT the BJP's win is in interest of the citizens of West Bengal
motions.i: For the past 15 years, West Bengal had been ruled by a populist leader named Mamata Bannerjee from the regional party TMC. Recently, she lost the State elections in a landslide to the BJP.

motions.m: THR the prevalence of 'kiasuism' in Southeast Asia
motions.i: Kiasuism is a cultural phenomenon derived from the Hokkien word "kiasu" (驚輸), which means "afraid to lose." It describes a pervasive social mindset characterised by an intense fear of missing out, losing, or being worse off than others.

motions.m: THBT it is in the interest of Japan to further invest in arms manufacturing and sales to Southeast Asia
THBT Operation Epic Fury has done more harm than good to the Trump Administration
TH prefers the Loans Programs Office Model to the Tax Credit Model for Clean Energy Financing
motions.i: The government finances clean energy in two main ways. The Loan Programs Office (LPO) directly lends government money to clean energy projects. The tax credit model, most prominently used in the IRA, instead reduces the tax liability of companies that deploy qualifying clean energy assets like solar farms or electric vehicles.

motions.m: THS the ASEAN Power Grid (APG)
motions.i: The ASEAN Power Grid (APG) is a regional initiative aimed at interconnecting the national power systems of Southeast Asian countries.

motions.m: In times of energy crisis, THS subsidizing energy consumption rather than reducing energy demand (e.g., extend holidays, enforce fuel rationing)
THBT the feminist movement should advocate for the feminization strategy over the neutralization strategy
motions.i: In many languages, masculine forms are often used when gender is not specified. Two main approaches aim to make language more inclusive. Feminisation makes women more visible by naming women and men equally. For example, instead of saying "the applicant… he," one might say "the applicant… he or she." Other examples include using split forms such as "Vegetarier/innen" in German to encompass both "Vegetarier" (the male form) and "Vegetarierinnen" (the female form). Neutralisation reduces the focus on gender by using gender-neutral words and pronouns. For example, Swedish uses "hen" as a neutral alternative to "hon" and "han," while Norwegian has moved away from some feminine suffixes.

motions.m: THR the establishment of regional blocs in Africa e.g. COMESA, EAC, SADC, ECOWAS
THR the dominance of the narrative that love is only legitimate if it is long-term.
THBT the rise of public intellectuals has done more harm than good.
motions.i: The term 'public intellectual' describes the intellectual participating in the public affairs discourse of society, in addition to an academic career.

motions.m: THP a cashless world
THS the decision by the Government of Kenya to lift the ban on GMOs.
motions.i: In October 2022, the Government of Kenya revoked a long-standing ban on the importation of Genetically Modified Crops (GMOs).

motions.m: THP afterlife A over Afterlife B
motions.i: In afterlife A, after a life well lived, the 'ethereal soul' is separated from the 'physical body' and joins an eternal, blissful community of souls and 'God' (no physical body). In afterlife B, after a life well lived, the 'ethereal soul' is separated from the 'physical body' and leads a next life, in a new body, in a new world with rewards from the previous life carried into the new world. After the life in the new world ends, the 'ethereal soul' leaves the physical body and leads into another subsequent world under the same conditions. In this version of the afterlife, the soul is on an endless journey of moving from one world to another carrying accumulations of moral rewards from previous lives into every new life. In both instances, people have the same knowledge about the afterlife as in the status quo.

motions.m: THP a world with the BCS
motions.i: A Behaviour Credit System (BCS) is a worldwide integrated technology that allow persons to vote on the social standing of anyone living in that country. The BCS can be accessed through any technological device whereby upon interacting with you, people can rate you on a numerical scale on their device based on their personal and discretionary feelings about you (not on legal grounds). In this system, businesses independently set minimum positive point and maximum negative point limits as requirements before they render services to you and by law, your positive or negative points tally has a bearing on the extent of punishment you get when you are tried for a crime.

motions.m: Without foresight, THW rather be a late bloomer than a child prodigy
THBT globalization has failed
THS the creation and use of municipal bonds within developing countries in Africa.
motions.i: Municipal bonds (or "munis" for short) are debt securities issued by districts, cities, counties and other governmental entities to fund day-to-day obligations and to finance capital projects.

motions.m: THW establish universal jurisdiction for biodiversity.
THS the continued existence of consumer company monopolies in Africa.
THBT that the AU should recognise the statehood of Somaliland.
motions.i: Somaliland is an autonomous region in northern Somalia, which broke away and declared independence from Somalia in 1991. No foreign power recognises Somaliland's sovereignty.

motions.m: THS the gig economy in developing Africa countries
motions.i: "The gig economy" is a term used to describe a labor market where freelance or short-term contracts are more common than permanent positions. Independent contractors are those who work in the gig economy. They perform services for another party in accordance with the conditions mentioned in a verbal or written agreement. In contrast to an employee, an independent contractor works for an employer on an as-needed basis rather than on a regular basis. Since they typically receive freelance compensation, independent contractors are considered "self employed."

motions.m: THBT the criteria for determining the G.O.A.T in team sports, such as football and basketball, should prioritise individual achievements and impact over team success and championships.
THR the glamorisation of being a savage in pop culture
THW Hold accountable all existing African kingdoms that took part in the Trans-Atlantic slave trade
THR eco-terrorism by citizens in the Niger Delta
motions.i: Eco-terrorism refers to the use or threat of violence against property or people, committed in order to forward environmental causes or for environmental-political reasons.

motions.m: THW use Social Impact Bonds for charitable programmes as opposed to direct government funding
motions.i: A "Social Impact Bond" is a funding model where governments arrange for private investors to fund a service provider to solve a specified social problem within a pre-alloted time frame determined by the government. The government only repays the investors if the project is 'successful', as set out in the original contract. If successful, the government repays the investors' upfront investment plus an additional premium.

motions.m: THS The Rise of Product Placement in the Production of Movies
motions.i: Product placement has long been a feature of Hollywood. Seeking a boost in brand recognition and association with cool characters, alcohol and car companies, especially, have for decades paid or engaged in a kind of quid pro quo to get their products into films. The first documented example was in 1896, when the Lumière brothers, often credited as the earliest filmmakers, agreed to feature soap in their film "Washing Day in Switzerland." But the rise of streaming has led to an explosion in product placement. Product placement is now a $23 billion industry, up by an estimated 14 percent since 2020.

motions.m: THW ban the use of gene editing technology for the creation of genetically enhanced children.
THR the "Kill Them With Kindness" narrative.
motions.i: "Kill them with kindness" is an idiom used by Shakespeare that means to overwhelm someone with over-indulgence. It can mean that in itself but it can also imply that someone can be harmed, inconvenienced, or bothered by being treated with excessive favour or kindness.

motions.m: THBT Economically unstable countries should leverage foreign insurrections that boost it's economy e.g Turkey leveraging power over the Gulf Nations via it's ties to the Muslim Brotherhood, the US leveraging power over the middle east via its ocuupation of Afghanistan or the Taliban leveraging Uighurs in China for legitimacy.
As the Pevensie siblings, THW never leave Narnia.
motions.i: During World War II, the Pevensie children, Peter, Susan, Edmund and Lucy, are evacuated from a London to live in a stranger's country house. While playing hide and side, they discover a wardrobe and enters the fantasy world of Narnia. After defeating the White Witch and saving Narnia, the siblings are crowned Kings and Queens of Narnia. Fifteen years later, the Pevensies, now young adults, and discover the wardrobe they entered Narnia from. Presume for the purposes of this debate that the Pevensies are aware that, should they depart Narnia, they may never return, and will return to their residence in their former child forms (but with memories intact) seconds after they left.

motions.m: Champagne socialism refers to a form of socialism where a group of rich / upper class individuals advocate for a fair society where everyone has equal rights and the rich people help the poor.
motions.i: THW promote champagne socialism in Africa.

motions.m: This House as Ethan would continue the romantic relationship with Mia.
motions.i: Ethan is deeply in love with Mia, and their connection feels genuine and profound. They have shared countless moments, dreams, and experiences together, building a relationship based on trust and affection. However, Ethan's world is shaken when he discovers that Mia is not a naturally born person, but a genetically engineered clone. Despite Mia's assurances that her love for him is real and that she has developed her own identity, Ethan must grapple with whether the bond they've formed is strong enough to overcome the fact that she was created, not born, and whether it changes the authenticity of their love

motions.m: This House supports the formation of an African Super League (ASL)
motions.i: The African Super League (ASL)would be a proposed elite football competition featuring top clubs from across the continent. It aims to bring together the best teams in Africa to compete in a league. Teams participating in the ASL would not play in their local country leagues.

motions.m: TH as a tech university graduate would rather primarily focus their efforts on becoming an entrepreneur rather than going for a corporate job
This House Regrets IMF structural adjustment programs in African nations.
motions.i: Structural Adjustment Policies (SAPs) are economic reforms implemented by countries as a condition for receiving financial assistance from international institutions like the IMF and World Bank. These policies typically include reducing government spending, privatizing state-owned enterprises, liberalizing trade, devaluing currency, and encouraging foreign investment.

motions.m: This House Believes that secession movements should be allowed to vote for independence without central government approval
This House Would prioritize smaller local, unconditional aid over larger foreign, conditional aid for NGOs in developing countries
motions.i: A small NGO in a developing country has two funding options: a large foreign donor offering substantial financial support with strict conditions, or a local foundation providing smaller, unrestricted aid. The foreign aid comes with the promise of scaling operations but requires the NGO to implement programs that may not fully align with the local community's cultural needs, as the donor has set specific goals and oversight protocols. While the funding would allow the NGO to reach a broader audience, it would compromise its ability to independently design programs suited to the local context. On the other hand, the local aid comes with no conditions, giving the NGO complete freedom to tailor its programs to the community's needs. However, the local funding is limited, which means the NGO can't expand its reach as significantly.

motions.m: THBT African billionaires have a moral obligation to invest a significant portion of their wealth to philanthropic efforts.
This House Believes that Youth Movements in Kenya should remain grassroots movements without leaders rather than evolving into political movements with leaders.
motions.i: In Kenya, youth protests have been a significant part of the political landscape in 2024, especially in the context of economic challenges, rising taxes, and the demand for systemic reforms. Youth across the country have mobilized to demand change on issues such as unemployment, education, and economic empowerment

motions.m: THW actively prioritize Universal Basic Income (UBI) over Universal Basic Services.
motions.i: Universal Basic Services (UBS) is a system that guarantees free or affordable access to essential public services, such as healthcare, education, housing, and transportation, aiming to meet basic human needs and promote social equity whereas a Universal Basic Income (UBI) is an unconditional cash payment given monthly to all citizens or residents, regardless of their employment status, to ensure a basic standard of living and reduce poverty. For the purposes of this debate, the amount given to a citizen monthly under a UBI program is proportional to the amount the government would have spent to subsidise the citizen across the various UBS programs.

motions.m: Assuming Feasibility, This House Believes that Artificial Intelligence (AI) should be allowed to make life-and-death decisions in healthcare, including triage and end-of-life care, rather than leaving such decisions to human doctors and families.

This House opposes the practice of companies patenting or trademarking cultural elements or phrases that do not originate from their country of domicile eg Disney's Trademarking of "Hakuna Matata", The Patent of Basmati Rice by a Western Company or The Trademark of Mexican Tequila by Non-Mexican Companies
This House opposes Sahel states forming The Alliance of Sahel States
motions.i: The Sahel is a region spanning Africa, including countries such as Mali, Niger, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mauritania. This year, Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger had exited ECOWOS to form the Alliance of Sahel States.

motions.m: THR the decline of Ubuntu in African Communities.
motions.i: Ubuntu is a Nguni Bantu term from Southern Africa, meaning "humanity" or "I am because we are." It emphasizes the interconnectedness of people and the importance of community, compassion, and mutual support

motions.m: THR the societal expectation to "leave a legacy."
THP a World In Which the African Nations Championship (CHAN) was replaced with a quota system for domestic players at the Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON)
motions.i: CHAN is the biennial African Nations Championship, a football tournament organized by the Confederation of African Football (CAF) exclusively for players who play in their national domestic leagues.

motions.m: THR the concept of "South African Exceptionalism"
⁠THO the rise of retail/crypto investing in Africa
motions.i: Retail/Crypto investors are non-professional individual investors who buy and sell financial assets (e.g stocks, bonds, commodities,cryptocurrency etc.).
