In Africa, applications like Valr, Luno, Binance and Chaka have sparked significant interest in retail trading while several major African stock exchanges like the Egyptian Exchange (EGX) have released mobile apps in 2025.

motions.m: THBT African states should form a collective bargaining cartel for critical minerals (e.g., cobalt, lithium).
THBT the LGBTQ movement should adopt a gender-abolitionist approach as opposed to a gender-pluralist one.
motions.i: A gender abolitionist approach is one that seeks to eliminate the social, legal, and cultural significance of gender. A gender pluralist approach seeks to recognize and protect a multitude of gender identities

motions.m: THR the dominance of movies based on already existing Intellectual property (IP) in contemporary cinema
THS Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords on the condition of a clear commitment towards a two-state solution from Israel.
motions.i: The Abraham Accords are a series of diplomatic agreements brokered by the USA between Israel and several Arab nations, including the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Morocco, and Sudan. The accords establish diplomatic relations and promote collaboration across multiple sectors such as trade, education, and technology.

motions.m: THBT fiscal stimulus is more effective than monetary stimulus in a recession.
motions.i: Fiscal stimulus is when a Government boosts demand by increasing spending or cutting taxes. Monetary stimulus is when the Central bank boosts demand by lowering interest rates or expanding the money supply.

motions.m: TH as an agnostic person would adopt Pascal's wager
motions.i: Pascal's Wager argues that when we cannot be certain about God's existence, belief is the safer choice. Believing in the Christian God as the one and true only God and being right after living a life of adopting Christian values and practices may lead to eternal salvation (an infinite reward), while not believing and being wrong while pursuing other 'worldly' values and practices may lead to eternal damnation (an infinite loss).

motions.m: THBT research organisations in the Global South should prioritise South-to-South collaborations instead of partnering with institutions in the Global North
THBT African states should withdraw recognition of the ICJ's compulsory jurisdiction in response to its failure to uphold justice in Sudan.
motions.i: On March 6, 2025, Sudan filed an application to initiate a case at the International Court of Justice (ICJ) against the United Arab Emirates (UAE) for violations of the Genocide Convention. It alleges that the UAE, "through its direction and provision of extensive financial, political and military support for the rebel RSF (Rapid Support Forces) militia," is complicit in genocide. In May 2025, the International Court of Justice (ICJ) said that it "manifestly lacked" the authority to continue the proceedings and threw out the case because the UAE, upon ratification of the Convention, made a reservation to Article 9, the compromissory clause that grants the Court jurisdiction over disputes related to the Convention.

motions.m: TH, as an average 20-year-old African person, would enter the Waka-Trix.
motions.i: The Waka-Trix (Wakanda Matrix) is a fully immersive digital world presenting a plausible alternate Africa and global system in which colonialism and its direct consequences never occurred. This world is still subject to natural, political, and social imperfection. Upon entry to the Waka-Trix, your physical body dies, and all contact with the real world ceases. You enter the Waka-Trix as a newborn baby, with no memories or continued consciousness from the real world.

motions.m: ECCQ las megaciudades latinoamericanas (Buenos Aires, Rio de Janeiro, etc.) deberían reestructurarse hacia el 'urbanismo de los 15 minutos'.
motions.i: El **'urbanismo de los 15 minutos'** es una estrategia de planeación territorial que busca que las ciudadanas puedan acceder a servicios en un radio aproximado de 15 minutos caminando o en bicicleta desde su casa. Se busca organizar la ciudad en pequeños núcleos, y generar instalaciones en los ámbitos de vivienda, trabajo, comercio, sanidad, educación y entretenimiento. Los ejemplos más prominentes de este modelo son los planes de "Paris en Commun", el "Great City Plan" de Shanghai y el "Plan Melbourne". A efectos de este debate, una megaciudad es aquella con un mínimo de 10 millones de habitantes y una alta densidad demográfica.

motions.m: EC investigaría desde el enfoque de las causas en lugar de las consecuencias.
motions.i: Eres una investigadora de ciencias sociales experimentadas. Estás comprometida con los derechos sociales y has realizado múltiples investigaciones de campo. Para efectos de este debate tienes que escoger entre dos enfoques metodológicos para tu investigación sobre el auge de movimientos de ultraderecha. El **enfoque de causas** implica habilitar espacios de diálogo con comunidades de derechas y las personas que las conforman. Así, tu proyecto de investigación podría incluir entrevistas y reuniones cercanas con personas conservadoras para comprender su visión desde la empatía. El **enfoque de las consecuencias** implica habilitar espacios de diálogo con las comunidades afectadas por estos movimientos. Así, tu proyecto de investigación podría incluir entrevistas grupales con personas migrantes para comprender sus vivencias.

motions.m: EC dividiría Penguin Random House en sellos editoriales.
motions.i: Penguin Random House (RRH) es la editorial más grande del mundo, con presencia en más de 20 países, publicaciones en más de 30 idiomas y un posicionamiento significativo en el mercado global del libro impreso y digital. En el ámbito hispanohablante, posee los derechos de 42 sellos editoriales, entre los que destacan Alfaguara, Debolsillo, Debate y Taurus. En EEUU sus títulos ocuparon el 36.7% de los puestos en la lista de bestsellers de tapa dura en 2023 y en 2024 alcanzó una cuota de mercado del 25%.

motions.m: EC prefiere un mundo donde la concepción dominante sobre el derecho sea la principialista sobre la contextual.
motions.i: Para fines de este debate existen 2 concepciones sobre el derecho. La **concepción principialista** entiende el derecho como un conjunto de normas que son válidas porque respetan directrices éticas universales como la igualdad o la dignidad. Donde esta concepción, se asume que las juezas interpretan los textos legales de una forma lógica, sin dejarse guiar por su opinión personal. Por ejemplo, se entiende que la eutanasia es un derecho porque coincide con el principio de libertad personal y vida digna. La **concepción contextual** entiende el derecho como el conjunto de decisiones judiciales que son válidas por la única razón de que han sido adoptadas por una jueza. Desde esta concepción se asume que las juezas deciden sus fallos guiadas por sus valores e ideologías. Por ejemplo, se entiende que la eutanasia es un derecho, en algunos Estados, porque juezas liberales tomaron esa decisión.

motions.m: EC implementaría la pedagogía post radical en el sistema educativo tradicional.
motions.i: La **Pedagogía Post Radical** es una propuesta crítica basada en la resistencia activa a las prioridades normativas del sistema educativo tradicional. Algunas de las propuestas por las que aboga son: \- Que las educadoras usen prácticas artísticas para enseñar todas las asignaturas (p.e escrita creativa para enseñar biología, obras de teatro para enseñar historia, o dibujo artístico para enseñar matemáticas). \- Que el diseño de planes educativos despriorice resultados de aprendizaje basado en respuestas cerradas y estandarizadas en favor de resultados de aprendizajes experienciales y críticos. \- Que las instituciones de educación primaria y secundaria ganen independencia y competencias para adaptar el curriculum a su contexto, más alla de lo estipulado a nivel estatal.

motions.m: EC se opone a la Línea de Crédito Flexible del FMI
motions.i: La Línea de Crédito Flexible (LCF) es un instrumento creado por el Fondo Monetario Internacional (FMI) para proveer financiamiento con condicionamientos menos rígidos a países con indicadores económicos estables e institucionales fuertes. Esta línea tiene dos características básicas que la diferencian de otras facilidades del FMI: 1\. Su propósito es atender las afectaciones graves causadas por factores excepcionales. 2\. Es un instrumento no condicional, donde se puede acceder a los fondos sin compromisos posteriores de política económica.

motions.m: ECCQ los partidos políticos de izquierdas deberían priorizar las políticas universalistas por sobre las progresivas.
motions.i: Las políticas universalistas son políticas públicas orientadas a que toda la población pueda beneficiarse por igual, independientemente de su perfil socioeconómico. Algunos ejemplos son la reducción de la jornada laboral o la inclusión de la terapia psicológica en la sanidad pública universal. Las políticas progresivas son políticas públicas dirigidas específicamente a los sectores de menos ingresos o con mayor nivel de vulnerabilidad. Entre ellas se encuentran las subvenciones para personas en riesgo de exclusión social y la construcción de viviendas protegidas para familias de bajos ingresos.

motions.m: ECCQ República Dominicana debería acelerar sus políticas de transición energética.
EC prefiere la ética zoroastrista en lugar de la ética taoísta
motions.i: La ética zoroastrista de la salvación se centra en el cuidado hacia otras personas. Para ello, invita a realizar realizar acciones desinteresadas que prioricen el bienestar de las demás. La ética taoísta de la liberación se centra en el autocuidado del cuerpo y la mente. Para ello, invita a alejarse del sufrimiento propio mediante la disciplina con una misma.

motions.m: EC, siendo el movimiento LGBTIAQ+ en los países del norte global, priorizaría un activismo basado en la "denuncia como resistencia" por encima del "placer como resistencia".
motions.i: La denuncia como resistencia es una estrategia política que se centra en visibilizar la discriminación e injusticias que viven las personas LGBTIAQ+, exigiendo una transformación social desde la indignación, el coraje y la memoria de sufrimiento colectivo. Algunos ejemplos son marchas con consignas como "Nos están matando" o documentales como "Paris Is Burning", que denuncian la transfobia sistémica en los medios y la sociedad. El placer como resistencia centra la estrategia política en valorar el goce y el bienestar de las personas LGBTIAQ+ reivindicando que vivir con plenitud es un acto profundamente revolucionario. En lugar de poner el foco en las injusticias, lucha por imaginar futuros desde la alegría, el amor y el cuidado mutuo como motores de cambio. Algunos ejemplos son fiestas queer con espacios para la exploración sexual segura o series como "Heartstopper", que muestran relaciones queer positivas y felices.

motions.m: EC prefiere que la crítica artística se rija predominantemente por el CAMP en lugar del AURA
motions.i: La crítica artística es aquella que describe, analiza e interpreta diversas manifestaciones artísticas desde esferas académicas y periodísticas. Incluye, entre otras: reseñas, ensayos, perfiles de artistas y tesis. Para fines de este debate, existen dos formas de plantear la crítica artística contemporánea: * La sensibilidad CAMP valora todas las expresiones artísticas por igual. Para lograrlo, utiliza la ironía y señala la ridiculez del arte, mientras que reivindica formas de arte masivo y estéticas populares. A través de esta lente, una crítica CAMP equipararía 'Hamlet' con 'Ru Paul's Drag Race', o telenovelas con películas de culto; valorando los personajes, actuaciones extravagantes y el dramatismo de las tramas de ambas. * La sensibilidad AURA valora ciertas expresiones artísticas como más importantes. Para lograrlo, utiliza el análisis formal para buscar un subtexto profundo en el arte, a la vez que reivindica formas de arte únicas y que presentan una sólida base conceptual. A través de esta lente, una crítica AURA defendería el valor diferencial de una pintura original de Van Gogh en comparación de una impresión postal de la misma, enfatizando el esfuerzo introspectivo específico del proceso de la artista.

motions.m: ECCQ el Gobierno Federal de Etiopía debería delegar la gestión de los conflictos con milicias étnicas a las policías regionales
motions.i: Etiopía es un Estado Federal compuesto por 9 Estados regionales, cada uno con una etnia mayoritaria diferente. En octubre de 2022, el Gobierno Federal Etíope y el Frente Popular de Liberación de Tigray firmaron el Acuerdo de Paz de Pretoria, poniendo fin a casi dos años de conflicto. Sin embargo, la violencia se ha mantenido entre el Gobierno Federal y milicias étnicas como Fano y el Ejército de Liberación Oromo (OLA), que luchan para que sus Estados regionales tengan mayor autonomía respecto al Estado Federal. Hasta ahora, el Gobierno Federal ha combatido a las milicias de manera centralizada a través del Ejército Federal, que es un cuerpo de defensa multiétnico. Además, los 9 Estados cuentan con policías regionales propias formadas por la etnia mayoritaria de cada Estado.

motions.m: EC, siendo un unicornio tecnológico en expansión, priorizaría financiarse con prestamistas directos en lugar de empresas de capital de inversión
motions.i: Un unicornio tecnológico es una startup privada de base tecnológica cuya valoración actual es de al menos 1.000 millones de dólares. Algunos ejemplos de unicornios tecnológicos son SpaceX, Canva, ByteDnace y OpenAI. Un prestamista directo (direct lender) es una entidad financiera no bancaria que proporciona financiamiento a empresas de manera directa, sin intermediar con bancos comerciales. Suele tener procesos ágiles y condiciones personalizadas, a cambio de un mayor costo de financiación. Algunos ejemplos de prestamistas directos son Ares Management, Blackstone Credit y Oaktree Capital. Una empresa de capital de inversión gestiona fondos privados e invierte capital propio (equity) en otras empresas para hacerlas crecer. Suelen tomar parte del control de las empresas en que invierten adquiriendo participaciones accionariales. Algunos ejemplos de empresas de capital de inversión son KKR, The Carlyle Group y Advent International.

motions.m: EC, como una persona Otaku (オタク), reorientaría su vida hacia el concepto de Ikigai (生き甲斐)
motions.i: El concepto japonés Ikigai (生き甲斐) nos invita a encontrar propósito en la intersección donde coincide lo que sabemos hacer bien, lo que el mundo necesita y lo que puede darnos una recompensa monetaria. El concepto japonés Otaku (オタク) nos invita a encontrar propósito en una actividad muy específica por la que sentimos una pasión profunda que no podemos evitar.

motions.m: THBT the progressive movement should integrate post-work ideology into its messaging.
motions.i: The post-work movement believes that the ideal society is one that has obsoleted the need to work (e.g. a world in which work were mostly automated or everyone were provided a Universal Basic Income)

motions.m: THBT the continued adoption of the guerrilla policy style is not in the long term interests of the CCP
motions.i: China's ""guerrilla policy style"" is characterized by a decentralized approach to policy making, in which policy initiatives are often developed and implemented at the local level by officials who have a large degree of freedom from central authorities in undertaking policy projects. These policies, if successful, are then scaled up to a national level. Under China's guerrilla policy style, policymakers are often willing to accept some degree of disorder, experimentation, and even failure in the pursuit of policy goals.

motions.m: THP a world in which "Ask Culture" is the norm to one in which "Guess Culture" is the norm.
motions.i: ""Ask Culture"" is characterized by the expectation that individuals should feel encouraged to ask for anything when they want it. ""Guess Culture"" is characterized by the expectation that individuals should only make requests of one another in instances where they can infer or assume the answer will be yes.

motions.m: THP a world with the dominant belief that people can transition to different religious identities throughout their lifetime
THR the rise of student political wings of major political parties in developing countries
THB the US and Canada should ban Mandatory Binding Arbitration.
motions.i: Mandatory Binding Arbitration is a contractual provision in which both parties to a contract waive their right to sue one another in court for damages related to a perceived breach of contract. Instead, in the instance of a perceived contractual violation, members must undergo arbitration, an alternative form of dispute resolution in which a mutually agreed upon private arbiter determines both the legitimacy of the alleged violation as well as the potential punishment. MBA clauses can also often include other stipulations such as waiving one's right to appeal the decision or to join class action lawsuits.

motions.m: THP a world where art is submitted to and displayed in museums and galleries anonymously forever
THS a government madated halt of giant AI experiments
motions.i: Giant AI experiments include but are not limited to language learning models Like GPT-4. Other AI labs are currently also developing much more ambitious projects that are much larger than that of OpenAI (the creators of Dall E 2 and ChatGPT)

motions.m: THP a world with community based, democratically run social media instead of company run social media
motions.i: A community based, democratically run social media is one where users join specific social media platforms based on community or interest and members of a community determine their own policies and design in terms of algorithms, moderation, funding, and general functioning of the site for that platform.

motions.m: THBT the IMF should lend significant amounts of money to countries heavily indebted to China with the requirement that these countries default on all Chinese loans.
THO New Optimism
motions.i: New Optimism - the narrative that human history is a story of constant improvement, and that we should be optimistic about the future of the human condition. Supporters of new optimism point to evidence of progress in areas such as poverty reduction, technological advancement, and human rights as examples of constant improvement. Some suggest that this progress is due to increased globalization, free markets, scientific advancement, and collaboration among nations and individuals.

motions.m: THS Ukraine's assassination program
motions.i: Ukraine's assassination program refers to Ukraine's increasing use of assassination as a foreign policy tool after Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Examples of assassinations include Yevhen Yunakov, a former mayor of the Kharkiv region who had been identified as a collaborator with the Russians, and Mikhail Filiponenko, a Russian-backed politician in the Luhansk region. Dozens of people have been theorized to have been targeted by Ukraine's assassination program.

motions.m: THR the Left's tactic of strong anti-police rhetoric
THO the Purdue Pharma settlement agreement
motions.i: In 2023, Purdue Pharma reached a settlement agreement with the victims of the opioid epidemic who sued them in class actions. The class actions bundled together thousands of lawsuits from state and local governments, hospitals and individuals. However, a key part of this, as for any settlement agreement, is that it shields the Sackler family from further civil lawsuits.

motions.m: THR the norm of association between sex and romantic love
THW ban cryptocurrency
THO the use of Pink Glasses technology
motions.i: "Pink Glasses" are glasses that block any object or image that triggers unpleasant emotions to the person wearing them. For example, if a person finds sexualized advertisements, certain art or even homeless people unpleasant, they will not see them when wearing the glasses. The technology is affordable and widely accessible.

motions.m: THO the narrative that human progress is inevitable

THBT China should pursue aggressive economic stimulus policies (e.g. injecting central bank funds directly into the economy at large scale, providing stimulus checks to ordinary citizens and businesses, buying housing to push up prices, etc.)
THBT a second Donald Trump presidency is in the interests of the people of the United States of America
This house prefers a world without personal automobiles (e.g., cars, motorcycles).
This house regrets the win of Zohran Mamdani in the New York mayoral election.
This house supports Bulgaria nationalizing the Lukoil Neftochim refinery.
motions.i: Lukoil is a Russian energy company that owns the Lukoil Neftochim oil refinery in Bulgaria. In October 2025, the US and UK sanctioned major Russian energy firms, including Lukoil. Lukoil Neftochim is one of the largest oil refineries in the Balkans. Bulgaria currently has a sanctions waiver that allows the Lukoil Neftochim refinery to continue operations.

motions.m: This house, as the Chinese Communist Party, would allow farmers in rural areas to freely sell their homes and land.
motions.i: In rural areas in China, land is owned by the "collective" (the village). Rural households have a right to use a piece of land to build their homes, but may not sell their property to people elsewhere.

motions.m: This house regrets the global dominance of American popular culture.
This house believes that the doing/allowing distinction does not exist.
motions.i: The "doing/allowing distinction" is a moral principle according to which actively causing harm is morally worse than allowing the same harm to occur, even if the outcomes are equally bad (for example, killing someone vs. letting someone die). In practice, it implies that it is wrong to harm one person even to prevent a somewhat greater harm to another.

motions.m: This house regrets the CBC's 2025 decision to allow the Taiwanese dollar to appreciate.
motions.i: Taiwan runs a large trade surplus, about $15 billion overall and $70 billion with the United States. Its central bank, the CBC, has typically curbed appreciation of the Taiwanese dollar by accumulating foreign reserves, but amid the U.S. dollar's depreciation in early 2025, it allowed the Taiwanese dollar to rise instead of intervening.

motions.m: This house supports a widespread belief in the banality of evil.
motions.i: The "banality of evil" is the concept, coined by Hannah Arendt, that great evil is often committed by ordinary, thoughtless individuals who fail to reflect on the moral consequences of their actions, and not just by people motivated by deep hatred or ideology.

motions.m: This house hopes for the creation of human-level digital minds in this century.
motions.i: A digital mind is a theoretical technology consisting of a computer-simulated brain that responds to information in the same way as an ordinary brain and possesses a sentient, conscious mind. Human-level digital minds are digital persons: computer-simulated people who are fully conscious and react to virtual events (such as virtual hunger or virtual weather) in the same way that people react to real events.

motions.m: This house, as Mikasa, prefers a world where she never met Eren.
motions.i: Mikasa and Eren were childhood friends who fell deeply in love when they were eleven. Their relationship defined much of Mikasa's life and sense of purpose. When they were nineteen, Eren died tragically.

motions.m: This House Would ban faith-based schools in Western liberal democracies
motions.i: A faith-based school is an educational institution that is affiliated with a particular religious tradition or denomination. These schools often prioritise the integration of religious teachings and values into their educational programs, and may have strong ties to religious communities whilst also teaching standard academic subjects in line with the national curricula. Most of these schools receive some public funding. For the avoidance of doubt, for the purposes of this debate, "faith-based schools" do NOT include extra- curricular religious classes (e.g. Sunday School).

motions.m: TH, as Putin, regrets Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022
This House Prefers a world with the mass replacement of human police officers with Robocops to a world with no Robocops.
motions.i: A EU-backed agency has developed and programmed Robocop. Robocop is a fully autonomous robot police officer that is an advanced artificial intelligence- driven humanoid machine equipped with a range of sensors, cameras, and communication devices. It is capable of independently patrolling designated areas, detecting criminal activities or safety hazards, enforcing laws, issuing warnings or citations, and making arrests when necessary, all without direct human intervention. It can analyse data in real-time, communicate with human operators or other law enforcement units, and adapt its responses based on the situation, all while adhering to programmed ethical and legal guidelines. Where replacing armed officers, Robocops can be equipped with non-lethal or lethal weapons systems, designed to be deployed in accordance with programmed rules of engagement.

motions.m: This House Believes that modern rap and hip-hop has done more harm than good for women
THS the current economic decline of China
motions.i: China is facing economic issues including: deflation, a foreign investment deficit, high youth unemployment, a declining real estate sector, and a reduction in domestic consumption

motions.m: THBT European football leagues should require teams to include a minimum of five domestic players in their starting line-up
motions.i: HA! AS IF YOU THOUGHT ID CA THE LSE OPEN AND NOT SET A DEBATE ABOUT SPORT. SHAME ON YOU. UP THE CHELS!

motions.m: THW allow parents to be prosecuted for the crimes of their child
THBT Narendra Modi's tenure as Prime Minister has done more harm than good for India
THP a world where the natural ageing process is replaced with the Shani Ageing Process
motions.i: \- Under the Shani Ageing Process, the rate at which adults age is directly tied to the difference between their Carbon Footprint and the Carbon Footprint Benchmark. \- The Carbon Footprint Benchmark is the level of Carbon Footprint on a per capita basis that would maintain the current status of the climate. \- The Carbon Footprint Benchmark changes over time to account for the state of the climate. o For example, in 2024 the Carbon Footprint Benchmark may be X units; however, if by 2034, the health of the climate has improved, the Carbon Footprint Benchmark would increase, e.g. to X+2 units – i.e. individuals would be able to generate a higher Carbon Footprint before they reached the Carbon Footprint Benchmark. \- An individual's Carbon Footprint is the total amount of greenhouse gases, primarily carbon dioxide, emitted directly or indirectly by an individual; factors such as an individual's energy usage, travel habits, dietary choices, and consumption patterns are taken into account. \- Individuals with Carbon Footprints above the Carbon Footprint Benchmark age faster than natural, while those with Carbon Footprints lower than the Carbon Footprint Benchmark age slower than natural. \- The rate at which you age is linear not exponential (i.e. every Carbon Footprint unit above or below the Carbon Footprint Benchmark has the same impact on ageing as the previous unit). \- For example, consider two individuals, Abhi and Charlie, who are the same age but have different Carbon Footprints. o Abhi, who lives a sustainable lifestyle with minimal carbon emissions, may age at a rate equivalent to one year for every 18 months that pass. o In contrast, Charlie, whose lifestyle is characterised by high carbon emissions from excessive consumption and travel, may age at a rate of one year for every nine months that pass. \- An individual's Carbon Footprint is discounted by the degree of choice they had over the relevant act. o For example, consider two other individuals, Bob and Anna, if Bob invites Anna for lunch and makes steak, and they both eat the same amount of it, Bob's Carbon Footprint would be higher than Anna if he had decided, without Anna's input, to serve steak rather than a more environmentally friendly option. \- The Shani Ageing Process does not affect one's ability to die for causes not related to ageing. \- In a world with the Shani Ageing Process, people's understanding of it would mirror their understanding of the natural ageing process in the current world.

motions.m: EC se opone a la narrativa de que el actualismo es moralmente superior al relativismo
motions.i: A efectos de este debate, en relación con el consumo cultural, existen dos grandes posturas: 1) Relativismo: Hay que tener en cuenta el contexto de creación de la obra artística, relativizando así cuestiones como la falta de diversidad racial o de género y manteniéndolas en el canon y en el consumo masivo. 2) Actualismo: Las obras artísticas deben juzgarse con criterios contemporáneos, y obras que no respetan valores actualmente valorados como, por ejemplo, de diversidad, deben ser desplazadas del canon o no consumidas

motions.m: EC eliminaría el principio de no afectación en las Administraciones Públicas.
motions.i: El principio de no afectación de ingresos es aquel que establece que todos los ingresos públicos deben destinarse de manera indistinta y global a la cobertura de los gastos públicos, impidiendo vincular un determinado gasto a una determinado ingreso (por ejemplo, lo recaudado por el Impuesto de Bienes Inmuebles (IBI) o el Impuesto sobre la Renta de las Personas Físicas (IRPF) se puede emplear indistintamente para cualquier gasto).

motions.m: EC considera que los gobiernos, en busca de combatir la desigualdad de género, debería luchar por la regularización de los trabajos sexuales.
EC apoya la idea del centro de gravedad permanente.
motions.i: Para efectos de este debate, la idea del "centro de gravedad permanente" consistirá en la actitud de buscar, en aquellos casos donde haya posiciones discrepantes igualmente legítimas, un equilibrio entre dos cosas o el mantenimiento de una equidistancia entre los conflictos que puedan provocarse (no dar apoyo expreso a nadie o no posicionarse).

motions.m: EC lamenta la glorificación de la Revolución de 1934.
motions.i: En 1934, en medio de un contexto de crisis económica y polarización política, se produjeron levantamientos contra el Gobierno de la II República en varias partes de España, sobre todo en Andalucía (campesinado), Cataluña (Generalitat) y Asturias (mineros) con la excusa de la entrada de la CEDA, coalición derechista y no republicana, en el Gobierno. La Revolución del 34 fue un evento caracterizado por la acción violenta, la unidad de toda la izquierda y su repercusión internacional. Los revolucionarios logran establecer comunidades autogestionadas en varias localidades hasta que tras semanas de lucha y una rendición pactada son brutalmente reprimidos por las tropas del Gobierno republicano al mando del general Franco. Las cárceles se llenaron de presos políticos, entre ellos el gobierno catalán, dando argumentos al Frente Popular para triunfar en las elecciones de febrero de 1936. La Revolución del 34 ha sido exaltada por sus partidarios como el último bastión de resistencia obrera en Occidente y usada por sus detractores como arma arrojadiza para legitimar el golpe de Estado de 1936.

motions.m: EC se opone a la influencia de la parábola de los talentos.
EC, siendo China, condenaría la invasión de Ucrania por parte de Rusia.
EC no contaría a nadie esta situación.
motions.i: Eres una persona de un estrato socioeconómico ligeramente por debajo del promedio. De un día para otro te tocan 25 millones de euros.

motions.m: EC, siendo un Alfa ++, elegiría el exilio en lugar de la oficina de Interventor Mundial.
motions.i: En "Un mundo feliz" la sociedad está dividida en castas desde su nacimiento y las personas son condicionadas para aceptar su rol. Las ciudades en las que viven cuentan con un alto grado de progreso tecnológico y grandes comodidades. Cualquier necesidad personal física o emocional que no solucione el sistema, es saciada con una potente droga, el soma. Prácticamente todo está controlado por el Estado. La investigación que conduzca a mayor avance tecnológico está prohibida para no causar inestabilidad social. A veces, miembros de la casta Alfa++ (la élite) toman conciencia de su individualidad (el soma ya no les hace efecto y perciben que no cumplen con sus deseos invididuales) y les entra un afán de autorrealización. Son considerados herejes. Es el caso de Mustafá Mond, un Alfa++ a quien se le da a elegir dos opciones. La primera, el exilio a una isla con las comodidades de la civilización y con otros seres conscientes de su individualidad, donde podrá investigar, pero alejado del resto de la sociedad. La segunda, un puesto de ayudante de Interventor Mundial, con vistas a ser un gobernante con poder, pero que tendrá que mantener el sistema tal y como está.

motions.m: In post conflict societies, THW actively suppress information and interpretations that portray any past dictator or tyrannical regime in a sympathetic light.
THBT it is in the interest of Saudi Arabia to develop closer ties with China, even if this will substantially weaken ties with the US.
This house would legalize active non-voluntary euthanasia.
motions.i: Active euthanasia is where proactive steps are taken to hasten someone's death with the aim of minimizing suffering. Non-voluntary euthanasia involves patients not competent to consent (e.g., those in a coma, with dementia, etc.), where someone else consents to hastening their death. If a patient has (while competent) officially filed an "advance directive" about hastening their death under their current health circumstances, then this is NOT a case of non-voluntary euthanasia.

motions.m: As a parent, THW raise their child to prefer being single.
This house believes that feminist movements in developing nations should prioritize the strategy of advocating for carceral feminism.
This house opposes the norm of company founders remaining in key C-suite roles in the company after its IPO.
motions.i: "IPO" = Initial Public Offering; (when a company changes from private to public ownership by offering stocks to the public) "C-suite" = a term used to describe high-ranking executive titles in an organization (e.g., chief executive officer, chief operating officer, etc.)

motions.m: THO the demonization of nationalism in progressive circles
motions.i: For this debate, nationalism is defined as a sense of national consciousness, exalting one nation above all others and placing primary emphasis on promotion of its culture and interests as opposed to those of other groups

motions.m: THO the trend towards increasing pathologization of mental health in Western liberal democracies
motions.i: Pathologization is to view or to characterize as medically or psychologically abnormal. In the past two decades, the rates at which mental disorders are being labelled as well as diagnosed have been rapidly increasing. New conditions such as Pre-menstrual Dysphoric Disorder, Hoarding Disorder, and Prolonged Grief Disorder are being added to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, or DSM. The percentage of American children diagnosed with Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD) tripled to 11.4% over the past 10 years, and 50% of adults aged 18-24 report symptoms of anxiety and depression compared to 30% of adults overall.

motions.m: THBT the prevalence of civil litigation in American society has done more harm than good
motions.i: Unlike criminal cases where the state acts as the prosecutor on behalf of society, civil cases involve disputes between individuals, groups of individuals, or organizations (e.g., companies, governmental organizations). When a person or entity 'sues', they file a lawsuit. 'Litigation' is this act of taking legal action. The American civil law system is one of the most expansive in the world. There are 4 lawyers and 24 lawsuits filed for every 1000 people in America, compared to 2 lawyers and 5 lawsuits for every 1000 people in Germany, and 0.3 lawyers and 1 lawsuit for every 1000 people in Japan.

motions.m: THS the increased presence of foreign private equity firms in Asian markets
motions.i: Private equity (PE) firms invest in businesses that are not publicly listed on a stock exchange, acquiring stakes in these companies with the goal of selling them for a profit in the future. In the past few years, Western PE firms have been increasingly active in Asia. In 2022, Blackstone announced plans to allocate more than 50 billion USD to Asian markets over the next decade, KKR increased its total stake in Vietnamese conglomerate Masan Group to 750 million dollars, and the American PE firm Carlyle Group acquired a controlling share worth 1 billion dollars in Rigaku, a Japanese scientific instruments maker.

motions.m: THW significantly re-allocate funding from police systems to community-based intervention programs (e.g. identifying young men susceptible to gun violence and providing them with cognitive-behavioral therapy; hands-off strategies involving initiatives like training 911 call receivers or engaging local community members to intervene in conflicts before they escalate into violence).
THP the narrative that committed long-term romantic relationships should be start ups over the narrative that they should be mergers
motions.i: A narrative that suggests romantic relationships should be "start ups" encourages participants to view a relationship as a joint venture where the goal is building a life together from the ground up. This can include working to make new joint friends (often "couple friends"), choosing hobbies to do together, developing close relationships with each other's friends/families and considering the effect of one's choices on the success of the relationship (e.g., what jobs to take, whether or not to move cities). A narrative that suggests that romantic relationships should be "mergers" encourages participants to view a relationship as a way to create synergies between two already fully formed lives. This can include having separate hobbies, prioritizing one own's friends/ family, and making choices about one's career or city independently. A "merger" relationship encourages both individuals to fully have built up their lives before seeking a committed relationship, whereas a start up relationship does not

motions.m: THP a world in which IMF Special Drawing Rights became the primary world reserve currency rather than the US Dollar in the 1970s
motions.i: Reserve currencies are often used as a transaction medium for international trade, held by governments in a country's reserves, and used as the currency for loans. Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are an international reserve currency created by the IMF. For the purpose of the debate, they are able to be held by private entities and individuals. The make up of the currencies in the basket is updated every 5 years. In 1974, the value of the SDR currency was based on the following basket: USD: United States Dollar (32.6%) DEM: Deutsche Mark (10.2%) GBP: British Pound Sterling (9.7%) FRF: French Franc (7.1%) ITL: Italian Lira (6.6%) JPY: Japanese Yen (6.0%) CAD: Canadian Dollar (5.9%) NLG: Dutch Guilder (4.3%) BEF: Belgian Franc (3.5%) SAR: Saudi Riyal (3.0%) ESP: Spanish Peseta (2.1%) AUD: Australian Dollar (2.1%) SEK: Swedish Krona (2.1%) IRR: Iranian Rial (2.0%) NOK: Norwegian Krone (1.5%) ATS: Austrian Schilling (1.3%)

motions.m: THBT it is in the interest of the EU to aggressively pursue a common foreign policy
In areas characterized by significant inequality, THBT religious leaders should actively preach the doctrine of "honorable poverty" to their followers
motions.i: "Honorable poverty" is the doctrine that excess, waste, and opulence are all sinful, and states that the accumulation of all wealth beyond what is necessary to meet one's basic needs is immoral.

motions.m: In the teaching of history in post-conflict states, THW prioritize the use of stories from communities/ individuals (ex. Diary of Anne Frank, texts from the minority community) over academic textbooks
THS the living having a strong duty to the dead
THW push the button
motions.i: Ambition is the strong desire to achieve something significant and to be better than others. There is a button which, if pushed, would remove ambition from all humans

motions.m: THS the rise of health and wellness influencers on social media.
motions.i: Health and wellness influencers are individuals who use digital platforms (e.g., Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, blogs, etc) to share content related to physical and mental well-being, diet, fitness, beauty, personal growth, and overall lifestyle choices.

motions.m: THO the arrest of Telegram's CEO
motions.i: Telegram is a social media and messaging platform which uses an extremely secure encryption network. Telegram's CEO, Pavel Durov, was arrested in France on November 18. The arrest is part of an ongoing criminal investigation led by French authorities. The inquiry focuses on allegations regarding Telegram's role in facilitating criminal activities through its platform. Critics claim the platform's encryption and limited moderation have been exploited for illegal purposes.

motions.m: THW break up FRELIMO
motions.i: FRELIMO (Front for the Liberation of Mozambique) is the dominant political party in Mozambique. Founded in 1962, FRELIMO began as an anti-colonial movement dedicated to gaining independence from Portuguese rule. Following independence in 1975, FRELIMO established a single-party state, initially adopting a socialist model under the leadership of Samora Machel, Mozambique's first president. Since independence, FRELIMO has been the ruling party, with its members holding the presidency continuously. Over the years, the party has overseen Mozambique's transition to a market-based economy and multiparty democracy. FRELIMO retains significant support, due in part to its historical role in the independence movement and its continued influence in Mozambique's institutions. In recent years, FRELIMO has faced growing competition from other political groups, particularly from the centre-right RENAMO (Mozambican National Resistance), with some opposition parties and civil society organizations alleging electoral manipulation and the undermining of democratic practices in the 2024 elections.

motions.m: From behind the veil of ignorance, THP to be born as a water sign rather than an air sign
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, people who are born as water signs tend to be emotional, intuitive, caring and often put others above themselves. People who are born as air signs tend to be more rational, emotionally detached and tend to prioritize their own needs ahead of others.

motions.m: THBT feminist movements should engage directly in electoral politics (e.g. endorsing and campaigning on behalf of specific political candidates, forming feminist political parties and wings of political parties, donating to political campaigns, etc.)
THP a world with mood meters
motions.i: A mood meter is a number that can be publicly seen above every single person's head, indicating their mental wellbeing on a scale of 1-10. Mood meters can fluctuate over time but cannot be concealed, hidden, or changed consciously.

motions.m: THS the introduction of green anti-trust policies
motions.i: Green anti-trust refers to a set of policies that aim to increase the competitiveness of green companies against their carbon-intensive counterparts by setting harder antitrust requirements on carbon-intensive firms (e.g. harsher market share thresholds or vertical integration limits)

motions.m: TH, as Ukraine, welcomes Donald Trump's presidency
That, as the US, we would ban proprietary military designs or technologies and financially compensate firms to make their research available to competitors.
That, in Western liberal democracies, we should nationalise the funeral industry
That we oppose the open access business model in the publishing of scientific journals
motions.i: Scientific journals operate on two main business models. "Closed access" refers to a model where fees are paid by institutions or individuals to access articles published by the journal. Under a closed access model, no fee is paid by the authors of the article. "Open access" refers to a model where access to the article is freely available and revenue is instead from fees paid by authors of articles. Established and more prestigious scientific journals tend to be closed access, while newer journals tend to be open access.

motions.m: That, where academics voluntarily reveal academic misconduct in their research, we should grant both those academics and their research groups amnesty
That we should allocate 50% of research funding by random lottery to scientific grant proposals which pass minimum requirements
That we prefer a world in which Veritaserum was in the global water supply.
motions.i: Veritaserum is an otherwise-harmless liquid that compels people to tell the truth at all times.

motions.m: That, as the Global South Fellowship of Anglican Churches, would leave the Anglican Communion
motions.i: For Anglican churches to be 'in communion' is for them to recognise each other's sacraments and bishops and view each other as being part of one overall church. The Anglican Communion is a denomination of regional churches who are in communion with the Church of England (CoE). Currently the Anglican Communion is divided over liberalising reforms (e.g. the ordination of females priests and bishops, the acceptance of and performing of same-sex marriages). The major conservative block within the Anglican Communion is the Global South Fellowship of Anglican Churches. These divides have also caused splintering of Anglicanism in the USA, with the conservative Anglican Church in North America (ACNA) splitting from the liberal Episcopal Church. The Global South Fellowship of Anglican Churches recognise the ACNA, but the CoE and other more liberal Anglican churches do not.

motions.m: That, as Marvel, we would bring back Tony Stark/Iron Man from the dead, assuming Robert Downey Jr would agree to return.
That we regret the criminal investigations and indictments of Donald Trump.
That we should require all US presidential primaries to occur on the same day.
motions.i: Traditionally, each US state selects its own day to hold its presidential primary. The usual order for the beginning of the primaries is Iowa 1st, New Hampshire 2nd, Nevada 3rd and South Carolina 4th. Some states joined together to hold their primaries on the same day, the most prominent example being 'Super Tuesday'. In 2024, the Democratic party opted to have South Carolina, rather than Iowa, hold the first primary.

motions.m: That, as the Republican party, we regret blocking the bipartisan border deal.
motions.i: Following Republican outcry that money should not be going to protect Ukraine before protecting the US southern border, a bipartisan group of senators crafted a legislative package which packaged together money for Ukraine and Israel with $20 billion for increased border protection and a number of policy changes which would've made it easier to prevent immigrants entering the country. Following strong opposition from Donald Trump, Senate Republicans rejected the proposal.

motions.m: That successful black filmmakers should opt out of the Academy
motions.i: The Academy is a prestigious institution dedicated to the advancement and recognition of film. It controls which films are nominated for, and awarded Oscars (Academy Awards). Artists become members of the academy by sponsorship from two existing academy members, and this entitles them to vote for which films are nominated for, and win, Oscars. Between 300 and 1000 members are added each year. As of 2022, The Academy was 81% white.

motions.m: That, as the CCP, we would limit the import quota to six foreign films a year
motions.i: China has an import quota capping the number of imported foreign films to 34 a year. Films that are jointly produced with China are not subject to the import quota because China controls at least 50% of the copyright and profits/losses of the film. China is the 2nd largest film market in the world

motions.m: That we support synthetic performers
motions.i: A synthetic performer is an AI generated performer made to look as lifelike as possible, to the point where you cannot tell the difference. However, synthetic performers are NOT based on a specifc real person. This is distinguishable from a digital replica, which is an AI generated performer based on a real person, eg Carrie Fisher in Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker. Synthetic performers are also distinguishable from computer-generated animation

motions.m: That the ACCU Scheme should not give carbon credits to vegetation management projects
motions.i: The Australian Carbon Credit Union Scheme (ACCU) provides carbon credits to companies which can show they have exceeded their targets for emissions reduction. These credits can be sold to other firms or the government for a profit. In 2024, almost half of carbon credits were issued for vegetation managment projects. The majority of vegetation management projects involve agreeing not to allow livestock to graze where they were previously planned to, thus allowing the environment to regenerate.

motions.m: That, as China, we would encourage the consolidation of electric vehicle makers
motions.i: China manufactures more electric vehicles than the rest of the world combined. China hosts over 300 domestic electric vehicle companies as well as Tesla, Volkswagen and other foreign brands who have gigaplants manufacturing EVs in China. Chinese EV companies are steeply cutting car prices to maintain market share. The CCP has historically encouraged market consolidation in other industries by removing merger barriers and using CCP officials to initiate merger negotiations.

motions.m: That, as Australia, we would prioritise energy infrastructure investments for the domestic market over export opportunities (e.g. LNG terminals, undersea power cables)
That we prefer unicameral legislatures to bicameral legislatures
motions.i: A unicameral legislature refers to a legislature with only one house or assembly. Examples include New Zealand and South Korea. A bicameral legislature refers to a legislature with two houses or assemblies. Examples include the United States and Australia.

motions.m: That we should establish a technocratic independent board to decide which taxes to implement, from a target tax rate set by the government
That we support the 'parachuting in' of candidates
motions.i: In Australia, the major party candidates for each electorate are typically chosen by the local branch of the respective major political party. However, the practice of "parachuting in" candidates occurs when the federal party overrides this process, appointing a candidate to run in a specific electorate instead of deferring to the local branch's choice. Notable instances of this include the selections of Kristina Keneally in Fowler and Andrew Charlton in Parramatta.

motions.m: That the UN should militarily liberate West Papua unless Indonesia agrees to a UN overseen independance referendum
motions.i: In 1969, a plebiscite was held to determine whether West Papua would be an independant nation or a province of Indonesia. While the UN was supposed to provide oversight under the New York Agreement to ensure a fair vote, the actual vote featured a little over 1000 Indonesian selected delegates, who voted unanimously to be a province of Indonesia. The result was recognised by the international community and West Papua became a province of Indonesia. Since then, Indonesia has been fighting a low level insurgency in West Papua. On December 1st 2018, the West Papua National Liberation Army killed 19 Indonesian civilians and a soldier in the Nduga massacre prompting resprisals. Since the violence escalated, between 60,000 and 100,000 people have been internally displaced. UN Special rapporteurs have confirmed allegations of extrajudicial killings (including of young children), enforced disappearance, torture, inhumane treatment and the forced displacement of at least 5,000 indigenous Papuans by security forces between April and November 2021.

motions.m: That we support the 1998 waterfront reforms
motions.i: In the 1990's, Australia had inefficient ports as a result of union domination. For instance, the Patricks Company (a dock working company) had an exclusive contract with the Maritime Union of Australia (MUA) that required them to hire union workers. Industrial action was common, prices were high and there were substantial delays. The Australian government and Patricks colluded to union bust the MUA, and began a campaign to reform the waterfront. Patricks secretly trained a non union workforce in Dubai. The Australian government made funds available to Patricks in the event of an industrial dispute, and passed legislation which put further limits on industrial action. In 1998, Patricks locked out its union workforce and replaced them with the non union workforce. Eventually a deal was reached between Patricks and the MUA to reform the way the waterfront operated. These reforms included allowances for the hiring of non union labour on the waterfront, casualisation of the workforce, paycuts, increased working hours and redundancies. This lead to a substantial weakening of the MUA's power. Collectively, this process is referred to as the 'waterfront reforms'

motions.m: That ECOWAS should offer to end sanctions against Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger in exchange for withdrawal of their notices to leave ECOWAS
motions.i: Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger are Sahelian states in Western Africa that experienced military coups in 2021, 2022,and 2023 respectively. Nigeria leads the regional bloc, the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS), which placed sanctions on all three nations with a longstanding policy of sanctioning any ECOWAS states that overthrow democratically elected governments. After the coup in Niger, Mali and Burkina Faso declared they would militarily support Niger if Niger was attacked. In January 2024, all three countries gave notices to leave ECOWAS which take a effect a year after their submission. The Sahelian countries mostly export gold (~$15 bil combined), with some cotton (~$600 mil combined), sesame seeds (~500 mil combined), and Niger exports radioactive ores (~300 mil). All three countries depend on imports for food, weapons, industrial goods, and chemicals.

motions.m: That we prefer a public option healthcare system to a single payer healthcare system
motions.i: A 'public option' healthcare system involves the government directly providing public healthcare for vulnerable members of the population (poor people, disabled people, old people etc) for free. The rest of the population may choose to buy into that public system for a fee. A 'single payer' healthcare system involves the government directly providing public healthcare for the entire population.

motions.m: That we should use directly observed treatment for anti-psychotic medication prescribed for schizophrenia
motions.i: "Directly observed treatment" refers to medications that must be taken while being directly observed by a medical professional. When given a prescription, a patient is required to be observed when they take each dose of that medication, either in person or by video link. It is often used for highly communicable diseases like tuberculosis. Anti-psychotic medication is medication prescribed to manage psychosis (including delusions, hallucinations and paranoia), often for those with schizophrenia.

motions.m: That SSRIs should be available over the counter
motions.i: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs) are a class of antidepressant medications commonly prescribed to treat depression and anxiety disorders (for example, Lexparo and Prozac). They work by increasing the levels of serotonin, a neurotransmitter, in the brain. The majority of individuals do not experience severe side effects, and overdosing is extremely rare. The period in which severe side effects are the most common is when coming on and off SSRI's

motions.m: That Lebanon should conduct a new census
motions.i: Lebanon runs under a confessional political system in which political power is divided between various religious groups. For instance, seats in the parliament are split 50/50 between Christians and Muslims. The president is required to be a Maronite Catholic, the prime minister a Sunni Muslim. This split is based on the 1932 census, which showed a slim majority of Maronite Catholics. More recent surveys have shown the population is closer to one- third Maronite, with Muslims a slim majority. Lebanon has not had a census since 1932.

motions.m: That the Nippon Steel purchase of US Steel is in the interests of the United States
motions.i: Nippon Steel is a Japanese company which is the 4th largest steel producer in the world. In December 2023, they agreed to purchase US Steel, the US's 2nd largest steel company. In the last 15 years, US steel has shrunk in market capitalisation and output, from the 8th largest in the world to the 24th largest in the world. Nippon has promised to uphold all of US Steel's obligations to workers under it's collective bargaining agreements. Despite this, the acquisition is opposed by the United Steelworkers Union, Joe Biden and Donald Trump.

motions.m: That we support governments creating publicly run gig economy platforms
motions.i: A gig economy platform is a platform which connects those looking for short term 'gig' work to those looking to hire people to perform that work. Examples include Uber, Airtasker and Fiver. In 2018, the workforce board of Long Beach, California launched a gig economy platform of its own. The platform covered both public sector gig work (for instance in education and healthcare) and private sector gig work (for instance in childcare and events). Workers are given 'digital badges' reflecting their skills and qualifications to help match them with employers. There are similar platforms planned for Portland, Oakland, Louiseville and Southern Indiana.

motions.m: That the Australian Government should bail out failing residential construction companies
motions.i: Since mid-2021 in Australia, more than 2000 residential construction companies have entered administration. This includes many small building firms, as well as larger firms such as the Porter Davis Homes Group who at the time were responsible for 1700 building projects.

motions.m: That we oppose university rankings (e.g., QS, Times Higher Education)
That we prefer schools to adopt a primarily academic educational philosophy rather than a holistic one.
motions.i: Holistic educational philosophies emphasise making people, rather than teaching people. This emphasises moral instruction (e.g., politeness, de- emphasis on individuality in favour of group harmony) and community participation/service (e.g., cleaning and running events), alongside traditional academics.

motions.m: That, as a professional tennis player, we would choose to be on an inside sports documentary series (e.g., Breakpoint).
That, as the Victorian Liberal Party, assuming we win next year's election, we would meaningfully continue treaty negotiations with the Victorian Aboriginal community.
motions.i: Approximately 65% of Victorians support entering into a treaty with Victoria's Indigenous People. The Victorian government is currently involved in negotiations with the Treaty Authority (a special Statutory Authority) which are expected to result in a treaty within the next 2-3 years.

motions.m: That, as a deeply conservative voter, we regret that Josh Frydenberg lost his seat in the 2022 Federal Election.
motions.i: Josh Frydenberg is a moderate Liberal politician. He supported the Voice to Parliament and belonged to the centre-right faction of the Liberal party.

motions.m: That we prefer the 'Help to Buy' scheme to the 'Super for Housing' scheme.
motions.i: Both major Australian political parties have proposed competing measures to assist first-home buyers. Labor have proposed the 'Help to Buy' scheme where the federal government would contribute up to 40% of the purchase price of a property, reducing the deposit and interest payments, in return for a 40% stake in the property. Homeowners could pay the government back over time or when they sell the property. The Coalition have proposed the 'Super for Housing' scheme where buyers can access up to $50k from their superannuation to contribute to a deposit on a house. Both schemes have eligibility requirements and other conditions.

motions.m: That it is unethical for lawyers to pursue unjust outcomes even when it is in the interests of their clients.
That we would ban private prosecutions.
motions.i: Most criminal prosecutions are brought by the state. A private prosecution is a criminal case which is brought by an individual, a business or an organisation which considers itself to have been the victim of a crime. Private prosecutions have to comply with guidelines, and are still heard before a state appointed judge or jury.

motions.m: That we support Luigi Mangione's assassination of Brian Thompson
That, assuming feasibility, we support a tax on all unsolicited communications.
motions.i: A communication is unsolicited when it was not reasonably sought or expected. Examples include junk mail, spam, public social media posts and comments or unexpected private communications to strangers (email, direct message, letter, etc). This does not include any communications with friends or family, nor face-to-face interactions.

motions.m: That we should implement a global 'use rights' system for fishing.
motions.i: Use rights generally provide an exclusive right to fish a particular place at a particular time, to certain groups of fishers (companies, individuals or communities).

motions.m: That we regret the reversal of Lava Jato.
motions.i: Lava Jato was a 2014 Brazilian anti-corruption probe. While it uncovered widespread misconduct among top government officials and state-owned enterprise executives, the probe was later marred by allegations of political bias. Lava Jato has since been 'reversed'. The task force was disbanded in 2021 before their work had concluded, several fines have been voided, and many of those imprisoned have been released early.

motions.m: That we would allocate LULUs by using the tender process.
motions.i: A Locally Undesirable Land Use (LULU) is a type of land use that creates externality costs on those living in close proximity (bad smells, poor aesthetics, health hazards etc.) Examples of LULUs include prisons, garbage dumps, power plants and homeless shelters. In a tender process, each local community is required to submit a bid for an amount of compensation for which they would accept the LULU near them. The community that submits the lowest bid, is paid their requested compensation and the LULU is placed near them.

motions.m: That we regret the queer movement's emphasis on highly specific rather than broad labels to reflect individuals' sexual and gender identities.
That it is in the interests of the CCP to substantially liberalise its position on Queer rights.
That the feminist movement should adopt the narrative that happiness is hedonic rather than eudaimonic.
motions.i: Hedonic happiness is the belief that happiness comes from pleasure and self- indulgence, following current desires and maximising immediate enjoyment. Eudaimonic happiness is the belief that happiness comes from the pursuit of goals and having a clear purpose. ﻿﻿

motions.m: That technology companies should be required to release any software that achieves a highly dominant market position (e.g., Google Chrome, Photoshop, Zoom, etc) as open source.
motions.i: Open source software is released under a license allowing anybody to freely examine, alter, and distribute the software and its source code.

motions.m: That central banks should raise their inflation target rate.
motions.i: Many countries currently target an inflation rate of 2%.

motions.m: That western countries should refuse to negotiate with the United States to avoid the imposition of tariffs.
That it is in the interests of the Catholic Church to adopt this position as dogma.
motions.i: "This isn't dogma, just my thought: I like to think of hell as empty. I hope it is." - Pope Francis

motions.m: That Kiwistan would be justified in using military force against Ozlandia.
motions.i: Kiwistan and Ozlandia are neighbouring countries. Ozlandia is significantly larger and richer than Kiwistan, and Kiwistan relies assymetrically on trade with Ozlandia. However, Kiwistan has a stronger military than Ozlandia. Amid a diplomatic breakdown, Ozlandia has cut off trade with Kiwistan and imposed harsh sanctions. Attempts at negotiations have failed.

motions.m: That we prefer a world where all humans cease to feel disgust.
That US civil servants should actively work to keep their jobs in the Federal Civil Service
motions.i: Shortly after the commencement of Donald Trump's second term, the Trump Administration began a campaign to significantly reduce the size of the Federal Civil Service. This entailed efforts to fire civil servants, and convince them to resign, such as: offering redundancy payments, changing job requirements, transferring civil servants, and applying political pressure to senior civil servants.

motions.m: That we prefer a world where the Clinton Administration fully implemented the recommendations of the 1995 U.S. Commission on Immigration Reform
motions.i: In 1995, the U.S. Commission on Immigration Reform, chaired by Congresswoman Barbara Jordan, recommended a comprehensive overhaul of immigration policy that included reducing legal immigration from approximately 800,000 to 550,000 annually, eliminating visa preferences for adult children and siblings of U.S. citizens, prioritising skills-based immigration and nuclear family reunification, and strengthening border enforcement and employer sanctions. While the Clinton Administration implemented some of the Commission's enforcement-related recommendations, it declined to adopt proposals to reduce legal immigration levels or restructure family-based immigration categories

motions.m: That we regret the Obama Administration's decision not to criminally prosecute major banks and bankers in the aftermath of the global financial crisis
That states with low fertility rates should implement the Hanson Proposal.
motions.i: The Hanson Proposal would provide parents with a portion of all tax revenue paid by their children. It would also provide them with tax credits each year until their child turns 18. This would be revenue neutral, funded by the abolition of aged pensions.

motions.m: That we support the use of analogue listing rather than individual listing when prohibiting NPS.
motions.i: New psychoactive substances (NPS) are synthetic drugs designed to mimic the effects of illegal substances, with altered chemical structures to evade drug laws. New versions of these drugs are continuously being produced by clandestine drug laboratories and released into the public, predominantly for recreational use. There are two approaches to prohibiting these drugs. Individual listing, which is traditionally used in drug listing, only prohibits drugs explicitly named by legislation. Analogue listing pre-emptively prohibits drugs that are similar to existing illegal substances, either based on chemical composition or effects of consumption.

motions.m: That we should require AI companies to pay licensing fees to the owners of data they use to train their models.
That in post-colonial states we prefer an emphasis on regional and interstate identities (e.g. Pan-Arabism, Pan-Africanism) rather than building national identities.
That, as the United Kingdom, we would not return the Chagos Islands to Mauritius
motions.i: The Chagos Islands are a group of more than 60 islands in the middle of the Indian Ocean about 500 kilometres south of The Maldives. When Britain granted Mauritius independence in 1965, it retained the Chagos Islands for itself as the British Indian Ocean Territory. The native population of the Islands were expelled and an American military base was constructed on the largest island Diego Garcia. Keir Starmer has promised to return the islands to Mauritius.

motions.m: THBT major dating platforms should include a mutual-review and rating system for users.
THR the rise of nonchalance in popular culture.
THW ban fast fashion advertising aimed at minors.
THBT states should legally recognise marriage as a fixed-term contract that must be actively renewed (e.g., every 5–10 years).
TH, as a high ranking member of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps, would defect immediately.
THW wake up another passenger.
motions.i: You have left Earth and are on a space ship en route to a new planet and a new life. You were supposed to remain cryogenically frozen for the entirety of the trip. However, due to some inexplicable technical failure in the cryogenics system, you wake up prematurely; your ETA is 90 years. The ship can grow an unlimited amount of food and it recycles its water. You are the only passenger who is awake.

motions.m: TH Predicts Erika Vance.
TH, as a UWaterloo student, is ambivalent to busting
motions.i: "Cali or Bust" refers to the practice of Waterloo students using all of their coop applications on high paying, high clout positions in California, applying to extremely few safety postings. Waterloo Engineering students must acquire 5 / 6 co-op credits to graduate.

motions.m: THR the villainisation of cheating in relationships.
THS the popularisation of gay media marketed to and read primarily by female audiences (i.e. yaoi).
motions.i: In Japanese Manga (comics), Yaoi refers to a genre of gay fiction that features young and attractive male characters, often in the "bishōnen" (beautiful boy) archetype. Usually the stories are marketed to and read by straight female audiences

motions.m: TH Predicts that the Trump Administration will successfully overthrow Khamenei's regime in Iran
THBT Ethan Hunt should destroy the Sevastopol (and the Entity's source code)
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, the Entity is a near-omnipotent autonomous system capable of accessing, controlling, and weaponising global digital networks, infrastructure, and intelligence assets. It has been successfully able to embed itself into a majority of the world's critical infrastructure (including operational control of Earth's nuclear weapons). Its physical containment—the Sevastopol—is a heavily fortified facility housing the Entity's core systems and the key that grants control over it. Ethan Hunt is a 50 year old agent working for US intelligence, and has been at the job for the last 25 years. He has recently gained control of the Sevastopol.

motions.m: In emerging economies, THP industrial peace over labour autonomy.
motions.i: Industrial Peace refers to stable relations between workers, employers, and the state, marked by low levels of industrial conflict and predictable wage- setting. It is often associated with three-way agreements in which unions are formally incorporated into consultative or decision-making bodies. Labour Autonomy refers to a model in which trade unions operate independently of the state or political actors and retain full discretion to use collective action, such as strikes or work stoppages.

motions.m: THBT the "asian values" discourse has done more harm than good for the promotion and protection of human rights in Southeast Asia.
motions.i: The "Asian Values" discourse gained prominence in the 1990s, led primarily by leaders such as Lee Kuan Yew (Singapore) and Mahathir Mohamad (Malaysia). Proponents argued that Asian societies share a distinct set of values: such as filial piety, social harmony, collective well-being over individualism, and respect for authority, that are fundamentally different from Western liberal ideals.

motions.m: In states where capital punishment is regularly administered, THBT human rights groups should lobby for reforms in the punishment over the outright abolition of capital punishment.
THW allow South Indian states full ownership over coastal and offshore energy resources within their EEZs.
motions.i: Under current Indian law, the central government controls offshore energy resources — including oil and gas blocks, offshore wind projects, etc. States have no direct ownership or taxing rights over these resources, even when the exclusive economic zone (EEZs) is geographically off their own coast.

motions.m: THP a world where all high-stakes life decisions (e.g., career path, choice of spouse, medical treatment) are made by a perfectly objective AI, rather than by individuals.
THBT members of marginalised communities have a moral obligation to consider the social implications of their lifestyle choices.
TH prefers the controlled model to the flexible model
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, there are two models through which teacher training and hiring can be done. Under the controlled model, the government or the central authority sets strict qualifications for entry into the teaching profession, imposes rigorous training programs, and maintains strong oversight over recruitment, certification, and professional development of teachers. In contrast, under the flexible model, entry qualifications and training requirements are generally more relaxed, and local authorities or individual schools have more control over hiring and training of the teachers.

motions.m: THO the ban of Bangladesh Awami League by the interim government
motions.i: The July Revolution of 2024 in Bangladesh was a student-led mass uprising against the authoritarian government of the Bangladesh Awami League (BAL) under Sheikh Hasina, ending its fifteen years in power. Protests arose in response to widespread corruption, the detention and killing of dissidents by security agencies, and the mass shooting of protesters during July 2024. In its aftermath, the student revolutionaries installed an interim government led by Nobel laureate Dr. Muhammad Yunus, under whose leadership the BAL was banned. However, during the interim period, concerns over national security persisted, as lawlessness and mob violence reportedly increased. Today, Bangladesh's political landscape features the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP), which has been in power for two terms; the newly formed National Citizens Party (NCP), created by the July revolutionaries; and Jamaat-e-Islami Bangladesh (JIB), which has in the past aligned with both BAL and BNP. Alongside these parties, various Islamic groups and smaller left-leaning parties remain active

motions.m: THO the rise of Private Equity in residential housing markets
motions.i: After the waves of home foreclosures during the 2008 financial crisis, Private Equity (PE) firms bought distressed homes in bulk and turned them into rentals. Today, there are several PE firms that together own more than 2.2 million units, about 10% of the total housing stock in the U.S. alone. More recently in 2023, China implemented policy measures in a bid to attract PE funding into its housing market.

motions.m: In secular states with religious diversity, THBT states should aggressively support religious reinterpretative groups
motions.i: Religious reinterpretative groups are movements, organizations, or schools of thought within a religion that reinterpret existing religious texts, doctrines, or traditions. Examples include Liberation Theology, Islamic Feminism, Arya Samaj* etc. *Arya Samaj is an Indian Hindu reform movement that rejects hereditary caste and untouchability as later distortions, advocating for a merit-based social order

motions.m: TH, as a green investor, would invest in climate startups in the developing world as opposed to those in the developed world
TH, as a minority writer, would depict their community's struggles through the perspective of the direct victim rather than an observer
THBT it is in the interest of the US to significantly repeal its restrictions on semiconductor chips
motions.i: In 2022, the Biden administration announced sweeping restrictions on semiconductor chips, including export controls, restrictions on hiring, and on research collaboration.

motions.m: In areas with high incarceration, THBT the prisoners' rights movement should actively advocate for prisoners' access to voluntary assisted dying
THR the dissolution of habatsu within the Liberal Democratic Party
motions.r: English as a Foreign Language Semifinals
motions.i: "A habatsu is a formal faction within Japan's Liberal Democratic Party (LDP), acting as a ""party within a party."" They were unique as a highly institutionalized system that controlled LDP politics through patronage, fundraising, and power-brokering. Unlike informal cliques, habatsu had official names, leaders, and offices. Their primary function was to secure ministerial posts and party positions for their members in exchange for loyalty. In late 2023, it was revealed that key factions had systematically failed to report millions of yen from fundraising parties, channeling the money into illegal slush funds. In response to plummeting approval ratings caused by the revealed corruption, the scandal led to the dissolution of most major factions."

motions.m: TH prefers a world where people undervalued their losses as opposed to a world where people overvalued their losses
THR the imposition of the birth control requirement
motions.r: English as a Foreign Language Finals
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, birth control requirement refers to the conditionality attached to developmental aid that has been imposed by numerous international organizations including USAID, the World Bank, UNFPA (United Nations Population Fund), and IPPF (International Planned Parenthood Federation). These agencies required recipient countries to impose national policies on the proliferation of birth control, such as widespread promotion of contraception, sterilization campaigns, or restrictions on family size.

motions.m: In Northeast Asia, TH predicts that the average woman in 2050 will have a happier life than the average woman now
THP a world where individuals' primary collective identity is based on livelihood
motions.i: The Vezo people are a semi-nomadic ethnic group living along the southwestern coast of Madagascar, known for their deep connection to the sea. Their collective identity is primarily shaped by their trades and livelihoods, such as being exceptional swimmers or skilled fishermen. In contrast, in status quo, collective identity is often formed based on a mixture of inherent social traits such as race, gender, or nationality.

motions.m: THP a norm of matrilocal marriages over neolocal marriages
motions.i: Matrilocal marriage refers to a societal system in which a married couple resides with the wife's parents. Neolocal marriage is a type of post-marital residence in which a newly married couple resides separately from both the husband and the wife's natal household.

motions.m: In states at risk of democratic backsliding where anti-democratic politicians have won through legitimate elections, THBT violent resistance to the transfer of power is justified
