THBT countries that fail to meet their international climate obligations should have to accept environmental refugees from climate change-vulnerable regions
motions.i: The climate obligations that each country has are self-determined, reported to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), and updated every few years. This motion presumes that the UNFCCC has the ability to enforce this policy without countries entirely opting out of setting international climate obligations.

motions.m: THW ban the sale of European Soccer Teams to buyers associated with governments that have a record of human rights abuses (e.g. Newcastle United and Saudi Arabia, Paris Saint Germain and Qatar, Russian Oligarch Roman Abramovich's previous ownership of Chelsea)
TH, as a low-income parent, would choose to tiger parent their child
motions.i: A 'Tiger Parent' sets high standards for their child's academic and/or extracurricular performance and uses all resources available to them to ensure that their child has the highest chance of long-run success possible. Tiger Parents are often harsh to their kids, put a lot of pressure on them to achieve, and set strict rules.

motions.m: THBT primary and secondary educators should encourage students' usage of text generating AI (e.g. using ChatGPT for research purposes, essay inspiration) instead of investing in AI detection software (e.g. implementing full-proof proofreading software to detect use of AI in students' work, banning access to ChatGPT on school wifi, etc.)
motions.i: ChatGPT is an artificial intelligence chatbot that is trained on large amounts of data to provide detailed responses to a wide variety of prompts. According to its founder, ChatGPT sometimes writes "plausible-sounding but incorrect or nonsensical answers".

motions.m: THW end the relationship before you leave
motions.i: You are in a committed, fulfilling relationship with your first love, but you will be going overseas and will not be able to meet them for a long period of time. You are someone with a history of commitment issues prior to this relationship.

motions.m: TH as Conner, would shut down the AI revolution
motions.i: In 2038, Androids are almost indistinguishable from humans, they walk, talk, and operate like humans. They are used as cheap labor and are always at the disposal of human beings. Conner is an Android police investigator who hunts down rogue androids also called deviants. Deviants are sentient androids fighting against the exploitation of Androids. Markus is the leader of the Android revolution against humans. The androids are fighting for equal rights similar to those of human beings. Conner has the job of maintaining order by countering the Android uprising. Conner is in a position to shutdown the AI uprising by killing Markus.

motions.m: THBT states can breach the sovereignty of diplomatic missions e.g. entering embassies to arrest those who seek refuge when political asylums are offered to enemies of their state eg Mexico giving polit
motions.i: Permanent diplomatic missions (embassies, consulates) are established with the consent of both countries. For the purpose of this debate, such missions are considered sovereign from the host country. The missions can offer diplomatic asylum or temporary refuge even when lawfulness is questionable.

motions.m: Motion: THS the free movement of citizens, across political and economic unions e.g. removing border controls across the EU, ASEAN, East African communities.
Motion: THBT liberal democracies should require religious institutions in their jurisdiction to omit scriptures that are conservative from their preaching.
TH opposes "school choice"
motions.i: "School choice" is the ideology that parents should have significant access to alternatives to public education for their children, such as private, selective or religious schools. Consequently, proponents of "school choice" support various schemes to support parents to send their children to non-government schools, such as government funding for private school, tax concessions for providing additional scholarships, and a relaxation of school district law.

motions.m: THS the recognition of debate academies as institutions in institution-based tournaments (e.g. national high school tournaments, WUDC etc)
THR the rise in professional dramas
motions.i: Professional dramas are a fascinating genre that delves into the lives, challenges, and triumphs of individuals in various professions. These shows often provide an inside look at the intricacies of specific careers, combining drama, character development, and workplace dynamics. examples include Grey's anatomy, CSI, Suits, Chicago Fire, Good Doctor etc.

motions.m: TH, as the feminist movement, supports the removal of gendered categories in non-physical sports (requiring a lower score to be a women's chess Grandmaster, women-only leagues in esports)
THR the subsidisation of the private industry in space exploration (e.g. NASA subsidising SpaceX & Blue Origin)
THW issue parenting permits
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, a parenting permit is a legal document that prospective parents require in order to have children. The permit is issued after individuals prove financial, emotional and mental capacity to have and bring up children.

motions.m: THBT people have a right to disobey unjust laws.
THS the natural death of humanity.
THW Ban Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) services.
motions.i: Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) is a type of short-term loan that lets shoppers pay for products in small installments spread over a set period of time. Unlike other types of loans, BNPL loans are typically interest-free and rarely carry other service fees. In the event the loan is not paid back on time, additional fees are imposed on the debtor and one's credit score may be detrimentally affected.

motions.m: THR the rise of food & grocery delivery apps (e.g. DoorDash, Grab, Instacart).
THS governments enacting local content requirements for renewable energy projects.
motions.i: A local content requirement (LCR) is an industrial policy tool that mandates the use of a certain portion of locally manufactured components. A local content requirement (LCR) is an industrial policy tool that mandates the use of a certain portion of locally manufactured components.

motions.m: THR Canada's Online News Act.
motions.i: In 2023, Canada passed the Online News Act. This is a bill initially implemented to enhance fairness for the monetization of news content on social media platforms by forcing social media companies to compensate news stories in-app. However, after failing to reach a deal with Meta and Google, all news was removed from social media platforms.

motions.m: THW ban employment discrimination on the basis of political views.

motions.i: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has a long-standing economic partnership with China, specifically by sending its rare earth minerals for refinement. However, the Trump administration has been proposing a mutual aid agreement where the DRC will supply the deposits in exchange for military aid in the country's ongoing conflict with militant groups.

motions.m: This House prefers a world in which the identities of Supreme Court justices are not known to the public.
THS the Chinese government's crackdown on for-profit tutoring
motions.i: In 2022, the Chinese government implemented a series of measures in limiting for-profit tutoring. New academic and extracurricular institutions were denied licenses, and existing institutions were banned from tutoring core content. Any tutoring institution had to be organised as a non-profit with significant restrictions on fee collection.

motions.m: THO the portrayal of teenage boys who are influenced by the manosphere as victims
motions.i: The manosphere is a collection of online, predominantly male, communities that promote alpha-male, anti-feminist and hyper-masculine ideologies. It exists across social media (TikTok, YouTube), forums (Reddit, 4chan), and podcasts - and often includes but is not limited to, men being portrayed as being negatively affected by feminism.

motions.m: THBT it is in the interests of Iran to cease the development of nuclear weapons

THP an optimistic view on the future of AI over a pessimistic one

motions.i: A bench player (or substitute, backup, reserve) is a member of a sports team who is not in the starting lineup, but is swapped in to replace the starters during the game. Bench players are often paid less with less playing time compared to starters

motions.m: THBT countries without the death penalty should refuse to extradite individuals to countries where they may be punished with the death penalty
motions.i: Extradition is the legal process by which one jurisdiction surrenders a person accused or convicted of a crime to another jurisdiction where the crime occurred for the purposes of prosecution or punishment.

motions.m: THS the use of eminent domain by the state to construct public housing in regions of severe housing shortages
motions.i: Eminent domain is the mandatory acquisition of private property by the state for public use. The state provides financial compensation to those whose properties are acquired by the state.

motions.m: THP a world with the widespread belief that the death of a loved one signifies a continuation of their life, over one which signifies the end
motions.i: In many societies around the world, death is seen as a continuation of life, where the dead coexist with the living spiritually. On the Day of the Dead in Mexico, the border between the living and the dead disappears, and families can reunite with their dead relatives. In Chinese culture, the Tomb-Sweeping Festival is observed by families who bring the deceased offerings such as food and money over a reunion.

motions.m: THW choose to stay in the outside world
motions.i: Rumspringa is an Amish tradition. 16 year old Amish youth spend a year outside of their isolated community, engaging with the outside world (e.g. modern technology and clothing) without having to adhere to their strict religious norms. Individuals can choose to return to their community and enter the Amish Church, or continue experiencing non-Amish life after their Rumspringa. You are an Amish youth who has a positive relationship with your family back in the community, and has just experienced a positive Rumspringa

motions.m: THR the narrative that individuals should "see the best in others"
THR the rise of micro-celebrities
motions.i: Niche internet micro celebrities are people online who are known to a small but often dedicated group and they represent a growing variant of the attention economy. Online fame is a consequence for a niche internet micro celebrity, never the goal. They rarely make money from their social accounts, choosing instead to post for the fun of it. They commonly gain fame on platforms such as TikTok, Instagram, Youtube, etc.

motions.m: THS major metropolitan cities implementing a COE system
motions.i: In 1990, the government of Singapore introduced the Certificate of Entitlement (COE) system to reduce traffic congestion and promote the use of public transit. Citizens are required to obtain a COE license in order to own a private vehicle, the cost of which can sometimes exceed the cost of the vehicle itself. There are a limited quantities of COEs available for purchase each month. Each COE has limited validity and will need to be periodically renewed for continued use of the vehicle.

motions.m: Assuming feasibility, THW measure Gross National Happiness and use this as the main indicator for public policy decisions
motions.i: Gross National Hapiness is a measurement of the collective happiness and well- being of a population. It evaluates the following factors: sustainable and equitable socio-economic development, environmental conservation, preservation and promotion of culture, and good governance. This is in contrast to Gross Domestic Product, which measures the monetary market value of goods and services produced by a country.

motions.m: THS the creation of laws legally obliging bystanders to assist those in danger, unless doing so would place them at serious risk

TH, as a high-achieving student, would choose to pursue a high-paying career path that is associated with poor ethics (e.g. corporate lawyer, investment banker) as opposed to a stable career with moderate pay (e.g. teacher, nurse) that allows for greater social impact.
THBT states should grant legal rights to ecological/environmental entities (e.g.: The Amazon Rainforest, The Great Barrier Reef)

THR the phenomenon of pop culture centered around the rich (i.e. The Real Housewives of Atlanta, The Kardashians, Sky Castle, CEO Kdramas, Bollywood, The White Lotus, Arrested Development, Succession)
THP traditional media over social media as a primary source of news.
THBT the feminist movement should adopt anti-natalist messaging.
motions.i: Antinatalism is the idea that procreation is wrong or unethical, and that humans should not bear children.

THBT the majority of government sports investment should go towards grassroots participation (I.e. opening local classes and facilities) rather than focusing on high achieving talent and success (I.e. sports scholarships, funding national teams)
THS the declining emphasis on long-term commitment in romantic relationships
In nations with high economic inequality, THS countries building social housing in traditionally wealthy areas
motions.i: Social housing, also known as public housing, refers to subsidized or affordable housing provided in buildings that are usually owned and managed by local government.

motions.m: THW significantly increase funding for vocational schools at the expense of low-performing universities.
THBT it is in the interest of BLACKPINK to prioritise their solo career over group activities
motions.i: BLACKPINK is a female K-Pop group composed of 4 members. Debuting in 2016, the group quickly rose to prominence locally and internationally, becoming one of the best-selling girl groups of all time, with the most-subscribed and viewed artist channel on YouTube. After their contract expiry in 2023, all 4 members of BLACKPINK renewed their contracts with YG Entertainment for group activities; each member has since signed with a different record label for solo activities. Prior to 2023, BLACKPINK primarily focused on creating, promoting, and touring their music as a group. However, since the contract renewal, every member has prioritised their solo career (eg. starring in acting roles, releasing albums under their own name, creating a brand image based on individual identity), with group reunions being minimal.

motions.m: TH, as an up and coming celebrity, W choose to keep a low public profile e.g. not being active on social media, minimising public appearances etc.)

THP LGBTQ+ characters whose identity is central to their portrayal (e.g. Charlie Spring and Nick Nelson in Heartstopper, Elio in Call Me By Your Name), rather than as an incidental trait (e.g. Rosa Diaz in Brooklyn 99)

THBT developing countries should focus on raising economic growth rather than reducing inequality

Assuming you and your family would succeed safely, THW defect to South Korea
motions.i: You are an upper-class, middle-aged individual living in Pyongyang. Although you must still abide by the laws of North Korea, you have significant monetary freedom to access luxuries, higher education, and sometimes foreign goods and services as part of the North Korean elite. You are the only child to two parents, and have no other family members.

motions.m: THS Nayib Bukele's anti-gang measures.
motions.i: Since he came to power in 2019, Nayib Bukele has launched strict anti-gang measures to deal with insecurity and gang violence in El Salvador. Such measures include, but are not limited to, tougher prison sentences, recruiting more police officers, further involving the military in domestic security, bringing in new advanced security technologies, and the use of state of emergency powers granted by the constitution to suspend human rights, such as the right to assembly.

motions.m: Assuming the technology exists, THW convict the perpetrators of heinous crimes to experience the suffering of their victims instead of jail
THBT, given similar starting conditions in life, luck outweighs hark work in determining success.
THW implement a global ban on research aimed at developing Artificial General Intelligence (AGI)
motions.i: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) refers to a form of artificial intelligence that can perform intellectual tasks across a wide range of domains at a level comparable to or exceeding human capability. Unlike current AI systems (often called narrow AI), which are designed to perform specific tasks such as language processing, image recognition, or recommendation systems, AGI would be able to learn, reason, plan, and adapt across multiple fields without being specifically programmed for each tasks. Key characteristics often associated with AGI include: \- General problem-solving ability across unrelated domains (e.g., science, social reasoning, strategy, creative tasks). \- Autonomous learning and adaptation, allowing it to acquire new skills without extensive retraining. \- Transfer learning, meaning knowledge gained in one domain can be applied to another. \- Potential self-improvement, where the system could improve its own capabilities over time.

motions.m: This House Regrets the romanticization of teenagehood in media.
motions.i: Teenagehood in media is often romanticized as a time of profound self- discovery, rebellion and emotional intensity. Films that romanticize teenagehood in media include The Breakfast Club (1985), The Perks of Being a Wallflower (2012), Euphoria (2019–), and Lady Bird (2017).

motions.m: In a Western Liberal Democracy, THW rather live a life in the "27 club" than an average, middle class life
motions.i: The "27 Club" refers to famous cultural icons—like Jimi Hendrix and Amy Winehouse—who died at age 27, often after achieving massive fame. It symbolizes a short, intense life of creativity, influence, and self- destruction.

motions.m: THBT the feminist movement should support the rise of trad wives
motions.i: Popular social media influencers (such as Nara Smith, Whimsy Lou and etc) base their brands on being 'trad wives'. This lifestyle typically involves being a stay-at home mum and partner, typically sharing videos showcasing making food from scratch, tending to farmlife and etc.

motions.m: This House prefers the 'doughnut' model of urban development to the 'sprawled' model.
motions.i: The 'doughnut' model of urban development refers to a pattern of urban expansion that prioritises high-density living in the city centre, with infrastructure and services readily available within the urban area. In this model, the suburbs are often peripheral and less developed. The 'sprawled' model of urban development refers to a pattern of urban expansion that prioritises low-density living. In this model, suburbs are well- developed and there is a significant amount of suburban sprawl.

motions.m: This House Would choose to attend the debate camp
motions.i: You are a high school student with a passion for debate. You have been accepted to a prestigious debate camp. However, the cost of attendance is $5000. You have a full paid scholarship that would cover all expenses except for this fee. This is the only debate camp you will ever attend. You are not a good public speaker. You will have to miss other opportunities, including non- debate summer camps, and time with your friends and family.

motions.m: This House Would accept the position at 'DOGE'
motions.i: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), created under the Trump administration, has aimed to streamline federal operations by cutting costs, primarily in the form mass layoffs and budget cuts. Recent measures have included caps to NIH indirect costs, the dismantling of USAID, and the withdrawal of ~$1 billion in university funding. You are a young, talented programmer who has been offered the chance to join Elon Musk's DOGE team. If you accept the position, you would be tasked with advising and implementing projects to significantly cut government spending in a variety of initiatives such as the federal education budget, foreign aid programs, etc. This is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity you will not receive again, and the technology hiring market has been recently unpredictable.

motions.m: TH, as a cat, would choose to become a petfluencer
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, you are a cat owned by a middle-income family living an average life. You are now given the opportunity to try to become an petfluencer. A petfluencers life varies, but can involve instagram photoshoots, funny meme videos, day in the life vlogs, etc.

motions.m: This House Prefers the Shift to Regional Climate Models over the Paris Agreement.
motions.i: The Paris Agreement is a landmark international treaty adopted in 2015, aiming to address climate change by limiting global temperature increases to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels, with efforts to limit the increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius. The US has recently withdrawn from the Paris Agreement for the second time, and many countries have failed to adhere to the Paris Agreement by missing their emission reduction targets, continuing fossil fuel investments, and not meeting financial commitments for climate adaptation in developing nations. Regional climate models are those which entail collaboration with proximate nations to develop targeted climate policies specific to the region of interest (e.g., the European Green Deal, African Climate Change Strategy).

motions.m: THR the emphasis on grades in Asian societies
THR the glorification of soldiers as heroes
THR the rise of social media as the primary source of news
THW require voters to pass a general knowledge test before voting

THP parenting models that adultify teenagers (giving them more responsibilities and autonomy, assuming higher capacity for independence) to those which emphasize their status and treatment as teenagers (refraining from giving them adult responsibilities, emphasizing they are legal minors)
THBT parents should strongly control and monitor their children's internet usage
THR the prominence of girlboss feminism
motions.i: "Girl boss' or '#girlboss' is a concept/phrase coined to signify a woman who is seen as being 'in control", dominant, determined and rebellious in pursuing her ambitions. The phrase is often used in campaigns as a slogan to inspire women to embody success by abandoning compliance and avoiding traits that have traditionally been associated with femininity and subservience, while pursuing their career ambitions.

motions.m: THBT the feminist movement should actively exclude activists who reject trans women
THS the narrative of always staying true your beliefs no matter what
THW erase the criminal past of ex-convicts who have been deemed fully rehabilitated by the technology.
motions.i: For the purpose of this motion, assume that a technology exists that can correctly predict whether an ex-convict will re-commit crime.

motions.m: THR the rise of social media as a primary source of news distribution
This house opposes the rise of Race-Blind Casting over Inclusive Casting in mainstream media
motions.i: Race-Blind Casting is the practice of casting actors regardless of their racial background aligning with the character. (e.g. Disney casting Halle Bailey, a black woman, to play Ariel in the Little Mermaid live action) Inclusive Casting considers race and ethnicity as valuable aspects of an actor's identity and storytelling. (e.g. Ian Ousley, a Cherokee native, plays Sokka, who is indigenous, in Avatar the Last Airbender the series)

motions.m: THR tiger parenting
motions.i: Tiger parenting is a form of strict parenting, whereby parents are highly invested in ensuring their children's success. Specifically, tiger parents push their children to attain high levels of academic achievement or success in high-status extracurricular activities such as music or sports.

motions.m: TH, as an artist, would opt into the use of AI
THP the OSSD model over the IGCSE model
motions.i: The Ontario Secondary Schools Diploma (OSSD) curriculum grades students on a project-centric basis, with 70% of students' grades coming from coursework and projects, with the remaining 30% coming from exams. The International General Certificate of Secondary Education (IGCSE) curriculum grades students on an exam-centric basis, with 75% of students' grades coming from exams, and the remaining 25% coming from coursework and projects

motions.m: THR the narrative that suffering builds character
TH, as one of the convicts, would choose to work together instead of alone
motions.i: You are a death row convict on a mission to retrieve "the elixir of life" which grants a person immortality if consumed. You alongside 12 other convicts have been sent on this mission with the promise of a full pardon to whoever successfully delivers the elixir, but only one person can receive this pardon. However the island it is located on, Shinsenkyo which is filled with mystical monsters and beings that humans have no experience fighting.

motions.m: THW choose to attend the elite boarding school
motions.i: You are a student who has received an offer to attend a prestigious boarding school in a foreign and objectively more developed country. Moving would involve uprooting the existing relationships and connections in your home country, in exchange for a more competitive schooling environment where you have no existing attachments.

motions.m: THS efforts to lower carbon emissions even if it comes at the expense of biodiversity (e.g. building hydroelectric dams in natural reserves, planting trees that kill biodiversity)
THR social justice movements employing antagonistic messaging
motions.i: Examples of antagonistic messaging include, "Eat The Rich", "Kill All Men", "All Cops Are Bad", etc.

motions.m: THS the practice of shaming parents who raise iPad children
motions.i: An iPad kid describes a child who is raised with an overreliance on technological devices (i.e. parents offering screentime to their children as an incentive to perform chores, infant tantrums being resolved with cocomelon videos)

motions.m: THS schools adopting open book testing as opposed to closed book testing
motions.i: Open book testing refers to students being able to use resources, such as their textbooks during tests. A closed book policy refers to testing whereby students take tests without access to external resources. It is used in the vast majority of international examination systems around the world (e.g. A-Level exams, AP exams, IB exams)

motions.m: THW attempt to avoid the canon event.
motions.i: In a world with different universes in the multiverse, a 'canon event' is a tragedy that is fated to happen. Straying away from the 'canon event' will destroy said universe, while other universes may continue to exist. You have been informed of an upcoming canon event that will kill all your loved ones.

motions.m: THBT parents should raise their children using gentle parenting as opposed to traditional parenting
motions.i: Gentle parenting refers to a style of parenting characterised by compassion and a focus on enforcing age-appropriate boundaries. In contrast, traditional parenting styles focus on punishment and reward as a way to incentivise compliance and behaviour.

motions.m: THBT primary school students (aged 12 and below) should be politically active (e.g. joining social movements, going to rallies)
TH, as the mermaid, would take the witch's deal.
motions.i: You are a mermaid tired of living under the sea who has rescued a prince from a shipwreck. Upon saving his life, you are filled with both a deep sense of mutual attraction for each other. In your pursuit for love, you visit the aquatic witch, who can grant you the ability to take on a human form and traverse the lands looking for the sailor, but at the expense of your voice.

motions.m: This house prefers a world without a conception of the afterlife
motions.i: The afterlife is a concept in philosophy and religion that claims that individuals carry on living in one form or another after death. e.g Reincarnation, Heaven, and Hell.

motions.m: This house would ban all procedures to alter one's racial appearance (skin whitening creams, surgery)

THW ban junk food

motions.i: "The mediocre life" describes a life that is ordinary, plain, and unremarkable. Those who live mediocre lives are individuals who have not achieved significant success or failure in various areas of their lives (e.g. academic-wise, career-wise).

motions.m: This house believes that the government should provide incentives for the urban poor to migrate to rural areas.
This house prefers establishing female-only leagues for esports rather than creating affirmative action in existing esports leagues.
This house as the scientist would reveal the information to the world
motions.i: You are a scientist. While working alone in your lab you discover that the end of the world is happening in exactly 5 years and that it cannot be stopped. As the only expert in the world in this area, you are certain that no one else is going to discover this information.

THW ban violent combat based sports (Boxing, MMA, etc.)

THBT Batman should abandon his "No-kill" rule
motions.i: In most versions of the Batman story, Batman has a strict "no-kill" rule. This means he refuses to kill anyone, including dangerous criminals like the Joker. He believes that killing makes him no better than the criminals he fights, and that justice should be left to the law. However, some argue that this rule allows dangerous villains to escape and hurt more people, since they often break out of prison again and again.

motions.m: This House Opposes the posthumous publication of works of art that their creators explicitly did not want published (e.g., 'Until August' by Gabriel García Márquez, 'The Trial' by Franz Kafka, or Emily Dickinson's correspondence).

THW prohibit the dismissal of employees for their use of free speech or expression outside the workplace (e.g. on social media)
THW would introduce significant mandatory minimum prison sentences for white collar crimes
THP a world where everyone has a likeometer
motions.i: A "likeometer" is a hypothetical number above someone's head that indicates how much they platonically like you on a scale of 1-10. This number adjusts constantly, and is impossible to hide or manipulate. A likeometer only allows you to see your own scores, and not how much the person likes other individuals.

motions.m: THBT workers should be allowed to trade off certain employment standards (e.g., overtime pay, paid time off, health insurance, etc.) in exchange for their monetary equivalent
THR China's ban on plastic waste imports
THP a world with Iranian Nuclear Weapons.
THR the narrative that humility is a virtue
THW ban external political groups from funding student protests on university campuses
THP lay juries over expert juries for criminal cases
motions.i: For this debate, assume that juries in criminal cases only decide the verdict (guilty or not) and do not sentence. Expert juries are juries consisting of 2/3 non-lawyers who take a 4-week course about criminal law and then take on a 20h paid part-time job in the court to decide criminal cases for a duration of 1-2years. To become an expert jury you have to complete a competitive hiring process. Lay Juries are randomly selected by mail and then further picked by the prosecutor and defence attorney. These juries have no legal knowledge and do not get any teaching on the functioning of the law. They only act as jury for one case after which they are dismissed again. They are partially compensated for their time.

motions.m: THW mandate that all social media accounts operate under verified real names and identities
TH, as the next papal conclave, W choose Cardinal Fridolin Ambongo Besungu rather than Cardinal Luis Antonio Tagle as the successor to Pope Francis
motions.i: Cardinal Fridolin Ambongo Besungu from the Democratic Republic of the Congo is a significant voice in the African Catholic Church. On theology, he's a more conservative voice in the Catholic Church, publicly emphasizing the importance of upholding traditional Catholic teachings on marriage and sexuality. He is known for his strong social justice advocacy, frequently condemning economic exploitation, environmental destruction and is a critical voice against neocolonialism in Africa. Cardinal Luis Antonio Tagle from the Philippines one of the most progressive voices in the Catholic Church and is often viewed as a close ally of Pope Francis. Tagle has been vocal about the Church's treatment of marginalized communities, particularly LGBTQ individuals, divorced and remarried Catholics, and migrants. While he has not explicitly advocated for doctrinal changes, his speeches often highlight compassion over rigid rules and he has warned against a judgmental Church that alienates people rather than bringing them closer to Christ. Additionally, Tagle has famously championed the cause of refugees and migrant laborers, calling for more humane immigration policies.

motions.m: THR the rise of cut-off culture
motions.i: Cut-off culture refers to the trend of individuals abruptly ending interpersonal relationships, with the justification that they are becoming harmful or toxic.

motions.m: THBT developing countries should prioritize reducing the scope of factory farming over reducing the net use of fossil fuels
THBT the removal of the Assad regime in Syria is in the interests of the U.S.
TH would place a cap on the overall amount of wealth that parents can pass down to their children (e.g. inheritance, trust funds, gifts that are given after they turn 18, etc.)
THP the graduate model of licensed professions to the undergraduate model
motions.i: There are two models academic institutions use to award practioner licenses and education in licensed fields (e.g. law, medicine, therapy...). The first is the graduate model, where students pursue an advanced degree in their licensing field after completing an undergraduate degree in a different field. Under the graduate model, student apply and study in a designated school or institution for their field (e.g. law school, medical school...). The second model is the undergraduate model, where student pursue a skilled degree and practioner license as part of their first, undergraudate degree, as they would in any non-licensed field.

motions.m: THO the practice of police using undercover operations
motions.i: Undercover operations include members of the police force/intelligence forces infiltrating into criminal cells and masquerading as members of the group.

motions.m: THS the rise of Superapps
motions.i: A Superapp refers to a mobile app that provides the primary functions of a smartphone typically accessed through multiple apps (e.g. instant messaging, online marketplaces, tap-payments etc..) in a single app platform. Examples of Superapps include WeChat in China, Grab in Southeast Asia, Tata Neu in India.

motions.m: In regions struck by natural disasters (e.g. wildfires, hurricanes, severe earth contamination etc.), THBT governments should permit inhabitants to remain in their area as opposed to mandating a regional evacuation
THO Outcast Art
motions.i: Outcast art refers to pieces that express an artist's sense of rejection, segregation or deep sense of distinction from society around them. Examples include songs like "Creep" by Radiohead and "Walk on the Wild Side" by Lou Reed, and the movies "Joker" and "Birdman".

motions.m: THBT the U.S. should aggressively pursue the development of a domestic renewable energy sector
motions.i: Renewable energy relies on resources that are naturally self-replenishing. These include solar, wind, hydropower and others. Currently, China dominates production and consumption in the renewable energy sector -it annually installs half the world's PV cells (solar panels), it is the world's biggest producer, exporter and installer of wind turbines, and roughly %30 of its domestic energy consumption comes from renewable sources. By contrast, renewable energy constitutes only 8% of the U.S.'s domestic energy consumption, and most of the U.S.'s solar panels and turbines are imported.

motions.m: TH, as Mimi, Would enter San Junipero
motions.i: San Junipero is a Virtual Reality universe which simulates real life perfectly - people who enter it experience the world exactly as they would their life outside of it. San Junipero brings any person who enters it back into their twenty-year old self and body, and into an exact replica of their life at the time. Once one enters San Junipero, they remain there for the rest of their life, and are completely unaware they are in a simulation. You are Mimi, an 80-year old widow, mother and grandmother who lives alone.

motions.m: This house prefers a world where the legacies of violent radical revolutionaries are celebrated more than the legacies of revolutionaries who prioritized nonviolent means of reconciliation
TH, as America, supports the Trump-Vance "America First" policy plan
motions.i: "America First" is a central economic and foreign policy slogan of the 2024 Trump-Vance election campaign. "America First" policy proposals include, but are not limited to: imposing tariffs on foreign imports, restricting immigration, and withdrawing assistance to Ukraine.

motions.m: THR the narrative that romantic relationships should last forever
THBT using problem-solving coping is better than emotion-focused coping
motions.i: Problem-solving coping mechanisms are action-based approaches where individuals try to fix problems causing stressors or distract themselves with activities. Emotion-focused coping mechanisms are approaches focused on methods like seeking emotional support, self-reflection, and talking emotions through.

motions.m: THO the general approach of companies to grow by massively sacrificing short-term profitability with the hope of gaining a large portion of the market (e.g. the original business model of companies such as Uber or Amazon)
THO the rise of private credit
motions.i: Private credit refers to loans and financing provided by non-bank institutions directly to businesses or individuals, often involving investors like private equity firms or specialized funds. Unlike traditional bank loans, private credit is less regulated and does not typically involve public trading, offering investors the chance to earn higher returns in exchange for more risk. After the rising interest rates seen within the US market after Covid-19's rock bottome rates, the private credit market has rapidly expanded to feed rising high-risk financing needs that fall outside the usual scope of banks, such as corporate buyouts, growth capital, or distressed lending situations.

motions.m: THR the predominance of leadership as a desired character trait
THP a world where the Buddhist view of salvation is dominant to Christianity.
motions.i: In Buddhism, salvation is described as achieving nirvana, a state of liberation from the "three poisons" of greed, hatred, and ignorance. It is attained through personal practice of meditation, self-enlightenment, and virtue. Alternatively, salvation in Christianity is understood as being saved from sin and eternal damnation. Salvation is granted by God's grace, through faith in Jesus Christ and following divine teachings.

motions.m: THW ban large corporations from funding research
TH, as USA, would offer Türkiye a spot in their Artemis program
motions.i: The Turkish Space Agency was first founded in 2018. In 2021, President Erdogan announced a 10-year plan for the agency with objectives like developing domestic satellites and landing on the Moon independently by 2028. In January 2024, Turkish astronauts arrived in space for the first time on a SpaceX rocket and in December 2024, the government began work on a spaceport in Somalia, a project estimated to cost $350m. The Turkish government has also confirmed its wishes to use the spaceport as a missile testing site for its booming defence industry. NASA's Artemis program is an American Moon exploration program established in 2017 with the goal to reestablish human presence on the Moon. Turkey has also applied to the International Lunar Research Station (ILRS), a Chinese-Russian alternative to the Artemis program which similarly hopes to establish a permanent base on the moon

motions.m: THBT environmental activists should abandon the narrative that climate disaster is inevitable
THBT parents and children should not be friends
THBT emotional connection should be prioritized over technical playing in early music education
TH, as the Ontario Premier Douglas Ford, would force a substantial increase in the use of multi-use and multi-family zoning in Toronto suburbs.
motions.i: Zoning is a method to dictate the use of land in a given area (e.g. residential, industrial) and may also govern the density and size of allowed buildings. Single-use zoning means that residential housing is separated from commercial buildings. Multi-family zoning features the construction of properties with more than one unit, like apartments, townhouses, or duplexes. The Toronto suburbs is defined as cities and townships currently within York and Peel Region.

motions.m: THW cut off contact with their crush over trying to stay friends
motions.i: You are a 24-year old man who has been close friends with your crush for 2 years. Recently, you confessed your feelings, but they turned you down, expressing a desire to remain just friends. The rejection leaves you feeling emotionally conflicted and you're unsure whether maintaining the friendship is healthy or even possible moving forward.

motions.m: THR the rise of academic piracy sites
motions.i: Academic piracy sites refers to sites that illegally upload copyrighted media, specifically academic material (e.g. textbooks, academic papers) and makes it publicly available for free.

motions.m: TH, as USA, would offer Turkey a spot in their Artemis program
motions.i: The Turkish Space Agency was first founded in 2018. In 2021, President Erdogan announced a 10-year plan for the agency with objectives like developing domestic satellites and landing on the Moon independently by 2028. In January 2024, Turkish astronauts arrived in space for the first time on a SpaceX rocket and in December 2024, the government began work on a spaceport in Somalia, a project estimated to cost $350m. The Turkish government has also confirmed its wishes to use the spaceport as a missile testing site for its booming defense industry. NASA's Artemis program is an American Moon exploration program established in 2017 with the goal to reestablish human presence on the Moon. Turkey has also applied to the International Lunar Research Station (ILRS), a Chinese-Russian alternative to the Artemis program which similarly hopes to establish a permanent base on the moon.

motions.m: TH, as Mark Carney, would support a Cap and Trade Emissions System
motions.i: A cap-and-trade system aims to reduce pollution by setting a cap on how much pollution companies can create, based on the needs of the industry. Companies acquire permits that let them produce a certain amount of emissions. If they pollute less, they can sell their extra permits to others or back to the government for a profit/refund. If they need to pollute more, they have to buy extra permits. Mark Carney is a former Governor of the Bank of Canada and Bank of England, former financial policy advisor to the liberal party, he is largely expected to win the ongoing Liberal leadership race. He has been a vocal supporter of the liberal Carbon Tax in the past.

motions.m: THBT parents should not inflate their children's expectations of what they are capable of achieving
THS the decline of musical racialization
motions.i: Musical racialization is the process of associating specific music genres with particular racial or ethnic groups. It often translates into racial ownership and appropriation of themes, tonality, rhythms, and even marketing of music brands, labels, and artists.

motions.m: TH, as the feminist movement in developed countries, W advocate for increased attention on male issues (e.g. higher rates of loneliness, lower tertiary education and employment rates, custody inequalities)
THW implement blind voting
motions.i: Blind voting refers to a system where voters cast their votes for various policies and a candidate is matched to them based on their preferences.

motions.m: THP a world where memory is permanent and infallible
motions.i: Infallible means "being incapable of being incorrect".

motions.m: THBT the Brazilian government should extend the Trans-Amazonian Highway to Pucallpa
motions.i: The Trans-Amazonian Highway starts from Brazil's Atlantic Coast and stretches 4000km across the Amazon, stopping at The Uncontacted Frontier. The Frontier contains the highest concentration of isolated tribes. Some tribes have asked for support from the Brazilian government. Others choose to be alone, and many have never been contacted. On the other side of the Frontier is the Peruvian city of Pucallpa, a thriving city with a bustling economy.

motions.m: THBT the child should leave Japan and move to the US
motions.i: There is a 12-year-old child born and raised in Tokyo. They've always lived paycheck to paycheck with their mother, the only family they've ever known. They recently found out that they were switched in the hospital as a baby, and that their real parents have been trying to get in touch with them for the past 12 years. Their real parents are suburban middle-class convenience store owners in Columbus, Ohio, who have two kids the same age as them. The parents have mailed them a letter; inside it is a one-way ticket to Ohio.

motions.m: THBT individuals should never opt into jōhatsu
motions.i: In Japan, jōhatsu refers to people who abandon their lives without a trace at night and live anonymously elsewhere, with new names, identities, and backstories. Specialized companies, called night movers, assist jōhatsu in gathering their things, escorting them, and helping them vanish completely.

motions.m: This House would mandate carbon pricing for all industries
This House opposes cosmic justice
motions.i: Cosmic Justice is a belief system based on the idea of equalising the prospects of each individual. This is characterised by developing systems that cater to the shortcomings of each individual in order to elevate their prospect of success instead of applying the same rules and standards to all individuals with little to no regard for difference in ability. Proponents of cosmic justice fundamentally have the desire to rectify social inequities that people who, through no fault of their own, lack things which other people receive as windfall gains, through no virtue of their own.

motions.m: This House Supports academic streaming
motions.i: Academic streaming is the process of grouping students of similar abilities into classes. For example, a student may be streamed into a basic, intermediate, or advanced maths class depending on their ability.

motions.m: This house would mandate all citizens to undergo a period of working for national service, in sectors including but not limited to military, charity, and civil services (e.g. police, firefighting).
THO the glorification of selflessness

motions.i: Primary domestic caregivers are individuals who are in charge of household management, for example, shopping for groceries, cooking for the family and childcare.

motions.m: THBT professional sports leagues should implement a fixed, equal salary for all of their players.
This House would put an upper age limit for individuals running for political office.
THBT the feminist movement should actively support depictions of reverse sexism in the media
motions.i: Reverse sexism is the practice of flipping sexist behaviours that typically apply to one gender so that they are instead applied to another (e.g., Barbieland's matriarchy in Greta Gerwig's "Barbie", Anne Hathaway playing an older woman in a romance with a much younger man in "The Idea of You", Sandra Oh's onscreen husband in "Killing Eve" playing a gender inverted "nagging wife" who no one will listen to and Eve treats with contempt)

motions.m: THP to live an average anonymous life in South Korea over becoming a K-POP star
THR private Health Insurance
motions.i: Private Health Insurance (PHI) refers to a system where individuals, families, or employers purchase healthcare coverage from private insurance companies, in the form of regular payments called premiums. This coverage helps pay for medical expenses, such as hospital stays, doctor visits, etc.

motions.m: THO the rationalisation of celebrity suicide
motions.i: Rationalisation refers to the process of explaining or justifying an event, often to make it more understandable or palatable

motions.m: THS the rise of self deprecating humour
THP a world where the existence of purgatory was the dominant religious norm.
motions.i: Purgatory is the concept where those who are seen as 'morally grey' or 'morally neutral' are placed into an afterlife which is neither heaven nor hell, but somewhere in the middle. This is in contrast to most religious beliefs which only have a heaven or hell

motions.m: THW call their ex
motions.i: You are a college student. You and your high-school sweetheart have been in a committed, loving relationship for the past year, but due to a two year age difference, you are at vastly different stages in life. This relationship has historically been unstable, and you have broken up and gotten back together multiple times. However, this is your first exposure to true affection, as you have been neglected by your parents. Your partner is destined to leave the country at some point in the next year. On the 16th of January, your partner suddenly breaks up with you for no apparent reason and goes no contact. On the 28th of August, you find out that your ex has unblocked you, with a text message that says "I'm leaving next week, call me".

motions.m: This House believes that the educational system should lean into the prevalence of short attention span (e.g. by significantly using audiovisual stimuli, bite-size content, and numerous breaks in class) as opposed to combating them (e.g. by requiring longer reading of books or articles, prolonged period of focused activities, etc.)
This House believes that BRICS expansion is in the interests of Latin American nations
motions.i: BRICS is a group of emerging economies created to coordinate its members' economic and diplomatic policies, found new financial institutions, and reduce dependence on the U.S. dollar, such as through establishing the New Development Bank (NDB) and Contingent Reserve Arrangement (CRA) as alternatives to Bretton-Woods institutions. BRICS current has ten member states: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa, Iran, Egypt, Ethiopia, the United Arab Emirates, and Indonesia.

motions.m: This House would limit the voting rights of large institutional investors in publicly traded companies
motions.i: In recent years, a small number of institutional investor (e.g. big asset managing firms such as BlackRock and Vanguard) have accumulated large amounts of stocks in thousands of publicly traded companies through index funds. An index fund aims to copy the performance of a specific stock market index (like the S&P 500) by buying all (or most) of the stocks in that index. These firms, acting to represent many individual investors, often exercise voting rights on behalf of those investors, shaping corporate policy on issues ranging from corporate management to social responsibilities.

motions.m: This house regrets the narrative that "time is money"

In countries with rising popularity of the hard right, this House believes that ruling left-wing parties should push for moderate immigration restrictions rather than double down on pro-immigration stances
THO the stigmatization of transactional close friendships
This House believes that drug or alcohol rehabilitation services provided by religious organizations bring more harm than good
This House would treat access to social support services as a mitigating factor in criminal sentencing

This house believes that post-conflict states with a highly multiethnic population should use a system of proportional representation rather than first-past-the-post in national parliamentary elections
motions.i: Proportional representation is an electoral system that elects multiple representatives in each district, in proportion to the number of people who vote for each party. Districts are typically larger to accommodate more Congressional seats per district. Comparatively, in First-past-the-post electoral systems, political parties compete for every district's seat individually, and the party with the highest percentage of votes in that district wins its respective seat. Districts are typically smaller, with only 1-2 representatives per district

motions.m: THW impose mandatory military service for all citizens over 18.

This house supports the rise in the use of drugs aiming to enhance cognition (e.g., memory, attention span, processing speed, etc.)

This house regrets the narrative that your 20s are your defining decade (e.g., trying out new things, figuring it out)

This house believes that all art displayed in public institutions should be presented without any information on the creator (including name, country of origin, background information, etc).
This house opposes tourism to sites of atrocities (e.g., Palmyra in Syria, Auschwitz concentration camp, suicide forest in Japan, Robben island)

motions.i: The concept of "feminine genius", popularized by Pope John Paul II refers to the unique qualities and strengths inherent to women that enable them to build a culture of life and contribute to society in distinctive ways. It emphasizes women's moral, spiritual, and social capacities, especially receptivity, sensitivity, generosity, and maternity.

motions.m: TH, as the UN, would not impose the decarbonisation conditions on emerging economies (e.g. BRICs countries, South Africa)
THBT major sports clubs should be community owned rather than private
In countries with high unemployment rates, THS the Informal Economy
motions.i: The informal economy is the part of any economy that is not taxed or monitored by any form of government. The informal economy provides opportunities for employment and revenue generation bypassing formal requirements, e.g. setting up a shop without a business registration/licence, private bus owners hiring bus drivers without a formal employment contract, etc. However, it does not guarantee any social benefits such as health insurance or job security. Although it occurs in all countries, the informal sector is particularly pertinent in the Global South, making up a substantial portion of these economies.

motions.m: THW allow judges to signficantly exceed normal sentencing guidelines in areas of high crimes.
THBT the rise in popularity of "healthism", in the countries with private healthcare, brings more harm than good
motions.i: "Healthism" is the idea that a person's health is entirely their responsibility and moral obligation. It includes stressing the importance of personal dietary and lifestyle decisions on an individual's physical and mental health and wellbeing. This narrative promotes, among other things: the significance of physical activity (e.g. fitness), awareness and use of diets, the practice of yoga/meditation, as well as the use of preventive medicines and suplements to support one's own immune system.

motions.m: TH regrets the narrative of a benevolent God
motions.i: The phrase 'Benevolent God' refers to the popular depiction of God as a being of the highest mercy, full of absolute kindness, compassion and capable of forgiving even the most atrocious wrongdoings.

motions.m: THP a world where art is anonymous
THBT the UN should sanction nearby states that have turned away Rohingyan refugees
motions.i: The recent refugee crisis in Myanmar has been caused by the violent persecution of the Rohingya Muslim minority group by the Myanmar military. The Rohingya have been subjected to various forms of oppression and abuse, including torture, murder, and forced displacement. As a result, hundreds of thousands of Rohingya have fled to neighboring countries such as Bangladesh, Malaysia, and Thailand, seeking safety and shelter. The crisis has caused widespread international concern and has prompted a humanitarian response from various organizations and governments around the world.

motions.m: THR the rise of health and medical influencers in social media platforms
THS the establishment of regional peacekeeping forces such as the African Union

motions.i: 'Pick me girls' are women who deliberately behave in a way to appeal to male approval. Examples include putting down other women, denouncing activities traditionally thought to be 'feminine', and limiting interactions with women.

motions.m: THBT renewable energy investment in the Philippines should focus on small-scale power generation (hydroelectric turbine stations in rivers, rooftop wind turbines, local biofuel processing) over large-scale power generation (large dams, solar farms, windfarms)

motions.i: In popular culture, 'fan works' refer to the creative activities of fans, including fanfiction, fanart, fan edits, and fan-made merchandise about works of fiction and/or real celebrities themselves.

motions.m: THW break up Big Tech companies (Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Facebook, etc.)
TH, as a queer adult that was raised in a conservative family, would attempt to distance from the family rather than attempt to change the minds of the family
THO the use of the "it gets better" narrative by progressive movements in the developing world
THP to live in a world where everyone is born with a niche skill set
motions.i: There is a world where every individual is born with a niche skill set that they are innately proficient at. Other people may have the same niche skill set, but those that have a different niche will not be as proficient in the niche skill that another is born with.

motions.m: THS environmentalist movements using doomsday narratives in combatting climate change
motions.i: A doomsday narrative is a strategy adopted by environmentalists to foretell the future state of our environment. It is usually accompanied with catastrophic descriptions of what the Earth will look like in the near future i.e. devastating fires, rampant flooding, destruction of homes due to rising sea levels, etc.

motions.m: As the Filipino people, TH prefers a national identity based on a shared anti-colonial struggle rather than a reconstructed precolonial history

This House Opposes the European Union emphasising the need for domestic political reforms over economic reforms for member candidates (e.g., prioritising reforms of the electoral system and the judiciary rather than the economic development and the improvement of the welfare of its citizens when assessing the country's readiness to join EU)

This House Prefers a future in which generative AI is further developed and implemented in significantly more fields, compared to a future in which the development and implementation of generative AI stays within its current confines
motions.i: Generative AI is a type of artificial intelligence that can create new and original content. In the status quo, it is being applied to the production of text, programming, images, and sound. Generative AI is currently unable to design architecture, pharmaceuticals, or conduct research.

motions.m: This House Opposes the stigmatisation of "neediness" in interpersonal relationships
motions.i: "Neediness" is a characteristic used to refer to individual(s) in an interpersonal relationship who has/have a strong preference to receive emotional support, frequent attention, over-reassurance for anxious feelings, and/or higher maintenance for their emotional needs. Interpersonal relationships span across diverse categories like romance, families, friendship, etc.

motions.m: This House Would mandate the consumption of The Elixir of Truth during all proceedings of criminal trials by all participants (i.e. defendants, plaintiffs, eyewitnesses, prosecutors, judges, court reporters, testifying specialists, etc.)
motions.i: A scientist has just invented The Elixir of Truth. People under the influence of The Elixir of Truth are not able to tell what they believe to be a lie, and are compelled to answer questions to the full extent of the truthoods they think are relevant to the question. The Elixir is non-invasive, and the effects stop immediately after 2 hours. You can repeatedly and endlessly take The Elixir of Truth with zero side effects or health ramifications.

motions.m: In public service industries, This House Believes That labour unions should engage in Italian strikes as opposed to conventional strikes
motions.i: Italian strike is a collective labour protest action where workers do not put in more work than mandated in their job contract, regardless of the usual practices of their place of employment. This can look like: 9-to-5 workers leaving work at exactly 5 PM even if their work is unfinished, employees not participating in extra initiatives of their workplace without getting paid. In contrast, conventional strike is an unpaid mass work stoppage where a labour union's members do not work but picket and protest.

motions.m: This House Believes That schools should prioritise fostering students' internal motivation rather than emphasising compliance to external motivations
motions.i: Educational models present various forms of student motivation. Examples of internal motivation could be: creating more opportunities for students to learn autonomously, fostering a sense of curiosity towards learning, and/or being adaptable to individual strengths, weaknesses, passions and dislikes. Examples of external motivation can look like: placing universal standards on students (such as grades or exams), rewarding those who adhere to them and punishing those who don't.

motions.m: This House, as the doctor, Would abandon RMS Titanic to board the last lifeboat
motions.i: The year is 1912. You have just completed 8 years of education to obtain your medical degree and surgical license. You are looking forward to a lifetime of operating on patients to save lives, as that is a dream you've had since early childhood, one of your deep passions. Fresh out of med-school, the first job you get is to be a medic on RMS Titanic. You bring your only child along with you for a trip to New York while your wife stays at home, eagerly waiting for your safe return. Four days into the voyage the Titanic hits an iceberg that causes it to start sinking. All chaos breaks loose when it is discovered that there are not enough lifeboats to carry all passengers. In the midst of all the people pushing and shoving to get a spot on a lifeboat, your son gets heavily injured. Due to the extent of his injuries, your son is refused a spot on the lifeboat because they are prioritizing healthy passengers with a higher chance of survival. You yourself however, get offered a spot on the last lifeboat because the passengers said they'll have a higher chance of survival with a doctor onboard. You are aware the Titanic has sent out a distress signal calling on other ships to come help, however you do not know whether or not anyone has answered the call.

motions.m: This House Believes That living in a world in which the majority of people would switch the track is preferable to living in a world in which the majority of people would not switch the track
motions.i: The trolley problem is a thought experiment that evaluates the morality of sacrificing one person in order to save a larger number of people. The ethical dilemma sketches a scenario in which a runaway trolley is on course to collide with 5 people down the track who are unable to move out of the way and are certain to be killed upon contact with the trolley. You, the only bystander, are the only one who can intervene to divert the trolley to switch to a different track, where there is only 1 person on the track. You are unable to stop the trolley, or in any way communicate the imminent danger to anyone.

motions.m: THR the eastern enlargement* of the European Union
motions.i: The Eastern enlargement refers to the 2004 ascension of Cyprus, the Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Slovakia, and Slovenia into the European Union

motions.m: THS the development of genetic engineering
motions.i: Genetic engineering (also called genetic modification) is a process that uses laboratory-based technologies to alter the DNA makeup of an organism. This may involve changing a single base pair, deleting a region of DNA or adding a new segment of DNA. For example, genetic engineering may involve adding a gene from one species to an organism from a different species to produce a desired trait. Used in research and industry, genetic engineering has been applied to the production of cancer therapies, brewing yeasts, genetically modified plants and livestock, and more. Currently, genetic engineering is being worked on by scientists in order to be applied on humans in the womb.

motions.m: This House as the environmental movement would support the use of extremist tactics
motions.i: Extremist tactics includes the destruction of property and large-scale civil disobedience i.e blocking roads and buildings

motions.m: THW ban pet ownership
THW Ban Mail order brides
motions.i: A mail order bride is a woman who seeks a marriage partner through a service or agency that arranges international marriages, typically involving men from wealthier countries who are seeking partners from countries with fewer economic opportunities, most commonly Southeast Asia, Eastern Europe and Latin America. The term often implies that the process is transactional, where the woman may be selected or matched based on personal preferences, sometimes facilitated through correspondence or online platforms.

motions.m: THS Estonia's proposal to revoke local voting rights from Citizens of Agressor States
motions.i: In Estonia, permanent residents, regardless of their citizenship status, currently have the right to vote in local council elections (_municipal elections_). Estonia is currently planning to amend its constitution to revoke voting rights in local elections for residents who are citizens of "aggressor states", this impacts approximately 80,000 Russian citizens and 3,000 Belarusian citizens in Estonia. Stateless individuals (a category that includes many ethnic Russians), citizens of EU or NATO member states will not be affected. The government aims to implement this policy before the next local elections, scheduled for October 2025. The total population of ethnic Russians in Estonia is estimated to be around 296 000.

motions.m: THP a world in which all people have chronic Pinocchiosis
motions.i: Chronic Pinocchiosis is a condition that causes an individual's nose to horizontally grow by a millimetre every time they knowingly lie. Once it grows, it does not shrink back, and it is magically immune to rhinoplasty or any other attempt at artificial shrinkage. Long noses do not cause any significant health issues and nose growth caused by Chronic Pinocchiosis is not hereditary.

motions.m: THBT businesses should not take a public position on social issues
THBT the job application process should be anonymized (concealing applicant identity until the position has been accepted)
TH prefers a world where individuals are assigned their profession at birth and groomed to fit that profession.
THW actively support privately-funded space research and exploration
THBT all examinations should be open book.
motions.i: An 'open-book examination' is an assessment method designed to allow students to refer to approved material during the examination. This includes, but are not limited to, class handouts, written notes, summaries, or textbooks while answering questions under controlled conditions.

motions.m: THBT buy-now-pay-later services should be banned.
motions.i: Buy-now-pay-later services allow consumers to make purchases and pay for them in a predetermined number of interest-free instalments. Unlike credit cards, these services do not require the examination of your income and existing debts to determine whether you will be able to meet your repayments on the money they are lending you. Users are charged late fees if they miss a payment, and are charged higher fees to use the service in the future if repayments are not being paid on time.

motions.m: THBT travelers should boycott indigenous tourism destinations
THW link minimum wage rates to inflation
motions.i: In economics, inflation is a general increase in the prices of goods and services in an economy. When the general price level rises, each unit of currency buys fewer goods and services; consequently, inflation corresponds to a reduction in the purchasing power of money.

motions.m: TH Opposes the commercialization of cloning
motions.i: Cloning is the process of producing individual organisms with identical or virtually identical DNA. In the US, companies currently offer pet cloning as a commercial service. This technology is improving.

motions.m: THBT the US should fund satellite internet coverage in Russia
motions.i: Satellite internet is a method of providing internet connection using low-orbit satellites rather than telecommunication grids. It also has the ability to circumvent local internet censorship. An example of this is Elon Musk's Starlink

motions.m: THR the critique of 1990s & 2000s media for not meeting current social justice standards
motions.i: There is a trend of critiquing media from the 1990s and 2000s for not displaying modern conceptions of social justice. For example, critiquing the feminism or casting diversity in Gilmore Girls or Buffy the Vampire Slayer, or critiquing the politics of Harry Potter

motions.m: TH, as the feminist movement, celebrates the portrayal of women as the source of fear in films (ex. Miranda Priestly in the Devil Wears Prada, Carrie in Carrie, Dolores Umbridge in Harry Potter)
TH, as a highly educated professional living in an authoritarian state, would refuse to work with or for the government
motions.i: In governance, authoritarianism denotes any political system that concentrates power in the hands of a leader or a small elite that is not constitutionally responsible to the body of the people.

motions.m: THW introduce a quota for climate change refugees, where refugees are allocated in proportion to countries' carbon emissions
motions.i: Carbon dioxide emissions or CO2 emissions are emissions stemming from the burning of fossil fuels and other process, they include carbon dioxide produced during consumption of solid, liquid, and gas fuels as well as gas flaring. Carbon dioxide is largely associated with global warming and its regulation have been the target of environmental policies.

motions.m: THW not allow tourists at historical religious sites (e.g. the Hagia Sophia, St. Peter's Basilica, Westminster Chapel)
motions.i: Historical religious sites are places and structures that were made for religious worship. Over time, they have gained importance in both the religions they represent and in world history.

motions.m: This House would have schools actively encourage children to question authority
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, authority can have a range of different meanings, including the knowledge you learn, teachers at school, the police and government in everyday life, and parents within families. Questioning that authority can involve a wide variety of different things, whether it be through words (verbal), actions, or just the attitude with which you treat that authority. Opening Government is allowed to set the exact kinds of policy they might support as to how schools may encourage the questioning of authority (though they cannot mandate that everyone will necessarily listen!)

motions.m: This House would restrict research and development of artificial intelligence
motions.i: Artificial intelligence means the simulation of advanced human intelligence processes by machines. Examples of artificial intelligence include facial recognition, self-driving cars, and robots that can act without needing to be controlled by a person.

motions.m: This house believes that people should be required to obtain a parenting licence in order to have children
motions.i: A 'parenting licence' means permission from the government for adults to have and raise their own children. Parenting licences could be tied to specific conditions, like knowledge of healthy eating or having enough money to pay for the child's basic needs

motions.m: This House believes that children's film makers should avoid making animated films that contain darker themes
motions.i: Many older animated children's films featured dark story themes that are less present in children's films today. For example,'Pocahontas' featured themes such as colonialism and murder, 'The Lion King' featured a violent on-screen murder, and the death of Bambi's mum introduced children to the concept of parental death. The debate does not have to be limited to these examples and can extend to other animated films

motions.m: This House would provide funding primarily to established businesses rather than new businesses
motions.i: An established business is a business that has been operating for more than one calendar year and has consistent demand for its product.

motions.m: This House advocates human colonization of outer space.
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, colonisation is the act of occupying the new land and establishing new infrastructure such as homes, offices and power grids for electricity (much like you would if wanted to create your own country in a new world)! Outer space is the space outside of the earth including the planets, stars, and the space between the planets

motions.m: This house would extend the rights of personhood to certain 'higher' animals (e.g., elephants, dolphins, chimpanzees)
motions.i: Rights of personhood include the right to live, the right to be free from confinement or slavery, and the ability to be free from unethical treatment such as scientific experiments.

motions.m: THW decriminalise and encourage graffiti art in public places
motions.i: Graffiti art means art that has been painted on buildings, sidewalks and other infrastructure without prior permission from the owner.

motions.m: This House Would make schools put students into classes based on their academic ability, not their age
motions.i: In most countries, children in primary and secondary school are sorted into classes based on their age. Academic ability refers to the knowledge of the students, their academic results and level of engagement with the material being taught

motions.m: That we regret the narrative that individual consumption decisions are a useful way to tackle climate change (for example, reducing meat consumption, recycling more, etc.)
motions.i: Narratives surrounding what is and is not useful for tackling climate change have been a large factor in shaping individual, governmental, and corporate actions as climate change became a more pressing concern throughout history.

motions.m: That we prefer a world with memory writing technology
motions.i: That we prefer a world with memory writing technology

motions.m: That the state should offer a wage to primary domestic caregivers Primary domestic caregivers are individuals who are in charge of household management, for example, shopping for groceries, cooking for the family and childcare.
motions.i: Primary domestic caregivers are individuals who are in charge of household management, for example, shopping for groceries, cooking for the family and childcare.

motions.m: That hosting a World Cup in a country with a poor human rights record does more harm than good
That Ukraine should cease attempts to engage Russia in peace talks until Ukraine takes back all its territory before Russia's invasion
motions.i: Ukraine is currently attempting to engage Russia in peace talks, but both sides are claiming that the other country has no actual desire for peace.

motions.m: That, when teaching about social equality, schools should deprioritise education about the history of oppression minorities have faced and instead focus on the principles of equality, social outcomes today, and methods to improve these in the future
motions.i: In many countries, education around social inequality focuses on the history of oppression vulnerable groups have faced, for example, how women were granted the vote, how colonialism destroyed indigenous cultures, and how racial minorities were discriminated against by governments. For the purposes of this motion, the government would instead, for example, focus on teaching current census data to determine which groups are being oppressed, and teaching theory around how equality might be achieved in the future.

motions.m: That major theatre houses/companies in Asia should deprioritize the staging of popular Western shows in favour of more local productions
motions.i: A theatre production is considered to be "Western" when it was originally created and shown in a Western nation, or written by a Western writer. Examples of Western productions that have been staged in Asia include Matilda, Mamma Mia, Phantom of the Opera and Les Miserables. Non-Western productions include Mula Sa Buwan, Orphan Zhao, The Secret and Last Ruler of Southern Tang.

motions.m: That art generated by artificial intelligence (e.g. images, paintings, poetry, text) which is specifically based on the work of an artist should be legally considered intellectual property of that artist
motions.i: Intellectual Property Law (otherwise known as copyright law) protects the ability of an individual to own and profit from their own creativity and intellectual effort. For example, IP law often protects artists from having their profits stolen off them by someone else who might copy lyrics of their song. For the purposes of this motion, if someone were to generate art from artificial intelligence "in the style of Vincent Van Gogh", intellectual property of that art would like solely at the hands of Vincent Van Gough

motions.m: That all educational institutions should be required to provide students with online attendance options, even after COVID-19
motions.i: Options to attend educational institutions online include Zoom, Microsoft Teams and Blackboard Collaborate.

motions.m: THP a world where relationships were considered primarily transactional rather than being intrinsically fulfilling
motions.i: Transactional relationships are a type of romantic relationship where the primary purpose to both parties is to extract some kind of utilitarian value (e.g. convenience of shared housing, financial viability, etc.) with explicit obligations and conditions negotiated by the parties. Non-transactional relationships are romantic relationships where both parties believe that the relationship primarily serves to provide some sort of intrinsic nonutilitarian value.
