motions.m: THBT the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) should militarily intervene in Niger
motions.i: On July 26, 2023, the democratically elected government of Niger was toppled by a military coup headed by General Tchiani. In response, the Economic Community of West African States threatened to militarily intervene in the country if the coup did not reinstate the democratic government by August 6th. The deadline has since passed, yet the coup shows no signs of returning power to the democratic government.

motions.m: THBT the feminist movement should significantly deprioritise carceral feminism
THW decriminalize the act of joining or attempting to join terrorist organizations
THW impose a mandatory age of retirement for all public servants and elected politicians
THW make loans to developing countries conditional on significant environmental reforms
motions.i: For the purposes of this debate, loan conditions refer to specific policies or reforms a government must undertake in order to borrow money

motions.m: THS an indefinite halt to research into artificial general intelligence
motions.i: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) is a type of hypothetical artificial intelligence that could theoretically perform any task at a human or super-human level

motions.m: THW implement a wealth cap
motions.i: A wealth cap is a maximum legal limit to the amount of total wealth an individual can hold in any form. All additional wealth earned beyond the cap would automatically be taxed and withheld.

motions.m: THBT educational institutions should grant financial aid (subsidies, scholarships, stipends) solely to the poor
motions.i: financial aid is any form of educational funding that is given to students (e.g. subsidies, scholarships, stipends)

motions.m: THS the use of SEZs in middle income countries.
motions.i: A special economic zone (SEZ) is an area in a country that is designed to generate economic growth. SEZs have different regulations to the rest of the country. Regulations in an SEZ are designed to be favourable to businesses, to encourage businesses to invest. For example, an SEZ might subject businesses to fewer regulations, or offer tax incentives if they invest there.

motions.m: This house opposes school students protesting by refusing to attend school.
motions.r: U18 Semifinals, U16 Finals, U15 Quarterfinals, U14 Finals, U13 Semifinals
motions.i: Protest movements like the School Strike for Climate movement encourage primary and secondary school students not to attend school to protest issues in society.

motions.m: Assuming aliens exist, THBT we should try to contact them.
motions.r: U18 Quarterfinals, U16 Semifinals, U15 Pre-Quarterfinals, U14 Semifinals, U13 Quarterfinals, U12 Finals

motions.m: THW ban for-profit archaeology
motions.i: Archaeology is the study of human history through the excavation of historical sites and the analysis of artifacts. For-profit archaeologists work for companies (e.g. archaeology consulting firms) rather than for the government.

motions.m: THR the growing trend of promoting Veganism.
motions.i: Promoting veganism involves encouraging and advocating for a lifestyle that excludes the use of animal products for food, clothing, and other purposes. Vegans choose not to eat or use anything that comes from animals. This means not eating meat, dairy products, or eggs.

motions.m: This house believes that international communities should pay developing communities not to extract natural resources (e.g. Brazil and the Amazon)
motions.i: International communities includes governments of developed nations (e.g. USA, Russia, etc) or international organisations (e.g. the World Bank).

motions.m: THBT the individual should accept the offer.
motions.i: You are an athlete who has huge potential in a sport. Unfortunately, your country cannot provide the training and facilities you need to achieve your full potential. Another country has offered you the training and facilities that could potentially make you an Olympian champion. However, you must renounce your current citizenship and become a citizen of the new country.

motions.m: THS the incorporation of artificial intelligence in academia (examples may include but not limited to, allowing students to use large language models in assignments)
motions.i: Incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) in academia means using AI to assist, enhance, or automate various tasks and processes in the field of education.

motions.m: This House Believes That all countries found guilty of crimes against humanity should not be allowed to compete in the Olympics.
motions.i: Generally, all countries are permitted to attend and compete in the Olympics. The most recent Olympics in France saw Russia banned from competition due to the war in Ukraine. Many states also criticized the participation of Israel, who competed despite the war in Gaza.

motions.m: In discussions about climate change, This House Prefers the emphasis on climate doomerism as opposed to climate optimism in the messaging of the scientific community.
motions.i: Climate doomerism is the narrative which states that Earth, in reference to the impacts of climate change, is already doomed and individuals either need to take extreme action to protect themselves or accept that there's nothing they can do about it. On the other hand, climate optimism is the belief that the Earth can still be saved and that climate change can still be solved through the united efforts of humanity.

motions.m: This House Believes That it is in the best interest of the ASEAN to abandon the ASEAN Way.
motions.i: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations, or ASEAN, is an economic and political union of 10 states in Southeast Asia. However, despite being a political union, it has its unique diplomatic engagement with its member states, known as the ASEAN Way. The ASEAN Way follows the principle of non- interference among its member states, which means that member countries cannot interfere with the internal issues of the other member states. Some manifestations of the ASEAN Way include member states not interfering with the current dispute in the West Philippine Sea and not interfering during the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.

motions.m: This House Regrets start-up culture
motions.i: In business, a "start-up" refers to a business which begins from nothing and attempts to grow into something more substantial. During the start-up period, many people working for the business often work very hard for little pay. Many famous companies like Microsoft were once start-ups.

motions.m: This House Believes That social movements should prioritize local activism as opposed to national activism
motions.i: Social movements refer to when groups of people try and work together in order to achieve social change. Examples of social movements could be LGBT rights, feminism, or racial justice.

motions.m: This House Opposes tiger parenting
motions.i: Tiger parenting is a type of strict parenting where parents are highly invested in ensuring their child's success. Tiger parents often push their children to excel highly in academics and/or in extracurricular activities such as music or sports.

motions.m: This House, as an author of a successful book series, would encourage the creation of fan fiction
motions.i: Fan-fiction refers to fiction which is written by fans, based on a property that they did not create themselves. Some properties like Harry Potter, Frozen and Mario would be franchises which are frequently used as starting points for fan-fiction. Fan-fiction is generally written without the permission of copyright holders, and attempts to financially profit from it are usually legally banned.

motions.m: This House Would ban all forms of private education (tutoring, private schools)
motions.i: Private education is any education which occurs outside of the public system, where education is funded collectively by taxpayer money and equally accessible to all.

motions.m: This house, as a couple raising a child, would adopt a Western parenting model rather than a "tiger parenting" model.
motions.i: Tiger parenting refers to a style of parenting characterised by strict discipline, high academic and extracurricular expectations, and prioritising achievement and obedience. A Western parenting model refers to a style that emphasises individual choice, emotional support, and encouraging children to explore interests at their own pace. For the purposes of this debate, assume the couple is raising their child in a multicultural urban environment (e.g., Sydney, London, New York, Singapore), where both Western and Chinese communities are present and accessible.

motions.m: This house would require citizens to pass a Civic Competence Test before gaining the right to vote
motions.i: A Civic Competence Test is a government-administered assessment combining elements of civic knowledge (e.g., understanding political institutions, rights, and obligations) and critical thinking (e.g., identifying misinformation, distinguishing facts from opinions).

motions.m: This house prefers media and art that depict trauma primarily through collective or societal experiences rather than through individual narratives.
motions.i: Examples of media focussed on individual trauma narratives include: A Little Life (by Hanya Yanagihara), The Perks of Being a Wallflower (by Stephen Chbosky), Euphoria, BoJack Horseman, 13 Reasons Why, Billie Eilish's "Bury a Friend," and Phoebe Bridgers' "Motion Sickness." Examples of media focussed on collective or societal trauma narratives include: The Kite Runner (by Khaled Hosseini), Chernobyl, Schindler's List, Kendrick Lamar's "Alright" and Childish Gambino's "This is America." This debate is not limited to the exploration of these examples.

motions.m: This house prefers EU legislation of AI to legislation imposed by each individual country
motions.i: The EU Artificial Intelligence Act is legislation that regulates AI Systems on a 'risk-based approach'. Under this Act, the higher the risk to society, the stricter the rules (for example, increased document keeping, greater obligations on users and distributors, increased human intervention and oversight or, in extreme cases, banning the use of the system). This legislation does not apply to military and defence systems.

motions.m: This house believes that prioritising emotional detachment in professions like healthcare, law and justice leads to greater long-term economic and social costs than allowing emotional engagement.
motions.i: Emotional detachment in professional contexts (such as healthcare, law, and justice) refers to an expectation that workers maintain neutrality and distance from the emotional aspects of their work. For example, a doctor may avoid forming personal attachments with patients, or a judge may avoid allowing empathy to influence sentencing.

motions.m: This house believes it is immoral for billionaires to engage in large-scale philanthropy instead of paying higher taxes.
motions.i: Large-scale philanthropy by billionaires refers to major charitable initiatives funded by wealthy individuals, often involving billions of dollars. Examples include the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation – funding global health, education, and development projects, and the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative to fund education, science, and policy initiatives.

motions.m: This house supports the complete replacement of human teachers with artificial intelligence in schools.
This house would remove all restrictions on how welfare recipients can spend their payments.
This house supports Inclusionary Development
motions.i: Inclusionary development describes practices where new residential developments are required to include residential units that are affordable to low and moderate income residents of an area.

motions.m: This house would allow parents to vote on behalf of children who are under the voting age.
This house believes that sports governing bodies should ban technical doping.
motions.i: Technical doping refers to the use of sports equipment that confers a significant competitive advantage (e.g., running shoes, swimming suits) over alternative equipment of the same category.

motions.m: This house regrets BookTok
motions.i: "BookTok" refers to creators on the short form media platform TikTok making content about books. The videos share reviews, recommendations, and discussions of books. The videos are typically more entertaining than other forms of book reviews.

motions.m: This house would allow the use of facial recognition technology in public spaces to combat crime.
motions.i: Facial recognition technology helps computers recognize and identify people's faces by looking at their unique features. It is commonly used for security, authentication, and surveillance purposes.

motions.m: This house would introduce a school voucher system
motions.i: A school voucher system allows parents to use public funding allocated for their child's education to pay for tuition at a private or charter school instead of a public school.

motions.m: This house would ban corporate donations on political campaigns
motions.i: In many countries, corporations are allowed to donate money to political parties or election campaigns, either directly or indirectly.

motions.m: This house supports the rise of students taking gap years before and during their post-secondary education
motions.i: A gap year is a planned break, typically taken between stages of formal education, during which individuals pursue activities such as travel, work, volunteering, or skill development.

motions.m: This house supports radical honesty* in relationships (e.g., familial, romantic, platonic)
motions.i: Radical honesty refers to sharing everything, not holding back harsh truths, opening up about all feelings.

motions.m: This house supports the rise of political outsiders
motions.i: A political outsider is a person who has not previously held a significant position within politics. They may have backgrounds in business, activism, entertainment, or other fields outside of politics.

motions.m: This house believes that superteams have done more harm than good
motions.i: A superteam is a sports team made by bringing together many star players, often from different teams, so that the team has a very high chance of winning.

motions.m: This house believes that paintings damaged by activists should be displayed with the damage rather than restored
motions.i: In recent years, activists have targeted famous artworks in museums to draw attention to political and environmental issues. Actions have included throwing substances such as soup, paint or glue at paintings and frames.

motions.m: This house opposes the narrative that suffering is required for success
motions.i: In many cultures, success is often associated with hardship, sacrifice, long working hours, and emotional or physical suffering. This narrative appears in areas such as education, careers, sports, and the arts.

motions.m: This house would create a market for organs
motions.i: In most countries, the sale of human organs is illegal, and organs are donated voluntarily. A market for organs would allow individuals to receive financial compensation for donating organs, such as kidneys.

motions.m: THR society's emphasis on success.
motions.i: Society's emphasis on success means that success is very important in life, and that everyone should aspire to be successful. Examples of society's emphasis on success include the narrative that people should aspire to prestigious and well-paid jobs, or the celebration of 'successful' people and celebrities.

motions.m: THW not allow terminally ill patients to choose alternative medicine in hospitals.
motions.i: "Terminally ill patients are patients who are sick and have very low rates of surviving their illness or disease. Examples of such include, late stage cancer patients. Alternative medicine is any practice that aims to achieve the healing effects of medicine, but is untested scienctifically or is proven ineffective. Traditional practices, such as traditional Chinese medicine, become ""alternative"" when used without the guidance of a medical profession"

motions.m: THW not punish emergency service workers (e.g. police, medical staff, firefighters) who refuse to work.
motions.i: Like other employees, emergency service workers may choose to all skip work when they feel like they have poor working conditions. Such examples include low pay, unfair or dangerous working conditions. Currently, the government does not allow emergency service workers to skip work and they will be penalised by losing their jobs and having their work passes revoked.

motions.m: THBT white lies do more harm than good
motions.i: A white lie is a lie about a small or unimportant matter that someone tells to avoid hurting another person. For example, you might lie about liking your friend's new haircut to please and not irritate the friend, while secretly disliking the haircut.

motions.m: THW make participation in a team sport compulsory throughout school (i.e. from primary school to secondary school).
motions.i: A team sport includes any sport where individuals are organized into opposing teams which compete to win. Team members act together towards a shared objective. This can be done in a number of ways such as outscoring the opposing team. Examples are basketball, volleyball, rugby, water polo, handball, cricket, baseball, and the various forms of football, and hockey.

motions.m: THW encourage children to read books about fantasy and magic
motions.i: Books about fantasy and magic include plots that could not happen in real life. Often, the plot involves magic or witchcraft and takes place on another planet or in another undiscovered world. Many times, the plot also involves mythical creatures or talking animals (that might wear clothes and live in houses), and witches. One of the most famous examples of fantasy fiction is the Harry Potter series.

motions.m: THBT scientists should not bring dinosaurs back to life
motions.i: This debate assumes that scientists have the technology to recreate and hatch eggs of previously-extinct dinosaur species.

motions.m: THBT teachers should be rewarded when their students do well in their exams.
motions.i: Reward means giving someone the recognition for their services, efforts, or achievements.

motions.m: THW make Sunday a family day where parents and children are not allowed to work, and must spend the entire day together.
motions.i: Family days are dedicated to spending time with family, rather than working or studying.

motions.m: THO the rise of celebrities openly supporting politicians
motions.i: There has been a rise of celebrities and socially important people coming out to publicly support politicians in upcoming elections, to push for various policies, etc

motions.m: This House Opposes Reality TV
motions.i: Reality TV is a genre of television programmes in which ordinary people are continuously filmed throughout their daily life. These programs are designed to be entertaining rather than informative.

motions.m: This House believes that the voting age should be lowered to 16
motions.i: Voting is the process by which citizens of a country, state, or municipality express their opinions or preferences on a particular issue or candidate. It is an important way for people to have a say in the decisions that affect their lives and communities. In most countries, the voting age is the minimum age at which a person is legally allowed to vote in an election. The voting age is typically set by law and can vary from country to country. In many countries, the voting age is 18.

motions.m: THBT neurodiverse students should not be separated from mainstream students in the classroom
THW pay individuals who would migrate from highly populated cities to rural areas.
THW opt out of social media
motions.i: Social Media refers to websites and applications that enable users to create and share content or to participate in social networking. Examples of social media are TikTok, Facebook, Reddit, and more.

motions.m: THW prosecute business leaders for environmental crimes committed by their companies.
motions.i: To prosecute means holding someone criminally liable and carrying out trial in the court of law. Examples of environmental crimes may include but not limited to wildlife crimes, illegal mining, pollution crimes, illegal fishing, and illegal logging. All of them breach environmental legislation and may cause significant harm not only to the environment but to human health as well.

motions.m: THW prevent guilty nations of human rights violations from hosting major sporting events
motions.i: Countries often bid to host major sporting events to enhance their global image, stimulate their economies, and showcase their infrastructural capabilities. Examples of major sporting events include the World Cup and the Olympic Games. In the past, concerns have been raised about human rights abuses and violations, in some host countries.

motions.m: TH regrets the glorification of child prodigies
motions.i: A child prodigy is a young child who has very great ability in something. Example(s) may include but not limited to - A child prodigy, they made their first professional tour as a pianist at the age of six.

motions.m: TH would impose a limit on the number of ECAs that can be pursued by a child.
motions.i: ECAs refer to an extracurricular activity, something students do which is not done during regular class time.

motions.m: THBT an artist's struggle with mental illness (eg. depression, mood disorders, anxiety, bipolarism, etc.) should not be emphasized when celebrating their work
motions.i: Artists throughout history have created incredible works across various mediums while contending with mental health challenges. The relationship between mental illness and artistic expression is complex, and discussions often arise regarding the extent to which an artist's personal struggles should influence the appreciation of their creative achievement.

motions.m: Recognising the hazardous impacts of industrial revolution on climate change, THP that the world remained undeveloped
motions.r: U13 Main Semifinals, U12 Main Semifinals, U12 Rookie Semifinals, U11 Semifinals

motions.m: THW offer criminals sentenced to life imprisonment without parole the option of the death penalty
motions.r: U13 Main Quarterfinals, U13 Rookie Semifinals, U12 Main Quarterfinals, U12 Rookie Quarterfinals, U11 Quarterfinals
motions.i: Parole is the temporary or permanent release of a prisoner before the end of a prison sentence, on the promise of good behaviour.

motions.m: THBT parents should have a vote on behalf of their children
THW prevent athletes under the age of 18 from competing at the Olympic Games
motions.i: There are currently no age limits in the Olympic Games. At the most recent Tokyo Olympics, the youngest competitors were 12 years old.

motions.m: THW allow incarcerated single parents to raise their children in prisons.
motions.i: Raising kids in prison involves mothers or fathers caring for their children while serving a sentence, often within designated family units or programs. The goal is to maintain family bonds, providing a structured and supportive environment for the children despite the challenges of being in a correctional facility.

motions.m: THW allow individual workers to waive their labour rights in exchange for additional pay.
motions.i: To waive labour rights means to give up rights such as days off, hours limits, parental leave.

motions.m: This House regrets the emphasis on miracles in the spread of religion
motions.i: A miracle is something amazing and unusual that happens, which people often believe is caused by God or a higher power because it doesn't seem possible by normal means.

motions.m: THW ban people with criminal records from running for political office
THBT education system should stream children according to their abilities from a young age
motions.i: Streaming children according to their abilities means grouping them into different classes or levels based on how well they perform in certain subjects, starting from a young age. For example, students who are strong in math might be placed in a more advanced math class, while those who need extra help could be in a different group where they can learn at a pace that suits them better.

motions.m: This House Would make parents legally responsible for the crimes of their children
motions.i: Being legally responsible means that a person is required by law to take care of something or to make sure something is done correctly. If they don't do what they're supposed to, they might face consequences like paying a fine or going to court.

motions.m: This house believes that AI Research and Development should only be allowed to be done by government owned or government funded entities.
motions.i: AI refers to Artificial Intelligence and in the status quo, there is no restriction regarding who can carry out research and development pertaining to AI.

motions.m: This house would make Organ Donation mandatory for every citizen around the world.
motions.i: In the status quo, individuals have the option to either sign up to be an organ donor where, once they die, their organs are made available to be used by ones who might be able to use them or an individual can opt out as well from donating their organ.

motions.m: This house regrets the expectation placed on able children to take care of their elderly parents.
motions.i: It is a general and common expectation that when parents become older, they are looked after and taken care of by their children, given the children are able.

motions.m: This house believes that the salary of teachers should change every year based on the performance of their students in a standardized test.
motions.i: A standardized test is a large-scale assessment where all test takers answer the same questions in the same way and are scored consistently. This allows for the comparison of student performance across groups or individuals.

motions.m: This house regrets the rise of Kidfluencers.
motions.i: Child influencers, or "kidfluencers", are children under 18 who create content on social media platforms and build large online followings. They typically share videos about their daily lives, toy reviews, challenges, and personal activities, often with their parents managing their accounts. Some examples may include but not limited to Ryan Kaji whose YouTube channel is known as Ryan's World and Txunamy Ortiz, a 12-year-old social media star with 5.4 million Instagram followers. She's known for fashion content, lip-sync videos, and comedy skits.

motions.m: This house supports the use of White Lies.
motions.i: A white lie is generally referred to as a trivial or small lie that is said primarily with the intent of not hurting the sentiments and/or feelings of someone.

motions.m: This house believes that children should be paid for doing household chores.
motions.i: Household chores are tasks that are performed to keep a home clean and organized. Examples may include but not limited to taking out the trash, organizing the bad, and sweeping the kitchen floor.

motions.m: This house would reduce the sentences of prisoners serving life imprisonment who unconditionally volunteer for medical trials.
motions.i: A prisoner serving life imprisonment or life sentence is someone who has been convicted of a crime and is sentenced to spend the rest of their life in prison. A medical trial is a research study that evaluates the safety and effectiveness of a new medical treatment or intervention on human health.

motions.m: This house would set a limit on the number of extracurricular activities that a child can get involved with.
motions.i: Extracurricular activities refer to the activities that are outside of the academic studies, can be under school or external entity, the examples may include but not limited to debate, speech, singing, dancing, playing an instrument, KUMON, swimming.

motions.m: This house believes that we should ban TV programs that glorify crime.
motions.i: TV programs that glorify crime, like those portraying characters similar to the Joker often frame criminals as charismatic antiheroes, emphasizing style, wit, or tragedy over the harm they cause. Other examples include Conversations with a Killer: The Ted Bundy Tapes and Suicide Squad.

motions.m: This house believes that it is better to have one best friend than a large group of friends
motions.i: A best friend is a close friend whom someone trusts and spends the most time with. Having a large group of friends means having many friends, but usually fewer close connections with each person.

motions.m: This house prefers happy stories to realistic stories
motions.i: Happy stories usually focus on positive events, fun adventures, or problems that are solved with a cheerful outcome. Realistic stories aim to reflect everyday life, including both successes and struggles, and may not always end positively. Both types of stories are popular in books, films, and other media.

motions.m: This house believes governments should ban plastic
motions.i: Plastic is one of the most common materials in the world and is used in bags, bottles, packaging, medical supplies, and more. It is cheap and light, but also takes a long time to break down. Because of this, it can cause pollution and other harms.

motions.m: This house believes that schools should allow students to protest during schooling hours.
motions.i: School student protests are organized or individual demonstrations where young people voice their views on issues like climate change, education policy, or social justice. One example of this is Greta Thunberg who, while she was a school student, skipped school to demand urgent climate action.

motions.m: This house believes parents should talk openly about money problems to their children
motions.i: Money problems are when a family struggles to pay for important things like food, housing, or healthcare. Some people think children should know about the family's money problems so they can understand and help. Others think it may worry children unnecessarily.

motions.m: This house believes that sports should not separate competitors by gender
motions.i: Many sports, like football, basketball, and gymnastics, have separate competitions for boys and girls, so each gender only competes against the same gender. Some sports, like chess, allow everyone to compete together, no matter their gender.

motions.m: This house believes that AI has done more harm than good
motions.i: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a type of technology that allows computers to "think" like humans. While AI has created benefits such as faster information, helpful tools like translation apps, and better medical technology, it has also replaced workers, spread false information online, and even be used in dangerous weapons.

motions.m: This house supports the ban of mobile phones in schools
motions.i: Mobile phones are devices students use to call, message, and go online, but they can also distract them in class.

motions.m: This house would require movies and books for children to include morally flawed heroes
motions.i: A "morally flawed hero" is a main character who has clear weaknesses, makes mistakes, or sometimes acts wrongly, but is still presented as the hero of the story.

motions.m: This house believes housewives should be paid an average salary for the labour they do at home
motions.i: A housewife is someone who works at home by cooking, cleaning, and taking care of children or family members, but does not get paid.

motions.m: This house would ban competitions of combat sports
motions.i: Combat sports are sports like boxing or mixed martial arts where people fight each other following rules.

motions.m: This house believes that it is better to choose a high paying job than a job you love
motions.i: A high-paying job is one that earns a lot of money, while a job you love is work that you enjoy and feel happy doing.

motions.m: This house believes that peace is more important than justice.
motions.i: Peace refers to the absence of violence or conflict, especially after wars or major disputes. Justice refers to holding people accountable, punishment for wrongdoing, or restoring fairness.

motions.m: This house would impose sanctions on politicians who don't fulfill campaign promises
motions.i: Campaign promises are commitments politicians make to voters about what they plan to do once elected.

motions.m: This house believes that tourism brings more harm than good.
motions.i: Tourism is when people travel to other places for holidays, sightseeing, or leisure. Many places depend on tourism for jobs and income, but they can also suffer from problems like pollution, overcrowding, and higher prices for locals.

motions.m: This house would live in a world where everyone always tells the truth.
motions.i: Telling the truth means always saying what is real and honest, even if it might hurt someone's feelings or put you at a disadvantage.

motions.m: THS states financially rewarding families that exhibit behaviours that the state determines to be pro-social behaviours
motions.i: Pro-social behavior refers to any actions or behaviors that are intended to benefit or help others, either individuals or society as a whole. Pro-social behaviours for families in this motion is determined by the state, but it typically includes sending one's children to school, vaccinating their children, volunteering for their communities, donating and recycling

motions.m: THW allocate the best-performing public school teachers to underperforming schools.
motions.i: Underperforming schools are educational institutions that consistently fall short in meeting expected standards of academic achievement and overall educational quality. These schools typically exhibit lower student performance levels and lower graduation rates.

motions.m: THW implement self-directed learning in public education
motions.i: Self-directed learning describes a process in which individuals take the initiative, with or without the help of others, in diagnosing their learning needs.

motions.m: This house believes that we should allow businesses to fire employees on the basis of personal (legal) online behavior (e.g. personal Facebook posts).
motions.i: Online behavior refers to the actions, conduct, and interactions that individuals engage in while using the internet or participating in online platforms and ocmmunities. It encompasses how individuals communicate, express themselves, and engage with tohers in the digital realms. Online behavior can include activities such as positng and sharing content, commenting on posts, sending messages, participating in online discussions, and engaging in online gaming or virtual environments.

motions.m: THBT children should be placed in classes based on academic ability instead of age
motions.i: In most countries, children in primary and secondary school are sorted into classes based on their age. 'Academic ability' refers to the knowledge of the students, theira cademic results and level of engagement with the material being taught.

motions.m: THW choose a career based off of money, instead of passion
motions.i: A career refers to an individual's professional journey or the sequence of jobs, roles, and experiences they undertake throughout their working life. A career typically involves a long-term commitment to developing skills, acquiring knowledge, and achieving personal and professional growth.

motions.m: THW ban all anonymopus accounts on scoial media sites
motions.i: An anonymous accoutn on social media refers to a user profile that conceals the true identity of the person behind it. It allows individuals to participate in online platforms without revealing their real name, personal information, or recognizable details. Instead, they may use pseudonyms or avatars to maintain their anonymity.

motions.m: THS heritable human genome editing
motions.i: Heritable human genome editing (HGE) allows scinetists to make changes in human DNA, the material that carries information about how humans will look and functionm that can be passed to future generations.

motions.m: THBT all children should raise a pet
motions.i: A pet is an animal that is domesticated or kept for companionship, pleasure, or amusement. Pets are typically kept in households and treated as members of the family. They are often chosen based on their ability to provide emotional support, companionship, and entertainment to their owners. Common types of pets include dogs, cats, birds, fish, rabbits, hamsters, guinea pigs, and reptiles, among others. Pets require care and attention, including feeding, grooming, exercise, and medical attention, to ensure their well-being and happiness.

motions.m: THBT children's books featuring characters from racial minorities should prioritize stories that do not focus on racial injustice
motions.i: Racial injustice refers to the unfair or discriminatory treatment of individuals or groups based on their race or ethnicity

motions.m: That war journalists should be forced to reveal their sources in international criminal tribunals (including the International Criminal Court) when trying cases of war crimes, crimes against humanity or genocide
That the environment movement should heavily prioritise nuclear power in their energy advocacy
That we oppose the use of facial recognition technology by supermarkets.
motions.i: Facial recognition technology is being trialled by supermarkets to try reduce crime by attempting to match, in real time, the faces of people who enter a store against that store's record of offenders and accomplices.

motions.m: That Indonesia should legalise the sale of votes to political parties
That students from financially disadvantaged backgrounds should be given a lower bar to qualify for academic special considerations
motions.i: Special considerations include, but are not limited to, having a longer time to submit assignments and applications, and having an easier pathway to passing university courses and degrees

motions.m: This House would deduct points from sporting teams when their players are found guilty of racism or homophobia.
This House would allow pharmaceutical companies to advertise their products to consumers
motions.i: In the USA and New Zealand, pharmaceutical companies are allow to advertise medicines.

motions.m: This House regrets the existence of Valentine's day.
motions.i: Valentine's Day is celebrated on February 14, when couples express their affection with greetings and gifts.

motions.m: This House would limit the copyright period for media to five years.
motions.i: Creators of media (like films, books, TV) can hold the copyright. This means that no one else is allowed to make media with those ideas. In some countries, the copyright lasts over 100 years.

motions.m: This House believes that the government should charge higher taxes on goods and services with a big carbon footprint.
motions.i: A carbon footprint is how much carbon something uses. A bigger footprint uses more carbon and is worse for the environment.

motions.m: This house, as a voter in a democratic country, would prioritise individual candidate qualities over party affiliation when voting in elections
motions.i: Party affiliation refers to the political party or group a candidate belongs to. On the other hand, individual candidate qualities refer to traits that the candidate possesses, including but not limited to experience, positions on key issues, and personal beliefs.

motions.m: This house regrets the dominant belief that humility is a virtue
motions.i: Humility is defined as the quality of being modest and having a low view of one's importance.

motions.m: This house would ban the use of artificial intelligence systems in judicial decision-making processes
motions.i: Currently, AI systems are being introduced experimentally in judicial systems in various jurisdictions, e.g. predictive algorithms to suggest sentencing or bail outcomes.

motions.m: This house believes that schools in developing countries should priotise a specialised education system over a liberal education system.
motions.i: A specialised education system involves students studying fewer subjects selected according to their interests from an early stage in their schooling, focussing on depth in those specific areas. A liberal education system involves students studying a diverse array of subjects designed to develop general knowledge, critical thinking, and interdisciplinary understanding.

motions.m: This house believes that the feminist movement should prioritise the advocacy of structural reform, rather than challenging social norms
motions.i: Structural reform refers to policy-level institutional changes (e.g. creating laws) aimed at improving people's lives. Examples include pushing for subsidized childcare and anti-discrimination laws. On the other hand, challenging social norms refers to efforts to shift societal attitudes around gender. Examples include questioning beauty standards, toxic masculinity and gender roles.

motions.m: Thise house believes that the feminist movement should prioritise the advocacy of structural reform, rather than challenging social norms
motions.i: Structural reform refers to policy-level institutional changes (e.g. creating laws) aimed at improving people's lives. Examples include pushing for subsidized childcare and anti-discrimination laws. On the other hand, challenging social norms refers to efforts to shift societal attitudes around gender. Examples include questioning beauty standards, toxic masculinity and gender roles.

motions.m: This house supports religions characterizing human nature as innately evil (e.g., the original sin in Christianity)
motions.i: Human nature refers to the characteristics, feelings, and behaviors that are considered typical or innate to humans. It includes qualities that are believed to be part of being human across cultures and time.

motions.m: This house believes that governments should heavily invest in geo-engineering to combat global warming
motions.i: Geo-engineering refers to large-scale technologies used to purposely change Earth's climate. Examples include spraying tiny reflective particles high in the atmosphere to bounce sunlight back into space, altering ocean surfaces to absorb more carbon dioxide, and covering large areas of land with reflective materials to reflect more sunlight instead of letting it warm the Earth.

motions.m: This house would ban the use of cars.
motions.i: In recent years, countries have begun a move towards increasing public transport services due to the environmental concerns around cars and the high costs of building roads and highways to accommodate for cars. Public transport includes buses, trains, and any form of transportation meant to transport large groups of people. In response, many car companies have begun producing electric vehicles, which are more environmentally friendly.

motions.m: This house believes that parents should not promote university education as the primary path to success in life
motions.i: University education is often viewed as a standard route to career stability, higher income, and social status. However, alternative paths such as vocational training, entrepreneurship, and creative industries also lead to successful and fulfilling lives.

motions.m: This house regrets the existence of parenting influencers
motions.i: Parenting influencers are individuals who upload videos or stream on platforms like YouTube or Twitch. With the primary subject of their videos being about how they raise their kids, videos about their life as a parent, and content advising other parents on how they should be raising their children.

motions.m: This house would ban fast fashion
motions.i: Fast fashion refers to quickly made, inexpensive clothing that follows the latest trends and is produced in large quantities. Brands like Zara, H&M, and Shein are known for fast fashion, offering new styles every few weeks at low prices.

motions.m: This house would ban all fast food restaurants (e.g., McDonald's, KFC, Domino's, Taco Bell, etc.)
motions.i: Fast food restaurants run on a model of making cheap and unhealthy food, that is accessible to many people but is also bad to consume in the long term. These restaurants have been linked with increasing obesity, heart problems, and nutritional deficiencies. But also, they provide a lot of part time work for individuals and have become a significant part of many local economies.

motions.m: This house believes that team sports are better than individual sports
motions.i: Team sports like volleyball, basketball, and soccer, prioritise the success of the team as a whole. Where teamwork is what allows for players to win and any joy from success is shared across the team. Individual sports like badminton, tennis, and boxing, prioritise the success of the individual. Every win is due to the player's own efforts and is proof that they are the best in their respective sport.

motions.m: This house believes that the feminist movement should oppose popular fairy tales which portray traditional gender roles.
motions.i: Many classic fairy tales, such as Cinderella, Sleeping Beauty, and Snow White, portray female protagonists as passive, gentle, and dependent on external rescue—often by a male hero. These characters are frequently valued for their beauty, obedience, and kindness rather than their agency or decision-making. In contrast, male characters like princes are typically cast as brave, active saviors who resolve the central conflict and determine the story's outcome, reinforcing traditional gender expectations of women as caregivers and men as protectors.

motions.m: This house believes that aid agencies should not be allowed to undertake missionary activities.
motions.i: Aid agencies are organizations that provide humanitarian assistance, such as food, healthcare, and disaster relief, often in response to crises or long- term development needs. Missionary activities are efforts, usually led by religious groups, to spread their faith while often providing social services like education, medical care, and community support.

motions.m: This house believes that the development of AI will do more harm than good to education.
motions.i: Artificial intelligence (AI), has risen in popularity in recent years with many teachers using it to write exams, create worksheets and help them mark homework. Students have also begun to start using AI to improve their own understanding of subjects but also to cheat on exams or to have AI do their homework for them. Because of AI's improving ability to write like a human, it is becoming increasingly difficult to identify AI works. Some people are concerned that this increase in AI usage may limit the amount that students are actually learning, whilst others believe that AI will make teaching easier and allow students to ask more questions and get help with their studies even when their teachers aren't around.

motions.m: This house believes that environmental movements should heavily prioritise geopoliticised narratives to advocate for greener technologies and policies
motions.i: "Geopoliticisation", in the context of energy and climate issues, refers to narratives that justify policy decisions through a geopolitical lens (e.g., advocating for decreasing the reliance on fossil fuels in the EU to reduce dependence on Russia, promoting domestically manufacturing electric vehicles in the US to compete with other producers etc.)

motions.m: This house would raise their child to prefer being single
motions.i: Some people seek out romantic relationships and consider them an important life goal and a source of fulfillment, while others believe that staying single is the preferable or "default" lifestyle. Such preferences, among other factors, are modelled by parents through advice, guidance, encouragement and role modelling.

motions.m: This house believes that news organisations should prioritise "slow journalism" over real-time reporting
motions.i: Slow journalism is a news model that prioritises depth, verification, and long-form investigation over rapid, real-time updates. It produces fewer, more thoroughly researched stories, avoiding breaking-news style reporting.

motions.m: This house believes that corporations should adopt the practice of "radical transparency"
motions.i: Certain companies operate on the practice of Radical Transparency. This means that internally every conversation, meeting, and report is recorded and available to all employees upon their request. Additionally, employees are required to rank each interaction and collaboration with one another, effectively reviewing each other.

motions.m: This house opposes the rise of health-based influencing
motions.i: In recent years, influencers have increasingly begun producing physical and mental health-based content. Examples include dietary recommendations, the marketing of certain products, general lifestyle advice (e.g., "gymfluencers" promoting exercise routines), and sharing techniques which are supposed to aid in reducing stress and improving mental health

motions.m: This house believes that the rise of Artificial Intelligence will increase, rather than reduce, socioeconomic inequality
motions.i: Artificial Intelligence refers to computer systems that perform tasks which would normarlly require human intelligence, and such systems include, but are not limited to, chatbots, image generators, and various advanced automation systems. The rise of Artificial Intelligence refers to the widespread adoption and increasing use of AI technologies across society, such to education, healthcare, business, and entertainment. Socioeconomic (in)equality is normally measured through differences in income, wealth and living standards.

motions.m: This house prefers a world where the dominant norm for classical novels is experimental adaptations over true-to-novel adaptations
motions.i: For the purpose of this debate, experimental adaptations are those which meaningfully deviate from core elements of the original story when a novel is adapted into a film, play, series, or other form of media. This includes changes to the message, plot, characters, and/or setting. Examples include Wuthering Heights (2026), Clueless (2026), The Bride! (2026), and Apocalypse Now (1979).

motions.m: This house supports the American Dream as a dominant narrative in society
motions.i: The American Dream is the belief that anyone, regardless of where they were born or what class they were born into, can attain success, since upward mobility is possible for everyone. The American Dream is achieved through sacrifice, risk-taking, and hard work, rather than by chance.

motions.m: This House would ban personalized advertising on the internet
motions.i: Personalized advertising is when companies track what you do online to choose exactly which ads you see. For example, if you spend the afternoon watching gaming videos or searching for shoes, websites will automatically start showing you ads for those exact items.

motions.m: This House would ban the use of ghostwriters in the entertainment industry (e.g., for music, celebrity books, and social media).
motions.i: A ghostwriter is a professional writer who is paid to write a book, song, speech, or social media post for someone else. Once the work is finished, the ghostwriter is paid a fee but receives no public credit. Instead, the piece is published under the name of the famous celebrity, politician, musician, or influencer, making it look as though they wrote it themselves.

motions.m: This House believes that the amount of money in professional sport should be reduced.
motions.i: Professional sport often includes high value transactions like player transfers and sponsorships. There is a lot of investment in infrastructure like stadiums or arenas and large tournaments are accompanied by high ticket prices, purchases of broadcasting rights and ad revenue. Professional athletes of the highest level can earn up to 500 000 USD a week, while large leagues like the NBA are worth around 5 billion USD.

motions.m: This House believes that movie studios should focus on creating entirely new original characters rather than changing the gender, race, or background of existing legacy characters in remakes.
motions.i: A legacy character is an iconic, deeply established fictional character who has been famous for decades, such as James Bond, Ariel the Little Mermaid, Spider-Man, or Batman. When movie studios want to update an old franchise or make a remake, they sometimes change the race, gender, or background of these legacy characters to make the cast more diverse (for example, casting a Black actress as Ariel). An original character is a brand-new persona created from scratch with their own unique backstory, lore, and identity.

motions.m: This House supports the rise of self-care culture.
motions.i: Self-care culture refers to the growing emphasis on prioritizing one's own physical, mental, and emotional well-being. This can include healthy habits such as balanced food choices, exercise, sleep, and hygiene, as well as practices like setting personal boundaries, taking breaks from work or school, and focusing on mental health. Critics argue that self-care culture can encourage excessive focus on oneself or turn well-being into a commercial industry, while supporters believe it helps people live healthier and happier lives.

motions.m: This House would abolish the minimum age requirement to vote, replacing it with a basic political literacy test.
motions.i: Currently, almost every country uses a minimum age requirement (usually 18) to decide who is allowed to vote. A political literacy test is an exam that measures a person's basic understanding of how their country's government works.

motions.m: This House believes that education should prioritize preparing students for employment over general education.
motions.i: Preparation for employment includes training students for more practical vocations and sometimes redirecting them to fields that are more in demand. General education prioritises covering a wide spectrum of different fields some of which may not be connected to the job the students end up working.

motions.m: This House would ban the commercial media based on real-world criminals.
motions.i: Commercial media based on real-world criminals refers to entertainment made for profit — such as Hollywood movies, television series, and video games that dramatize, re-enact, or tell the life stories of actual, living, or historical criminals.

motions.m: The Filial piety laws require adult children to financially support impoverished family members or relatives, as opposed to the state providing for their care.
motions.i: The Filial piety laws require adult children to financially support impoverished family members or relatives, as opposed to the state providing for their care.

motions.m: This House Believes That the environmental movement should prioritize advocating for radical individual lifestyle change over solutions by organizations and/or institutions.
motions.i: Some examples of the changes of lifestyle can be, including but not limited to, vegan diets, limited air travel, recycling, reusing, and composting, etc. Some examples of organizational and/or institutional responsibility can be, including but not limited to, carbon capture technologies, reducing pollution, waste, natural resource consumption, and emissions through its manufacturing process, as applicable, etc.

motions.m: This House opposes the narrative of identifying sodiers as Heroes.
motions.i: In the status quo, soldiers (usually ones who are part of the armed forces of a country) who fight and at times sacrifice their lives are identified as "heroes" by majority.

motions.m: TH regrets the trend of celebrities and artists openly endorsing politicians.
motions.i: In the status quo, it has become a common scenario for famous celebrities and artists such as Musicians come in and support candidates, including but not limited to, ones running to be Presidents and/or head of states or Member of Parliament, at times.

motions.m: TH regrets the commecialization of "self care" products.
motions.i: Self-care refers to activities that give people time to relax and prioritize their own wellbeing, examples may include but not limited to, spas, massages, meditation, etc. Commercialization refers to the process of actively managing or using something with the intent of gaining financially.

motions.m: THW mandate that all secondary and post-secondary examinations be open-book tests.
motions.i: Open-book tests refer to assessments or tests, where students are permitted to use their notes and textbooks. Typically, students are still not allowed to access the internet during open-book tests. Secondary and post-secondary would refer to grades 8 and above.

motions.m: TH would support to have Open borders.
motions.i: In the status quo, there are 195 globally recognized countries in the world; they have their own borders that separates and/or distinguishes them from other countries and/or states. Open borders refers to a country or countries that have no limits on immigration - in other words, people can move freely between countries as they please and would desire.

motions.m: This House Would ban Moonlighting.
motions.i: The concept of "Moonlighting" is to have a second job and/or work in addition to one's regular employment; there are multiple reasons that can influence a person towards opting for Moonlighting.

motions.m: This house believes that it is better to have loved and lost than to never have loved at all.
This house opposes the narrative that "music is a universal language".
This house prefers a world where individuals can implement Mute.
motions.i: "Mute" is a technology that can be implemented into one's brain where an individual can decide to mute another entity, ranging from a specific person or group of people. The effect of muting is that the muted entity is no longer seen, heard, or felt by the individual.

motions.m: This house would abolish anti-social behaviour orders
motions.i: Anti-social behaviour orders are civil penalties applied to people found to be engaging in anti-social behaviour (e.g. drunkenness, swearing, rioting, vandalism, racist language)

motions.m: This house, as South Korean government, would actiely facilitate the remilitarization of Japan.
motions.i: Japan has recently shifted its security approach from a pacifist stance based on the post-World War II Constitution to an increase in military spending, an expanded military role, and more intensive joint military exercises with the US. The recently elected Prime Minister of Japan, Shigeru Ishiba, is also well known for his advocacy for a more rapid remilitarization.

motions.m: This house believes that the feminist movement should prioritize supporting positive masculinity over abolishing gender roles
motions.i: Positive Masculinity mantains gender roles for men but attempts to match those roles with positive traits (e.g., empathy, caretaking, social responsibility and self-control)

motions.m: This house believes that Australasian countries should disincentivise students from pursuing education in other countries
This house would severely limit companies ability to replace workers with technology
motions.i: In recent years, companies have increasingly replaced workers with technology, primarily through the automation of tasks once performed by humans. Examples include, but are not limited to, replacing factory workers with robotic arms, utilizing artificial intelligence to replace human interaction, and implementing self-service machines.

motions.m: This house believes that governments should strongly prioritise stopping climate change, even if it comes at the cost of economic growth
motions.i: Climate change is the long-term warming of Earth caused by human activities, like burning fossil fuels and deforestation, which release greenhouse gases that trap heat. Addressing climate change can impact economic growth by requiring costly changes to energy systems, stricter regulations on factories, or reduced use of cheap but polluting resources.

motions.m: This house believes that schools should teach children to question parental authority
motions.i: Parental authority refers to parents' rights and responsibilities toward their children. Because of parental authority, parents make decisions that affect their children's well-being.

motions.m: This house supports the rise of academic piracy sites (E.g. Sci-Hub, Lib-Gen)
motions.i: Academic piracy refers to unauthorised access and distribution of scientific articles. Websites like Sci-Hub and Lib-Gen provide free access to books and journals that otherwise are restricted to users who pay a fee to access them.

motions.m: This house regrets the rise of child influencers
motions.i: Child influencer is who has built or is building a presence on social media platforms (TikTok, Instagram, Youtube) creating content to generate views and engagements, that is often sponsored. Examples are Like Natasya, Vlad and Niki, etc.

motions.m: This house would abolish juries in criminal trials
motions.i: A jury is a group of people brought together during a trial. By listening to the evidence, they decide whether the accused person in a trial is guilty or not guilty. If the jury finds the defendant guilty, the judge then decides the appropriate punishment.

motions.m: This house believes that all international sporting events (e.g. FIFA World Cup, the Olympic Games) should have a permanent host nation
motions.i: A host nation provides the facilities for that event to take place. Currently, the host of an international sporting event is decided per edition. For example, Tokyo hosted the 2020 Olympics while Paris hosted the 2024 Olympics because these cities were supported by their country in bidding to host the Games.

motions.m: This house prefers the depiction of platonic over romantic relationships in media
motions.i: A platonic relationship is a close bond between people that does not involve romantic elements. This contrasts with a romantic relationship, which typically includes emotional closeness and feelings of romance or love.

motions.m: This house would invest in long-term debate classes for their child
motions.i: You are the parent of a young child currently in middle school who has never displayed interest in debate before. You would like your child to become a good debater as you have become aware that it is very helpful for university applications and general success.

motions.m: This house regrets the rise of thrifting
motions.i: Thrifting refers to the practice of buying second-hand clothing and other items, often from charity shops, vintage stores, or online resale platforms. It has grown significantly in popularity in recent years, particularly among younger consumers

motions.m: This house would remove patents for eco-friendly technologies.
motions.i: Patents give inventors exclusive rights to produce or sell their inventions for a certain period of time, preventing others from copying or using the same technology without permission

motions.m: This house believes that history education should prioritize the experiences of everyday people over the actions of great leaders.
motions.i: Great leaders are famous people from the past, like kings, generals, or presidents, who had a big influence on their country or the world, for example Napoleon or Martin Luther King Jr.

motions.m: Space exploration means sending people, rockets, or machines into outer space to learn more about planets, stars, and the universe. It includes things like landing on the Moon, exploring Mars, or using telescopes and satellites to study space.
motions.i: This house supports the rise of space exploration

motions.m: This house would invest into the development of AI caretakers for children and old people
motions.i: AI caretakers refer to artificially intelligent systems or robots designed to help care for vulnerable groups, such as children or the elderly. These technologies may assist with tasks like supervision, companionship, health monitoring, education, or daily routines.

motions.m: This house prefers a world where individuals are aware of their "natural expiration date"
motions.i: A 'natural expiration date' refers to the date whereby an individual would die. This date is determined based on the physical and mental condition of the individual when they are 18.

motions.m: Many schools use grades (like A–F or 1–5) to show how well a student is doing. Written feedback means the teacher explains in words what the student did well and what they need to improve, instead of giving a number or letter.
motions.i: This house would ban grades and replace them with written feedback.

motions.m: This house believes that rich countries should do more to fight climate change than poor countries.
motions.i: Rich countries (also called developed countries) are nations with strong economies, advanced technology, and higher living standards (e.g., USA, Germany, Japan). Poor countries (developing countries) have weaker economies, less technology, and lower living standards (e.g., Bangladesh, Kenya, Nepal). Climate change means long-term changes in the Earth's climate caused mainly by greenhouse gases from human activity. Examples of its effects include rising sea levels, stronger storms, droughts, and floods.

motions.m: This house would ban beauty filters on social media.
motions.i: "Beauty filters" are special effects on apps like Instagram, TikTok, or Snapchat that change how someone looks. For example, by making their skin smoother, eyes bigger, or face slimmer. Banning beauty filters means people would not be allowed to use or post pictures and videos with these effects.

motions.m: This house believes that the final say in the medical treatment of minors should belong to medical professionals (doctors) instead of parents
motions.i: A minor means someone under 18 years old (a child or teenager who is not yet an adult). In many countries, parents normally make the final decision about their child's medical treatment (like surgery or medicine)

motions.m: This house believes that childhood years are the best years of life.
motions.i: Childhood usually means the years before adulthood, when people are still dependent on their parents or guardians (commonly before the age of 18).

motions.m: This house would make museums free for everyone.
motions.i: Museums are places where people can see and learn about history, art, science, and culture. For example, old artifacts, famous paintings, or dinosaur fossils. Right now, many museums charge an entry fee. Making museums free means anyone can visit them without paying any money.

motions.m: This house would rather live in a world where God sometimes does wrong things than in a world where God is always right.
motions.i: In this debate, "a world where God sometimes does wrong things" means a world where God can make mistakes or act unfairly, just like humans sometimes do. "A world where God is always right" means God never makes mistakes and everything He does is always good and perfect.

motions.m: This house would ban companies from making a profit from life-saving drugs.
motions.i: "Life-saving drugs" are medicines that people need to survive. For example, insulin for diabetes, or medicine for cancer or serious infections.

motions.m: This house believes helping poor people in your own country is more important than helping people overseas.
motions.i: Helping people in your own country means giving food, money, homes, or jobs to citizens who are struggling. Helping people overseas means sending aid, donations, or supplies to other countries, especially during disasters or wars.

motions.m: In times of economic need, THBT states should sell and/or mortgage their national treasures and monuments
THBT the human body should be government-owned property upon death
THW actively encourage families to have only one child
THW ban all fast food advertisements.
motions.i: Fast food are easily prepared processed food served in snack bars and restaurants as a quick meal. Some of the fast food establishments in South Korea include, Lotteria, McDonald's, Mom's Touch and Kyochon Chicken.

motions.m: THW allow South Koreans to volunteer and fight in foreign legions
motions.i: Foreign legions are volunteer military units made up of international fighters. Most of these foreign soldiers are former servicemen with military experience. Examples of foreign legions include, but are not limited to, the Kurdish People's Protection Unit in Syria, Peshmerga in Iraq, and the most recent International Legion of Territorial Defense of Ukraine. In light of the recent Russian invasion of Ukraine, several South Koreans including South Korean former Navy Seal and YouTuber Ken Rhee has gone to Ukraine to fight the Russians. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a warning in early March that citizens who enter Ukraine without permission are subject to criminal punishment and administrative restrictions.

motions.m: THBT welfare for the poor should be given in cash, without condition
motions.i: Welfare policies for the poor usually provide assistance in the form of vouchers, food stamps and subsidies where recipients are meant to spend them on specific items, usually essentials. The recipients are directed to use these supports to buy subsidised grains, apartment rental, healthcare or daily items such as kerosene. In certain cash-transfer welfare policies, welfare recipients are given cash. They must however fulfill certain conditions such as vaccinating their children, keeping their children in school and/or making regular health visits. Unconditional transfers are those that are given to beneficiaries without any specific requirements beyond income eligibility.

motions.m: THBT South Korea should impose a Casino Entry Levy and allow its citizens to gamble at all local casinos.
motions.i: Lotteries, horse racing, boat racing, and cycling are all legal forms of gambling in South Korea. However, casino gambling is strictly prohibited. South Koreans face a three-year prison sentence if they're caught at a casino. Any citizen found to have gambled in a casino abroad can also face prosecution on their return to the country. There are approximately 23 land-based casinos in the South Korea that are meant for tourists to gamble at. Only the Kangwon Land Casino, located near Pyeongchang, allows Koreans to gamble. --- Singapore Citizens who wish to gamble in Singapore casinos must pay for a Casino Entry Levy prior to entering the casino premises. These entry levies allow patrons to enter the casino premises for a fixed period of time. A day-levy (24 hours) costs USD120, and an annual levy (365 days) costs ~USD2400. It is an offence for Singapore Citizens to enter the casino without a valid entry levy ticket. Tourists do not need to purchase a Casino Entry Levy.

motions.m: THW ban schools from giving homework to students.
motions.i: Homework, or a homework assignment, is a set of tasks assigned to students by their teachers to be completed outside the classroom and school hours.

motions.m: THW allow victims to have a say in the punishments meted out to school bullies.
motions.i: Bullying in school can exist in many forms: It can be physical (pushing, punching, or hitting); verbal (name-calling or threats); or psychological and emotional (spreading rumors or excluding someone from a conversation or activity). With the pervasive use of social media, bullying can occur outside of school hours via emails, text messages, and Facebook posts. These exchanges are known as cyberbullying.

motions.m: Jane robbed a bank at gun point. In her bid to escape the police, she fell and hit her head. As a result of the fall, she lost all her memories after age 5. THW not convict Jane of any crime.
motions.i: When someone is found and proven guilty of committing a crime, they are convicted of that crime and must serve the penalty and punishment as required by the law.

motions.m: THBT communications are better on social media
motions.i: Social media is an internet-based form of communication that allows users to converse and interact with each other virtually.

motions.m: THB video gaming should be included in school curriculum
THBT all new public transportation contracts should only be awarded to fully electric bids
motions.i: Public transit is a system of vehicles used to transport a mass of people from one place to another such as trains and buses. An electric bid means that a public transportation vehicle uses electricity as its primary source of energy generation in order to function.

motions.m: THBT teachers should be replaced by computers
motions.i: A computer is a machine or device that can be instructed to perform processes, calculations, and other operations. With the improvement of technology, computers have evolved from only being able to perform simple arithmetic tasks to being capable of carrying out complex functions.

motions.m: THBT school students are too young for romance
THW set up homeless shelters in wealthy neighborhoods
motions.i: Due to poverty, many people often find themselves in a situation of homelessness, which is the state of not having stable, safe, and adequate housing. Government and non-government agencies usually build homeless shelters that provide temporary residence for homeless individuals and families.

motions.m: THS the rise of children social media influencers
motions.i: Social media is an internet-based form of communication that allows users to create web content and interact with each other. People who have large audiences of followers on their social media accounts are called social media influencers.

motions.m: THW ban plastic bags

THBT countries with a track record of human rights violations (e.g. Qatar, Russia, etc.) should be banned from hosting major sporting events and the citizens of those countries should be disallowed from owning sports teams.
motions.i: Countries with authoritarian political structures are generally understood to have a track record of human rights violations through taking away rights of people. Example of such countries are Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia, etc. In recent times, there has been a trend of such countries hosting major sporting events and institutions of such countries owning sports teams.

motions.m: TH prefers a world where art cannot be generated by artificial intelligence and would always require significant human input
motions.i: Artificial Intelligence is the simulation of human intelligence processes by machines, especially computer systems that can perform a lot of activities including generation of artwork

motions.m: TH would impose term limits on head of state positions in democracies
motions.i: In democracies head of states generally have 3 to 5 years terms. A term limit refers to a maximum number of term limits that a head of states can have e.g.: a US president can be elected a maximum of 2 terms

motions.m: This House prefers a world where people do not feel anger
motions.i: for the purpose of this debate, people are able to experience a world without feeling a particular emotion

motions.m: This House would give more votes to low income individuals
motions.i: for the purposes of this debate, the number of votes someone can have in an election can exceed the normal one vote per person

motions.m: This House Believes That western liberal democracies should penalize tourism in countries that abuse the human rights of their citizens
motions.i: western liberal democracies refer to countries from the global west along with Australia and New Zealand that generally have open and democratic countries.

motions.m: This House Would allow ex-convicts to join the police force
motions.i: an ex-convict is an individual who has been deemed guilty of a crime, punished, and has fully served their punishment. For example, someone that has finished the entirety of their prison sentence and is now allowed to go back into society is an ex-convict.

motions.m: This House Would tax non-renewable energy to match the cost of the cheapest available renewable energy source
motions.i: Currently renewable energy sources cost more per unit of energy than non-renewable sources of energy. The two prices can only be matched through a tax imposed on non-renewable sources such that it rises up to match renewable energy sources.

motions.m: This House believes that primary and secondary schools should allocate an equal portion of time in school to academic and extracurricular activities
motions.i: extracurricular activities refer to activities that are beyond the academic curriculum of children such as debating, sports, etc.

motions.m: TH, as young professional in China, would support the "lying flat" (tangping) movement
THBT universities should implement race-blind affirmative action admissions
THW break up major media conglomerates (e.g. Disney)
THW mandate that buy-outs of major social media platforms be subject to a vote of approval by their users
THBT the Bretton Woods Institutions have done more harm than good
TH, as parent from a minority community, Would raise their child to have pessimistic outlook on life
TH, as the Republican Party, would abandon the strategy of claiming the 2020 presidential elections were fraudulent
THBT States should aggressively prioritize funding rent-assistance programs over public housing project
TH Regrets the narrative that children are inherently pure and good
motions.i: Currently, children are believed to be inherently good and pure beings. Meaning, even if they can be influenced by bad actors and actions, they start out innocent and good and can go back to being good since this is a built-in characteristic in them.

motions.m: THR the rise of influencer-lead marketing campaigns
This House Would pay stay-at-home mothers a salary
motions.i: A stay-at-home mother is a mother who chooses not to seek formal employment and chooses to raise their children and manage the household.

motions.m: TH Would allow employers to access, or request access to, the criminal records of job applicants
motions.i: Currently, employers are allowed to access the criminal record of job applicants. This gives them access to knowledge of if an applicant has ever been convicted of a crime or imprisoned.

motions.m: TH prefers a world without any government restrictions on international migration
motions.i: Currently, governments use passport applications, visa applications, and immigration restrictions to control and limit the people moving into and out of countries.

motions.m: TH welcomes the automation of labor
motions.i: The automation of labor is the trend of substituting technology for human labor to perform tasks and jobs that technology can reasonably carry out.

motions.m: THW revoke all government subsidized health benefits for individuals who choose to lead unhealthy lifestyles
motions.i: An unhealthy lifestyle is a way of living that results in long term harms to one's health. E.g smoking, eatings fast food, and drinking excessive volumes of alcohol.

motions.m: This House Believes That internet anonymity does more harm than good
motions.i: Internet anonymity is the practice of not using one's real name and identity when interacting on the internet.
