motions.m: This house believes that feminist movements in developing nations should publicly disavow western forms of feminism
motions.i: Western feminism refers to the feminist movements in the Western world, as well as their particular goals and strategies (e.g. closing the gender pay gap, using high profile celebrities as spokespeople)

motions.m: This house believes that governments should be able to censor the publication of economic news during confidence crises (e.g. stock market crashes, bank runs)
motions.i: Confidence crises occur in economies when there is a general belief that the economy is doing poorly. This can often prompt rapid selling of shares, or bank runs when people seek to withdraw their money from banks because they fear that they could collapse. This can form a feedback loop that worsens the crisis by tanking the stock market or causing a credit crisis.

motions.m: This house opposes China's effort to deepen its relationship with Myanmar
motions.i: The governments of China and Myanmar have grown closer since the military coup in Myanmar in 2021. The two foreign ministers of each country have agreed to 1) establish a China-Myanmar Economic Corridor, 2) boost a range of cross-border cooperation including cross-border industrial zones, 3) increase Chinese support for development in Myanmar 4) allow the Myanmar regime to open a consulate in Chongqing, and 5) that China will provide a 650 million RMB grant to Myanmar, among other measures. The Myanmar junta has also agreed to open a Liquid Natural Gas plant financed by China, and is likely to approve multiple other new Chinese projects within the Economic Corridor. The Myanmar government has recently ramped up its war against rebel groups, and has been documented to have repeatedly committed atrocities against civilians.

motions.m: This house would ban sports with a significant risk of traumatic head injury
motions.i: Several popular sports (e.g. boxing, rugby) have relatively high risk of traumatic head injuries, which can cause significant damage to the brain. In response to several high-profile cases of brain damage in former athletes in later life, some of these sports have recently updated their concussion protocols to try to reduce the risk to athletes (e.g. making it easier for doctors to force a boxing match to end for medical reasons)

motions.m: This house believes that the state should offer criminals the option of a 'new identity' upon completing a prison sentence.
motions.i: A 'new identity' for the purposes of this debate includes a new name, relocation to a new area, a new background story, and optional plastic surgery.

motions.m: This house would ban private tutoring
motions.i: Private tutoring is a type of teaching that occurs outside the formal government education system. It is usually sought after by parents who wish for their children to excel in school. It is not subject to any curriculum requirements, and frequently has limited government oversight. Some examples include 'cram schools' in many Asian countries, as well as one-on-one private lessons in particular subjects (e.g. Maths). Private tutoring can consist of one on one lessons or group lessons.

motions.m: This house would reduce the minimum qualifications required to become a teacher
motions.i: Currently, many countries require school teachers to have a university degree in the subject they are teaching, as well as secondary degree specifically in teaching. Most countries in the world are experiencing a teacher shortage due to relatively low wage, high entry requirements and poor working condition.

motions.m: That we should focus on societal factors (e.g. socioeconomic factors) as opposed to individual factors (e.g. personal history) when addressing the causes and consequences of mental health issues
motions.i: Socioeconomic factors refer to the social and economic conditions that shape an individual's life, including their income, education, employment, social status, and access to resources and services. Individual factors, on the other hand, refer to an individual's personal characteristics and experiences, including their genetics, personality, life history, and coping strategies.

motions.m: TH supports the development and use of moral enhancement drugs
That medical staff should be able to opt out of providing treatments on religious or ethical grounds.
motions.i: Medical staff may opt out of providing treatments on religious or ethical grounds, such as objections to procedures like abortions or medications that conflict with their beliefs. For example, some may object to certain procedures such as abortions or assisted suicide, on the basis of religious teachings or ethical principles that prohibit taking a human life.

motions.m: That we should ban juvenile detention centers, and, instead, force all children who commit crimes to complete community service
motions.i: judge will determine the appropriate amount of community service a child needs to perform. This debate should not be about the exact amount of community sentence that would be appropriate.

motions.m: That adult children, where they are financially capable, should have a legal obligation to look after their elderly parents
motions.i: for the purpose of this debate, this law might force adult children to pay for their parents' food, clothing, shelter and medical needs. If children fail to do so, they can be fined, or even go to jail.

motions.m: This house supports the creation of an AI non-proliferation treaty
motions.i: An AI non-proliferation treaty would involve governments mutually agreeing to cease developing artificial intelligence for military and espionage purposes

motions.m: This house prefers Disney optimism to Ghibli optimism
This house believes that humanity should invest considerable amount of resources into the exploration of deep space
This house would require all politicians to use publicly run utilities/infrastructure where the options exists to do so
motions.i: Publicly run utilities/infrastructure are services provided by the government, for example public schools, public transportation and government-run hospitals.

motions.m: This house regrets the belief that journalists should cover 'both sides' of current affairs
motions.i: There is a norm in media that journalisms should provide a platform to all sides of contentious issues, for instance by interviewing supporters and opponents of a policy, and treating different perspectives on contentious issues as valid and worthy of receiving coverage.

motions.m: This house regrets the rise of teenagers at the forefront of social movements
motions.i: In recent years, teenagers and young people (e.g. Greta Thunberg, Malala Yousafzai, the Parkland students) have increasingly been central parts of campaigns by social movements (e.g. the climate movement, feminism, and in favour of gun control). This means they have advocated publicly for their positive, received extension media attention, and campaigned directly to politicians.

motions.m: This house supports the active integration of ChatGPT (and technology of similar functionalities) as a tool for education in schools (secondary schools onwards)
motions.i: ChatGPT is a powerful artificial intelligence (AI) system that uses advanced neural networks to understand and generate human-like text, learning from an enormous amount of data to provide helpful and accurate responses to your questions or prompts

motions.m: This house believes that developing countries should heavily discourage urbanization
motions.i: Urbanization is the process where more and more people move from rural areas (like the countryside or small villages) to urban areas (like cities and towns). As cities grow, they attract even more people, creating a cycle of expansion

motions.m: This house believes that, in established democracies, all major government policy decisions should be decided through referendum.
motions.r: U15 Grand Finals, U14 Grand Finals, U13 Grand Finals
motions.i: A referendum is a direct vote in which the entire electorate is invited to either accept or reject a particular proposal, law, or constitutional amendment. It is a form of direct democracy where citizens have the opportunity to express their opinion on a specific issue by casting their votes.

motions.m: This house regrets the glorification of the criminal way of life in certain series.
motions.r: U18 Grand Final, U16 Grand Finals, U15 Semifinals, U14 Semifinals, U13 Semifinals
motions.i: Narcos, Breaking Bad and Fariña are some examples of series where the central character(s) live a life of crime and various aspects of their ways of going about are portrayed to entertain the audience.

motions.m: This house prefers a world where gods are seen as imperfect beings.
motions.i: Currently, most religions conceive God as perfect beings which means viewing them as entities that know everything, see everything, and have power over everything in the world.

motions.m: This house supports the active promotion of Slum Tourism.
motions.i: Slum tourism is a type of tourism that involves visiting impoverished areas - usually through a service provided by tourism agencies. Slum tourism is now becoming increasingly prominent in developing nations, including but not limited to India, Brazil, Kenya and Indonesia.

motions.m: This house, as a young and emerging artist, would sign up for a music company with Western label model (e.g. Sony Music, Universal Music Group) as opposed to one with a K-pop label model (e.g. HYBE, YG Entertainment)
motions.i: K-pop labels are involved in the training of artists, curation and production of the artist's content, and in creating a steady stream of 'trainee' artists who debut after spending time training with the label. Western labels, on the other hand, often select artists who have reached small amounts of fame, and allow for a lot more creative control over the content they produce.

motions.m: This house believes that the rise of Startup culture does more harm than good.
motions.i: Startup culture refers to the set of values, norms, practices, and behaviors commonly associated with entrepreneurial ventures and early-stage companies. It encompasses the unique atmosphere, attitudes, and working environment prevalent in startups, characterized by innovation, risk-taking, agility, and a strong emphasis on growth.

motions.m: This house believes that parents should use fear as a way to instill values in their children.
motions.i: The use of fear to instill values refers to the practice of employing threats or intimidation as a means to influence individuals' behavior and beliefs in alignment with certain moral or ethical principles. This approach relies on creating a sense of apprehension or anxiety about the potential consequences of not adhering to these values.

motions.m: THBT in countries where men are conscripted, eg: Singapore, South Korea, the feminist movement should advocate for the conscription of women as well
motions.i: Conscription refers to a compulsory period of military service. For example, all men in Switzerland must serve 21 weeks in the military before the age of 25.

motions.m: THR the commercialisation of sports
motions.r: U13 Pre-Quarterfinals, U12 Octofinals, U11 Semifinals
motions.i: Commercialisation of sports refers to making profit from sports. For example, by charging lots of money for tickets, or having advertisting during games.

motions.m: THBT governments should aggressively fund space exploration and technologies.
motions.i: Many private companies are developing space technology, for example rockets to go into space and explore other planets.

motions.m: THBT the government should move citizens and their homes away from areas with very high natural disaster risk.
motions.i: Some areas have a very high risk of natural disasters, for example cities next to volcanoes, or with lots of earthquakes.

motions.m: THW set the cost of fines based on the criminal's income, rather than the severity of the crime.
motions.i: The punishment for some crimes is paying money (a fine) to the government, rather than going to jail.

motions.m: THBT school students should be taught how to use AI.
motions.i: AI (artificial intelligence) is computer software with human-like intelligence. It can write, and make images and videos.

motions.m: THW ban the advertising of unhealthy products.
motions.i: Many companies advertise unhealthy products, such as cigarettes or junk food.

motions.m: This house would allocate the majority of government sports funding towards fostering participation in lesser-known or underrepresented sports rather than prioritizing investment in mainstream sports within the nation.
motions.i: Mainstream sports are widely popular and played or followed by large numbers of people globally. Examples include soccer, basketball, tennis, football, and baseball. This is in contrast to less well-known sports which refers to sports with a smaller following or limited regional popularity. Examples include handball, curling, lacrosse, sepak takraw and squash.

motions.m: This house believes that directors from minority communities in major Hollywood studios should impose preferential treatment (prioritizing them in hiring and lead roles, increasing the number studio staff, etc) for the people from their own community in their blockbuster films
motions.i: Preferential treatment refers to the act of giving someone priority or providing them with advantages, benefits, or more favorable conditions over others, often based on specific characteristics such as race, gender, nationality, or socioeconomic status.

motions.m: This house supports the environmental movement's shift towards climate litigation
motions.i: Climate litigation refers to the use of legal action, either through courts or tribunals, to address issues related to climate change. It involves individuals, communities, NGOs, or even governments suing corporations, states, or institutions for their role in contributing to or failing to mitigate climate change.

motions.m: Assuming the technological feasibility, this house would replace human soldiers in military with Autonomous Robot
motions.i: Autonomous robot is a machine that can perform tasks or make decisions without human intervention, using sensors, algorithms, and artificial intelligence to navigate and adapt to its environment.

motions.m: In districts where it is dominated with Arabic community, this house believes that Arabic should be the primary language of instruction in schools instead of French.
motions.i: In the 1950s–70s, France encouraged immigration to fill low-wage jobs in industry, construction, and public services. As a result, immigrants from Algeria, Morocco, and Tunisia has come and settled in urban industrial areas such as Paris, Lyon, Marseille, Lille, forming their own community. In these areas, Arabic is frequently spoken at home and in the community.

motions.m: This house would require startups and new businesses to pass financial sustainability test before qualifying for state subsidies and support
motions.i: A financial sustainability test is a tool or assessment used to evaluate whether a project, organization, or policy can maintain its financial operations over the long term.The test includes but not limited to minimum level of annual nett revenue, continuous operations plan for 2 years, minimum level of ratio of income to operational cost, etc.

motions.m: As an average person, in Western Liberal Democracies, this house would take the pill.
motions.i: There is a pill that maximizes an individual's potential based on their aptitude. For example, if someone has the potential to become the world's greatest chef, the pill will immediately unlock that potential.

motions.m: That the government should ban all personal mobile devices that can access the internet for people under 18
motions.i: A personal mobile device is any personal device that someone can carry with them, such as (but not limited to) a mobile phone, tablet, or laptop.

motions.m: This house would abolish sports involving animals.
motions.i: Animal sports involve competitions where animals participate in races, fights, or performances, often showcasing their speed, strength, or skill. Common examples include horse racing, greyhound racing, and bullfighting.

motions.m: This house would abolish school uniforms
motions.i: A school uniform is a designated attire that all students must wear during school hours. While some schools allow accessories, they must not disrupt the overall attire in any way.

motions.m: This house opposes the narrative that wisdom increases with age
motions.i: Wisdom is the trait of having good judgement and knowledge, often used in solving problems.

motions.m: This house regrets the creation of child influencers
motions.i: With the rise of social media, more parents have become influencers, frequently sharing content featuring their children. Many do this to boost their online presence, often posting videos that gain significant attention. Some of these videos go viral, exposing these children to millions of viewers across the internet.

motions.m: This house would heavily regulate junk entertainment
motions.i: Junk entertainment refers to media that prioritizes shock value, drama, or mindless consumption over substance

motions.m: This house believes that government should not provide healthcare services to those who lead an unhealthy life.
motions.i: An unhealthy lifestyle includes poor diet, lack of exercise, and harmful habits like smoking or excessive alcohol consumption. This often leads to serious health issues such as obesity and heart disease.

motions.m: This House opposes the narrative that fear is a flaw.
motions.i: In popular media, fear is often portrayed as a flaw or obstacle that needs to be overcome; however, others argue that fear is a natural and often useful emotion.

motions.m: This House believes that schools should employ edutainment as their primary education method.
motions.i: Edutainment is a way of learning that combines education with entertainment. It means teaching subjects through fun and engaging methods like games, videos, storytelling, or interactive activities. The goal is to make learning enjoyable rather than relying on activities that require high levels of concentration or effort.

motions.m: This House prefers a world where there is a one, single global government.
motions.i: A "global government" means a single political system that makes and enforces laws for all countries and people in the world, instead of separate national governments like those of South Korea or the United States.

motions.m: This House supports resurrection biology.
motions.i: Resurrection biology is the idea of bringing extinct animals back to life using modern science. Scientists use tools like genetic engineering and cloning to recreate species that no longer exist by studying their DNA and combining it with living organisms.

motions.m: This House prefers to raise their children with Type A personality traits.
motions.i: In a society where individuals can be distinctly categorized into two personality types, Type A individuals are ambitious, organized, proactive, and high-achieving; they tend to set high expectations for themselves. Meanwhile, Type B individuals are easygoing, creative, and relaxed; they are social, less stressed, and tend to "go with the flow".

motions.m: This House opposes the creation of AI machines for social interactions.
motions.i: As AI technology advances there is a question of whether or not AI should be treated like people in society this includes designing machines that could act as friends or companions.

motions.m: This House would legalize commercial organ selling.
motions.i: Commercial organ selling refers to the legal buying and selling of human organs (such as kidneys or liver segments) for transplantation, typically involving financial compensation for the donor.

motions.m: This House, as the South Korean Ministry of Culture, supports the adaptation of South Korean entertainment to appeal to global audiences.
motions.i: The global popularity of South Korean culture refers to how entertainment from South Korea, such as K-pop, movies, and TV shows, has become widely enjoyed around the world. This trend is often called the Hallyu or "Korean Wave." Popular works like Squid Game and Parasite have helped shape how people in other countries see South Korea, increasing interest in its culture and boosting tourism. At the same time, some argue that South Korean entertainment has adapted its style to appeal more to global (especially Western) audiences.

motions.m: THP a world where a god (or gods) is viewed as dystheistic rather than morally infallable
motions.i: Dystheism is a theological concept that refers to the belief in a deity or deities who are not wholly good or benevolent, and may even be considered malevolent or evil. Significant examples of religions that exhibit a dystheistic perspective include the belief system of the Ancient Greeks, who worshipped gods that frequently engaged in conflicts among themselves and manipulated humans for their own entertainment. Another example is Hinduism, where one of the gods, Shiva, is directly associated with deliberate destruction directed at people. The term ""morally infallible"" refers to the idea that an entity, typically a deity or a divine being, is incapable of making any moral mistakes or committing any morally wrong actions. In other words, a morally infallible being is perfect in its moral judgment and behavior, always acting in alignment with moral principles and never engaging in morally objectionable acts.

motions.m: THW require companies to report all power harassment complaints to the Labour Standards Bureau for investigation and prosecution instead of settling these grievances internally.
motions.i: Workplace bullying, (also known as "Power harassment" in Japan) is defined as a form of bullying by those who are in a higher work position. Based on Japan's Labor Policy Comprehensive Promotion Act, Power Harassment consists of "remarks and behaviour of people taking advantage of their superior positions in the workplace that exceed what is necessary and appropriate for the conduct of business, thereby harming the working environment of employees". Examples of Power Harassment include, but are not limited to, physical abuse, emotional abuse (e.g. threats, defamation, insults, rants), and excessive demand at work (e.g. unnecessary requests, unfulfillable tasks, obstruction of work).

motions.m: THW hold internet service providers and website companies legally liable for most content created by users of their services, including content that could be considered false or defamatory
motions.i: in many jurisdictions, online platforms are largely granted immunity from civil liability (such as fines) for content posted by others on their platforms, as well as for removing or failing to remove said content with the exception of copyright claims

motions.m: This House believes that Japan should seek to address its aging population crisis by focusing on opening its border to more immigrants, investing in automation, and encourage longer working lives as opposed to encouraging a higher birth rate (e.g. subsidies for child care, baby bonuses, paternity leave)
motions.i: Japan is facing an aging population crisis, characterized by a rapidly increasing proportion of elderly citizens, a declining irth rate, and a shrinking workforce. This demographic shift is a result of several factors, including increasing life expectancy and low fertility rates.

motions.m: This House regrets the decline of lifetime employment offers
motions.i: Shūshin koyō (終身雇用) is the term for permanent employment in Japan that evolved from Japan's impressive postwar economic growth during the Showa Era. In the practice of lifetime employment, companies typically hire new college graduates and implicitly guarantee them stable employment until they reach the retirement age. This gave rise to the notion of a salaryman with unshakable loyalty to their company in exchange for lifetime employment security. Japan's lifetime employment system, backed by seniority-based wages, became the standard after the 1950's. Today many believe that the concept of lifetime employment is outdated. In fact, only 8.8 percent of Japanese companies now have lifetime employment policies in place

motions.m: THBT convicted members of the Yakuza should not have the right to vote.
motions.i: The Yakuza is a collective term for Japanese organized crime syndicates, similar to the Mafia in Italy or other organized crime groups around the world. Yakuza groups are involved in a wide range of illegal activities, including drug trafficking, illegal gambling, loan sharking, extortion, human trafficking, and various white-collar crimes. They are also known to have legitimate business interests, which they use to launder money and maintain influence in society. At its peak in the 1960s, the yakuza had more than 180,000 members. Of late, stricter laws such as those forbidding yakuza members from opening bank accounts, obtaining a credit card, taking out insurance policies or even signing a contract for a mobile phone have made a life of crime increasingly unappealing. There are thought to be about 25,000 active yakuza members in Japan today.

motions.m: TH supports the move to restart or build new nuclear power plants in Japan.
motions.i: Nuclear energy has been a national strategic priority for Japan since 1973. Japan's reactors provided some 30% of the country's electricity before the Fukushima accident in 2011. The Fukushima accident was a nuclear disaster that occurred at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant in Fukushima Prefecture, Japan. It was triggered by a massive 9.0-magnitude earthquake and subsequent tsunami, which caused widespread destruction along the northeastern coast of Japan. Radioactive particles were carried by wind and rain leading to widespread radioactive contamination of land, air, and water in the surrounding areas. The disaster also prompted the evacuation of over 150,000 people from the area surrounding the power plant, creating a large exclusion zone. The cleanup and decommissioning process is estimated to take several decades to complete. Since then, the government has reduced the role of nuclear power. In early 2023, Japan's Cabinet approved plans to extend the lifespans of nuclear reactors beyond 60 years and build new units to replace those that are decommissioned, reversing policies put in place after the Fukushima disaster in 2011.

motions.m: TH supports Anonymous in its retaliation against injustice where the state seems unwilling or unable to do so
motions.i: Hacktivism refers to the use of computer hacking skills, cyber techniques, and digital tools to promote, advance, or protest a social, political, or environmental issue. Hacktivists, or individuals who engage in hacktivism, often employ tactics such as website defacement, distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, data breaches, or the release of sensitive or confidential information to make a statement or raise awareness about a cause. Anonymous, a group of hacktivist, employs coordinated cyberattacks against various world governments, corporations or other groups, often in the name of social or political causes. On February 25, 2022, Anonymous declared that they had launched 'cyber operations' against the Russian Federation, in retaliation for the invasion of Ukraine ordered by Russian president Vladimir Putin. Since then, the group has claimed responsibility for various cyberattacks that disabled websites and leaked data from Russian government agencies

motions.m: TH, as Chie Yasutake, would keep the child
motions.i: Hana's Miso Soup was an iconic Japanese movie about a young woman, Chie Yasutake. In the movie, Chie, who is a recent college graduate, becomes engaged to the love of her life, Shingo. Her life, however, takes an unexpected turn when she is diagnosed with terminal cancer. The ensuing cancer treatment would also render her infertile. Despite that, life delivers another unexpected twist when Chie finds herself impregnated. She is now faced with the difficult choice of taking the risk or ending the pregnancy for which she has yearned.

motions.m: This house supports conscription in wartime
motions.i: Conscripts are civilians who are forced into military service by law, distinct from those who are employed full-time in the army, navy or air force. Conscription can include training and active involvement in war. Under conscription, refusing to join is a criminal offence.

motions.m: This house believes that teams' season ticket holders should be allowed to vote to veto major transactions involving their sporting team (e.g., hiring/firing of a manager/coach, sale of team, change of stadium)
motions.i: In many sports, teams sell season tickets to fans, giving them access to all of the team's games for an entire season. There are a limited number of these tickets and they can go for thousands of dollars. Often, a portion of these tickets are specially reserved for longtime fans who have attended a large number of games. These tickets are a key revenue source for many teams and sports leagues. Currently, only owners and senior managers can make major decisions regarding a team.

motions.m: This house believes that the rise of social media platforms focusing on short-form content (e.g., Twitter with 280 word max character limit, TikTok with 60 second videos) has done more harm than good.
motions.i: Short-form content is a recent phenomenon that has seen increasing popularity with the rise of apps like TikTok, and refers to videos, posts, songs and other content that tends to be under one minute in length. Recently, this form of content has been promoted by social media platforms, for example, short- form videos may pop up on someone's YouTube front page more often.

motions.m: This house regrets the glorification on academic achievement over social relationships
motions.i: Academic achievement refers to success in terms of grades, awards and any form of recognition of an individual's intelligence or achievement in school or university. Social relationships encompasses all relationships like friendships, romantic relationships and parental relationships.

motions.m: This house believes that judges should actively consider victim impact statements when sentencing a criminal.
motions.i: A victim impact statement is a written or oral statement presented to the court at the sentencing of the defendant. Victims and their friends and family may participate in both written and verbal statements. The statement serves as an opportunity for the judge to hear the personal suffering resulted from a criminal act. In status quo, these statements are often given low weightage.

motions.m: This house opposes the rise of the Mystic Arts (e.g., tarot reading, astrology calculations, witchcraft, dream interpretation)
motions.i: Mystic arts are special practices that people use to try to understand or connect with invisible powers, like magic, spirits, or energy. They're often about learning secret knowledge or finding deeper meaning in life.(e.g., tarot reading, astrology calculations, witchcraft, dream interpretation)

motions.m: This house believes that Bill should publish his findings
motions.i: Bill is a genius scientist who has accidentally discovered irrefutable evidence that a God exists. Bill has the opportunity to destroy this evidence where it is unlikely to ever be discovered again. Or he can publish his findings.

motions.m: This house would ban sports that have significant rates of head injuries
motions.i: Many popular sports like American football, rugby, boxing, MMA, and ice hockey have reported a high rate of concussions and other head injuries.

motions.m: This house would ban animal testing
motions.i: Animal testing is the process of using animals to test the effectiveness and safety of products like medicines, and cosmetics.

motions.m: Assuming it were possible, this house would clone a beloved pet that has passed away.
motions.i: Cloning is the process of making an identical copy of a living creature.

motions.m: This house supports bilingual education systems
motions.i: Bilingual education is a mode of education in which students are educated in two (or more) languages (i.e., some school subjects are taught in a second language).

motions.m: This house believes that governments should not fund space exploration
motions.i: In 2024, global spending on space programs hit a record of approximately 135 billion U.S. dollars. Space exploration projects have historically let to many modern inventions like portable computers, water purifiers, and solar panels. However, many critics say this money can be better spent on soving current problems on Earth.

motions.m: This house regrets the rise of short form content
motions.i: Short form content is social media content that focuses on short and quick consumption. This includes short-form videos like TikToks, Instagram reels, and YouTube shorts.

motions.m: This house believes that the environmentalist movement should prioritise advocating for nuclear rather than green (i.e., wind, hydro, solar) energy to replace non-renewable sources (i.e., coal, oil).
motions.i: Nuclear power is a highly efficient energy source. Compared to other renewable sources of energy, nuclear power has a high set up cost, but then very low long term maintenance cost. In addition, radioactive waste is produced by nuclear power plants.

motions.m: This house regrets the black and white narrative of good and evil in children's books and movies.
motions.i: In most modern children's cartoons (i.e., Disney), have a 'black and white' view of good and evil, where heroes always win at the end of the story and villains always lose. This is as opposed to a 'grey' view, where heroes may sometimes lose and die fighting for good, and villains may win.

motions.m: This house believes that governments should have the right to shut down the internet during periods of civil unrest.
motions.i: Government internet shutdowns refer to deliberate actions by authorities to restrict or completely cut off access to the internet or specific platforms within a region, typically during periods of unrest, protests, or perceived threats to public order.

motions.m: This house regrets the normalization of cutting off friends and family for ideological differences
motions.i: Ideological differences refer to disagreements in core beliefs, values, or worldviews such as politics, religion, or moral principles that shape how individuals understand society and make decisions.

motions.m: This house would abolish gendered media awards e.g., best actress in the Oscars
motions.i: Gendered media awards are award categories that separate nominees based on gender (e.g., "Best Actor" vs "Best Actress"), meaning individuals compete only within their gender group rather than in a single combined category.

motions.m: This house believes that the prominence of the gig economy in developing countries does more harm than good
motions.i: The gig economy involves short-term, flexible work (e.g., ride-hailing, delivery apps)

motions.m: This house would prohibit public expression of political opinions for religious leaders
motions.i: A religious leader is an individual who guides a community in spiritual beliefs, practices, and moral teachings within a religion. They can have influence in interpreting doctrine and leading rituals.

motions.m: This house prefers a world in which there is no desire for revenge
motions.i: Revenge is the act of seeking to harm or punish someone in response to a perceived wrong or injustice.

motions.m: This House would limit the maximum salary of professional athletes and celebrities.
motions.i: Nowadays, some athletes and celebrities earn millions and sometimes even billions, while teachers, doctors and lawyers make considerably less.

motions.m: This House would ban child actors.
motions.i: Child actors are performers under the age of 18 who work in movies, TV shows, and commercials while balancing their education and personal lives. To protect them, they are restricted to working short hours and are required by law to complete several hours of school on set every day.

motions.m: This House prefers to live in a world where individuals with superhuman abilities exist.
motions.i: In superhero media, individuals with superhuman abilities often use their powers to protect the public, but their actions frequently cause massive collateral damage and overshadow regular law enforcement. Furthermore, this severe power imbalance carries the constant risk that these individuals could use their abilities for personal gain or villainy, leaving ordinary citizens defenseless.

motions.m: This House would give students an equal vote on major school rules.
motions.i: Giving students a vote on major school rules means they would help decide on things like uniforms, the length of the school day, or which subjects are taught.

motions.m: This House would ban all single-use plastic items (like water bottles, straws, and snack wrappers).
motions.i: Single-use plastics are items like water bottles, straws, and food wrappers that are used once and then thrown away. Banning these items would reduce the amount of trash that ends up in our oceans and hurts sea animals, but it would also make it harder and more expensive for people to carry their drinks and snacks.

motions.m: This House would stop separating sports teams by gender and instead group all athletes by physical ability and skill level.
motions.i: Currently, most youth and professional sports are divided into separate categories for boys and girls to try and ensure fairness. However, some people argue that grouping athletes strictly by their actual physical ability, size, or skill level, regardless of their gender, would create more exciting, inclusive, and truly equal competitions for everyone.

motions.m: This House believes that social movements should actively support artists who include social messages in their art.
motions.i: Social movements are organized groups that work to create social or political change. For example, Fridays for Future is a youth movement that asks governments to protect the planet and fight climate change. STOMP Out Bullying promotes kindness and stopping bullying in schools. The Malala Fund supports girls' right to go to school. Some artists also share social messages through their art. For example, Billie Eilish speaks about protecting the environment, Bong Joon-ho, Director of Parasite the film highlights social inequality and the big gap between rich and poor people in society.

motions.m: This House prefers a world where death is primarily treated as a celebration rather than a moment of mourning.
motions.i: Different cultures treat death in different ways. In some traditions, death is commemorated through celebration rather than mourning. For example, in Mexico, the Día de los Muertos honors deceased loved ones through festivals, food, music, and colorful rituals meant to celebrate life and remembrance. Similar traditions exist in parts of Asia and Africa where death rituals emphasize celebration, continuity, and community gathering rather than solemn mourning.

motions.m: This House would ban parents from using GPS apps to track their children's location.
motions.i: Child tracking apps are tools that allow parents to see their child's exact location at all times through a smartphone or GPS device.

motions.m: This house believes that celebrities have a duty to speak out on political and social issues.
motions.i: Celebrities are people who are widely known to the public, such as actors, singers, athletes, and influencers. Speaking out on political and social issues means using their platform to raise awareness or take a stand on topics like climate change, racism, poverty, or elections.

motions.m: This house believes that the environmental movement should heavily prioritise nuclear energy in its advocacy
motions.i: Nuclear energy is an incredibly clean and efficient type of energy. That said, it is expensive to set up and if nuclear power plants are poorly contained, they can melt down in disasters such as Chernobyl.

motions.m: This house believes that the United Nations should focus solely on diplomacy, not military intervention
motions.i: The United Nations was founded in 1945 to maintain peace through diplomacy, cooperation, and conflict prevention. Over time, however, it has increasingly relied on military peacekeeping and armed interventions, deviating from its original role as a neutral peace-maker and mediator of disputes.

motions.m: This house would make volunteering a graduation requirement for high school students.
motions.i: Volunteering refers to unpaid work done for the benefit of the community, such as helping in charities, hospitals, environmental projects, or local organization

motions.m: This house opposes the narrative that people should get over a breakup after a certain time
motions.i: People are generally encouraged to get over a breakup within a certain period of time. As a rule of thumb, many people suggest that it takes about one month for each year that the relationship lasted to recover from a breakup.

motions.m: This house believes that parents should not financially support their adult children beyond the age of 25
motions.i: Many parents who have the means to do so help their adult children financially. This often includes paying for post-secondary education, helping to purchase a home, or providing a monthly allowance.

motions.m: This house believes that developing countries should heavily invest in charter schools.
motions.i: A charter school is a school that receives government funding but operates independently of the established state school system in which it is located. This gives charter schools the ability to pursue different curriculums and teaching methods than standard public schools and gives public school students the opportunity to have a private school experience at the cost of attending public school. Many charter schools have admission criteria (i.e., minimum grades).

motions.m: This house would restrict parents from monetizing their children's lives on social media (e.g., "family influencer" accounts).
motions.i: Family influencer accounts are social media accounts (e.g., on YouTube, TikTok, Instagram) where parents regularly post content featuring their children. Monetization refers to earning money from this content through sponsorships, brand deals, ads, or direct fan contributions.

motions.m: This house believes that parents should not help their children with their homework
motions.i: Many parents help their children with homework and other school assignments. If done correctly, this can improve children's learning outcomes, but if done poorly, it can result in a lack of independence.

motions.m: This house believes that the rise of gamified educational tools (e.g., Gimkit, Kahoot) does more harm than good
motions.i: Gamified education tools (like Kahoot!) are digital learning platforms that apply elements of game design, such as competition, points, rewards, leaderboards, and interactive challenges, to the educational process. Unlike traditional teaching methods, these tools transform learning activities into game-like experiences, often delivered through quizzes, polls, or challenges.

motions.m: This house would penalise sports teams for the violent behaviour of their fans
motions.i: Nearly all major European sports leagues have had experience with riots, violence or fights taking place at sporting stadiums and bar venues. These can lead to lots of injuries and tend to be started by intoxicated members of the public or individuals upset at the result of a sporting event.

motions.m: This house, as a young musician who's seen some early success in your career (e.g., small concerts, a Spotify and social media following), would sign with a large record label.
motions.i: In music, musicians sign contracts with record labels who take a cut of all money made by the musician in exchange for providing advertising, distribution of music, musical equipment, coaches and other aspects of music production. Labels also control the direction of one's musical career (e.g., singing style, target audience, public appearances). Comparatively, independent artists have more control over the direction of their music and produce their music alone or with a small team.

motions.m: This house supports the increased use of AI in mental health services
motions.i: Mental health services are special kinds of help from experts who support how we think, feel, and handle problems in everyday life.

motions.m: This house regrets the use of end-of-world narratives over hopeful narratives when advocating for climate change policy
motions.i: End-of-world narratives are portrayals that emphasize the potential destruction of the earth if global warming continues. This includes statistics of potential climate refugees, pictures of the harm of climate change, or emotionally strong language.

motions.m: This house would require both mothers and fathers to take the same amount of parental leave
motions.i: Parental leave is time off from work that parents are allowed to take after a baby is born or adopted, so they can stay home to care for their child.

motions.m: This house would ban celebrities from running for political office
motions.i: Celebrities: People who are very well-known by the public, usually because of their work in movies, music, sports, or social media. Political office: An official job in government, like being a mayor, president, or member of parliament, where a person has the power to make decisions and laws.

motions.m: This house believes that AI tutors harm students more than they help.
motions.i: AI tutors are computer programs that use artificial intelligence to help students learn. They can explain concepts, give practice questions, check answers, and adjust the difficulty based on the student's progress.

motions.m: This house would not allow employers to access, or request access to, the criminal records of job applicants.
motions.i: A criminal record is an official document kept by the state that lists a person's past interactions with the criminal justice system. This may include arrests, charges, convictions, sentences, and in some countries, even minor offenses. Today, employers are allowed to ask applicants about their criminal history or run background checks. Employers can use criminal history when deciding whether to hire someone.

motions.m: This house would choose a job based on passion instead of money.
motions.i: Passion is when you love doing something so much that it makes you excited and happy.

motions.m: This house would ban all meat and animal products.
motions.i: Meat and animal products refer to any food or products that come from animals. This includes meat (such as beef, pork, chicken, and seafood), dairy products (like milk, cheese, and butter), eggs, and animal-derived materials such as leather, wool, and gelatin.

motions.m: This house believes that the Feminist Movement should discourage the use of cosmetic products (eg. Make up, whitening cream, etc).
motions.i: Cosmetic products are items specifically designed to enhance or alter a person's appearance. Examples include make up products like foundation, lipstick, and mascara.

motions.m: This house would rather be a social-media influencer rather than an average person.
motions.i: A social media influencer is a person with a significant online following on one or more social media platforms. They may use their platform to create content, engage with their audience, and influence their followers' opinions and purchasing decisions, sometimes in collaboration with brands for marketing purposes.

motions.m: This House regrets the social expectation that people should always act selflessly.
motions.i: Acting selflessly means being concerned with the welfare of others, often putting their needs before your own. It involves acting out of a generous, unselfish, or altruistic motivation, such as helping those in need or volunteering your time, without seeking personal gain

motions.m: This House believes that post-conflict countries should actively pursue ethnic integration policies. (e.g ethnic quotas for public schools, housing, government positions, breaking up ethnic enclaves)
motions.i: Ethnic integration policies are government actions designed to reduce physical and geographical separation between ethnic groups after a conflict. Examples include: Ethnic quotas in public schools, housing, or government jobs to ensure mixed representation. Integrating or breaking up ethnic enclaves, so communities are not divided by group identity. Mixed housing programs and shared public services to increase daily interaction. Representation rules so decision-making bodies include multiple ethnic groups. Such policies are used in countries recovering from civil wars, ethnic violence, or long periods of segregation.

motions.m: This House prefers a world where the concept of an afterlife does not exist to a world with a widespread belief in an afterlife.
motions.i: The afterlife refers to the idea that some part of a person—such as the soul or consciousness—continues to exist after physical death. In many religions and cultures, the afterlife includes concepts like heaven, hell, reincarnation, spirits, or ancestral realms. These beliefs are often connected to moral systems of religions.

motions.m: THP Disney optimism to Ghibli optimism in children's media
motions.i: "For the purpose of this debate, there are two tropes in children's media. ""Disney optimism"" presents a world with 1\. clear good and evil 2\. wherein good always triumphs. ""Ghibli optimism"" presents a world with 1\. no true evil and morality is subjective 2\. where peaceful compromise is ultimately possible where neither side necessarily triumphs."

motions.m: THR the rise of retail investment
motions.i: Applications such as Robinhood have recently made it significantly easier for private individuals to invest in specific stocks and financial instruments. People use the app to purchase those stocks and financial instruments hoping the value will increase and they will turn a profit. This type of person is known as a "retail investor".

motions.m: TH, as an up and coming artist, would significantly pursue selling art through NFTs
motions.i: Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) are digital assets backed by blockchain technology, representing things such as art, collectibles such as photos, videos and music, or even real estate. These assets are non-fungible, which means each token is unique and one cannot get a like-for-like replacement when they are traded. They can be verified to prove uniqueness and digital ownership of the token in the blockchain network.

motions.m: THS efforts to colonise Mars
THR the rise of influencer culture
motions.i: 'Influencer culture' refers to the advertising and celebrity culture consisting of branded images of individuals called 'influencers' who have online presences on different social media platforms.

motions.m: THR the trend in cinema of remaking or returning versions of existing characters and stories in new movies (eg live remakes, rebooted franchises)
motions.i: Spider-Man: No Way Home is a 2021 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics character Spider-Man, produced by Columbia Pictures and Marvel Studios. In the film, Peter Parker asks Dr. Stephen Strange to use magic to make his Spider-Man identity a secret again following its public revelation at the end of Far From Home. When the spell goes wrong, the multiverse is broken open which allows visitors from alternate realities to enter Parker's universe. The film brings back multiple characters from previous Spider-Man franchises, including Tobey Maguire's Spider-Man from the Sam Raimi Spider-Man trilogy and Andrew Garfield's Spider-Man from the Amazing Spider-Man franchise.

motions.m: TH, as Summer, would date Person B, over continuing to pursue romance with Person A
motions.i: You are Summer, a single individual who has been romantically pursuing Person A, someone you have loved for a long time who has recently shown signs of minor interest. On the other hand, Person B is strongly infatuated with you and has been supporting you through your struggles.

motions.m: THW destroy the Death Note
motions.i: You are a staunch individual with an interest in the greater good and are in possession of the Death Note, a supernatural book which grants the owner of the book the ability to instantly and anonymously kill anyone whose name is written in its pages. For the purposes of the debate, only you are aware of the existence of the Death Note.

motions.m: THW drink the oreo milkshake
motions.i: There is an oreo milkshake that will grant immortality, until the end of humanity, to whoever drinks it. Its existence is only known to you, and only you can drink it.

motions.m: THBT ASEAN states should actively seek to participate in AUKUS
motions.i: AUKUS is a trilateral security pact between Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States, for the Indo-Pacific region. Under the pact, the US and the UK will help Australia to acquire nuclear-powered submarines.

motions.m: THP that the main opposition leader in authoritarian countries to be individuals with a strong background in politics and governance, as opposed to working class commoners
TH, as a mother, would teach their daughter to take a "hate all men" approach to their interactions
motions.i: The "hate all men" narrative refers to having an inherent prejudice or bias against men, especially through harboring suspicion towards their intentions.

motions.m: THBT developing states should heavily subsidise vocational schools over traditional colleges
motions.i: A vocational school is an education institution focused on education and training on technical skills required to complete the tasks of a particular and specific job.

motions.m: THR the heavy emphasis on internship experiences in the process of corporate hiring
motions.i: An internship is a professional learning experience that offers meaningful, practical work related to a student's field of study or career interest, by allowing them to work in a relevant company for a limited time.

motions.m: THW abolish standardised examinations (SPM, STPM, O Levels, A Levels) in favour of classroom based assessments/coursework
THBT marriage is incompatible with feminism
TH, as the feminist movement, would actively encourage women to reject acts of chivalry (e.g. men buying drinks, holding doors open, "ladies first")
THBT states that have largely benefited from the industrial revolution should monetarily compensate developing states to not cause greater harm to the environment
THO the rise of ecoterrorism by environmental groups
THW transfer the management of the global commons to states with strong environmental protection laws
motions.i: The 'global commons' is a term typically used to describe global resource domains in which common resources are found. Global commons include the earth's shared natural resources, such as the high oceans, the atmosphere and outer space and the Antarctic in particular.

motions.m: THW legalise usury.
motions.i: Usury, the practice of charging excessively high-interest rates on loans, is often considered unethical due to its exploitative nature and potential to harm borrowers financially. For lenders, usury can result in higher profits as the exorbitant interest rates allow them to generate significant returns on their loans, potentially outweighing the risks associated with lending to high-risk borrowers. Borrowers with poor credit history may be attracted to usury as it provides them with access to credit that they might not otherwise qualify for through traditional lending channels, offering a potential lifeline in times of financial need.

motions.m: TH, as Theoden, W not answer the beacons of Gondor
motions.i: In Lord of the RIngs, Theoden is King of Rohan, a large region of Middle Earth which is ruled by the race of men. Upon the orders of the Dark Lord Sauron, the neighbouring country of Isengard launched an attack on Rohan, forcing the people back into the safety of a fortress in the mountainside. After many battles, the nation of Rohan alone emerged victorious against the invading forces, winning the siege and pushing them out at the cost of the lives of many soldiers and civllians. A few days after the last major battle, beacons from the nearby country of Gondor are lit, signalling a call for aid from all of the nations of men. Gondor lies at the entrance of Barad-dur, the black gate in which behind lies the hosts and armies of the Dark Lord. It is obvious to all that a call for aid can only mean that Gondor is being attacked by the hosts of Mordor, signalling the start of the Dark Lord's war on Middle Earth. Due to the toll of the war, there remains only 6000 Rohirrim (Rohan's main calvary force) that Theoden can feasibly muster in time for the battle.

motions.m: TH, as the Pevensie children, W choose to stay in Narnia rather than return home
motions.i: The Pevensie children consisting of Peter, Susan, Edmund, and Lucy Pevensie stumbled upon the magical world of Narnia in their wardrobe. In Narnia, they joined forces with Narnia's magical creatures to overthrow the evil White Witch. After the battle, they were named the Kings and Queens of Narnia. However, at the end of every Narnian adventure, they return home to the 'real world'.

motions.m: TH, as Jon Snow, W choose to defy his punishment of being sent back to the Night's Watch
motions.i: In the Game of Thrones series, Jon Snow is revealed to be the son of Rhaegar Targaryen, the crown prince of the Targaryen dynasty, making him the rightful heir to the Iron Throne. However, he refused to take the Iron Throne and rule the Seven Kingdoms, thus allowing Daenerys Targaryen, his lover, to claim for the Throne. In a dramatic turn of events, Daenerys goes from liberator to murderer by killing innocent civilians in King's Landing by dragon fire in order to defeat her enemy Cersei Lannister. In an attempt to stop her, Jon murdered Daenerys and is thrown into prison. During a hearing to decide Jon's fate, it was decided to send him back to the ice Wall at the border of the seven kingdoms where he previously served in the Night's Watch.

motions.m: TH, as the US, W withhold aid from Israel until active steps are taken toward the two-state solution
motions.i: The two-state solution is a proposed framework to resolve the Israeli-Palestinian conflict by establishing independent states for Israel and Palestine. It envisions Israel existing as a Jewish state and Palestine as a sovereign state, with defined borders and mutual recognition. The aim is to achieve a peaceful coexistence, self-determination, and security for both Israelis and Palestinians. The solution would lead to multiple concessions on both sides regarding territory, standing military and diplomatic relations with each other and the rest of the Middle East. Currently, both sides are relatively reluctant to start this process

motions.m: TH, as India, W vote to reject new countries from joining the BRICS agreement
motions.i: BRICS is a new economic bloc consisting of Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa. The bloc is founded on the principles of forming global economic power, absent of the US and runs counter to the Western led G7 countries. Recently they have developed their own alternatives to large financial institutions like their own version of the IMF, a unique reserve currency meant to combat the USD and an alternative banking system off the Wall Street controlled SWIFT system. Multiple countries are seeking to join BRICS like Argentina and Iran while some are already benefits of it's subsidiaries like the UAE and Egypt. BRICS are also seeking new allies in Asia like Indonesia.

motions.m: THBT Western powers should actively support the Wagner group in their coup of Russia
motions.i: The Wagner group is a Russian state funded paramilitary group composed of mercenaries hired to carry out military operations. They have been instrumental in Russia's invasion of Ukraine and has carried most of the specialized strikes in the operation. Recently, the Wagner group has mounted a mutiny against Putin, marching armed forces into the country, stopping only 200km from Moscow. The head of the mutiny, Yevgeny Prigozhin, was greeted warmly by Russian citizens and met as well with strong anti-Putin sentiments. The mutiny was eventually stopped by a deal brokered by Lukashenko, President of Belarus and the Wagner group walked away unscathed from any Russian retaliation with the condition that he never returns to Russia.

motions.m: THP to live exclusively in UtopiaX
motions.i: UtopiaX is a simulated virtual reality (VR). This simulated world is designed to be a utopia where users are able to design their own world using the resources in the VR. Users are also able to interact with each other by joining each other's servers. However, a user's experiences in this VR is limited as physical interaction (i.e. touching, eating) is impossible.

motions.m: THS research that seeks to transfer human consciousness into simulations, computers, and artificial intelligence
THW mandate ESG investing for tech companies in developing countries.
motions.i: ESG investing is an approach to investing that focuses on Environmental, Social, and Governance factors alongside financial factors. Environmental factors assesses a company's carbon impact and environmental/green practices; Social factors assesses a company's impact on society, particularly on labour practices, employee diversity, and workplace safety; Social factors assesses how well a company is managed and governed with particular focus on shareholder rights, board composition, transparency, and more.

motions.m: THS the increase of religious narratives in environmental advocacy
motions.i: Various religions around the world incorporate the idea of environmental protection and conservation either implicitly or explicitly. For instance, Genesis 2:15 in the Christan Bible includes instructions from God to 'till and keep' the Garden of Eden and emphasises the idea of 'stewardship' over the Earth. In the Surah Al-A'raf 7:31, the importance of avoiding wasteful behaviour is highlighted.

motions.m: THW enact an 'Environmental Council' made up of environmental experts to create and manage environmental policies rather than governments
THW heavily subsidize climate migration
motions.i: Climate migration refers to the movement of people from one place to another due to the impacts of climate change. It occurs when individuals or communities are forced to leave their homes or regions because of environmental factors such as rising sea levels, droughts, extreme weather events, or other climate-related conditions that make their current living conditions unsafe or unsustainable.

motions.m: Assuming feasibility, THW ban public advertising of financial services to middle to lower income class citizens (loans, cryptocurrency, stocks, etc.)
THW nationalize all legal services in western liberal democracies
THBT SAG-AFTA should join the WGA in a worker's strike
motions.i: (A) In May 2023, the Writers Guild of America launched a strike against the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers (AMPTP), a trade association that represents over 350 American television and film production companies. The ongoing strike is the largest interruption to the American film and TV production since the COVID-19 pandemic. The guild, representing 11,500+ screenwriters, demands for the increase of wage and residuals (i.e. bonus/royalty) which have been decreasing due to the rise of streaming platforms and for the strict (and potentially regulated) boundaries of the use of AI technologies in the screenwriting process (i.e. as a complementary tool as opposed to a replacement tool). The Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG-AFTRA) owns a similar contract with AMPTP and the contract is set to expire soon. Many actors in the union has expressed overwhelming support for a similar strike if no deal is made.

motions.m: THP the act of loud quitting over quiet quitting
motions.i: Quiet quitting is the process of prioritizing boundaries in the workplace, not going above and beyond in your job in order to seek a promotion of better pay. This is often seen as a direct counter to the hustle culture that many companies engage in. Loud quitting is the process of taking active steps to harm the company, while undercutting their goals and opposing their leadership roles or upper management.

motions.m: THW ban extreme tourism
THBT western museums should return artifacts of significant cultural heritage to where they originated from
motions.i: Extreme Tourism is a type of travel that involves engaging in adventurous and thrilling activities in unique and often challenging environments. It's a way for people to push their limits, experience intense emotions, and explore remote and exciting destinations. Recently, the ante has increased with it including high octane trips to outer space or to the bottom of the ocean floor.

motions.m: THW implement performance-based pay over a fixed salary for teachers
motions.i: Performance-based pay is a form of compensation for teachers based on the performance of students in extracurricular (i.e. competitions) and curricular activities (i.e. performance on standardised tests)

motions.m: THR the Supreme Court's ruling on banning race-based admissions in universities
motions.i: The US Supreme Court recently ruled on a 6-3 majority that race based admissions in universities are unconstitutional on the grounds that it violates violate the Equal Protection Clause of the 14th Amendment.

motions.m: THW allow students in tertiary education to choose their subjects over choosing a degree
THR the narrative that a woman can have it all ( ie a successfull career and a happy family)
THBT it is in the interest of the international community to recognize Hamas as the legitimate government of Palestine
THR the dominant narrative that suffering and struggles are prerequisites to reward and happiness
THBT the narrative of "tough love" parenting has done more harm than good
TH, as SEA countries, W push to be integrated into the Quad
motions.i: The Quad is a group of countries consisting of Japan, India, Australia and the United States, with the intention of establishing presence within the Indo- Pacific region. The most recent edition of the Quad has been reestablished in 2017 and is seen as a response to the economic and militaristic actions of China within the wider Pacific region.

motions.m: THBT military retaliation is justified in situations where deliberate economic policies by another state have caused substantial economic harms.
THR the televisation of court trials of prominent actors (ie Johnny Depp & Amber Heard etc)
Assuming feasibility, THW erase all the memories of criminals and replace them with a fabricated set of memories, instead of sending them to prison
THS Vigilantism in high crime areas
motions.i: _Vigilantism refers to the practice of individuals or groups taking justice into their own hands without legal authority, often because they think official law enforcement is ineffective or corrupt._

motions.m: THS the rise in unschooling in developing countries
motions.i: Unschooling is a homeschooling method in which rather than following a specific curriculum, parents instead allow their children to choose what topics/subjects they would like to learn. For example, instead of studying standard algebra, children may want to learn about literary analysis instead, and thus a specific class would be constructed for it.

motions.m: THW establish vocational schools and aggressively stream underperforming students into them

THBT schools in lower socioeconomic communities should discourage students from pursuing degrees that are unlucrative (e.g., fine arts, philosophy, etc.), even if those subjects align with students' interests and aspirations.
TH, as Eitan, would actively be a homewrecker
motions.i: Eitan is an average teenager who has an intense crush to this girl since childhood. Due to his inaction and fear his long time crush is now dating someone else. Despite all this, Eitan remains a close friend , and does not believe that the current partner is good enough. Eitan has a significant amount of influence over his crush and their opinions. Eitan believes she can do better.

motions.m: THP a world where the norm for high school students is to avoid relationships until graduation.
THW go no-contact with their exes.
THP the existence of love potions
motions.i: Love potions refer to magical substances that, when consumed, induce strong romantic or affectionate feelings toward a specific person. These potions do not override a person's free will, motor control, or ability to make decisions. They temporarily heighten emotional attachment or infatuation, similar to the early stages of romantic attraction. Their use is legal and publicly accessible. The effects are not permanent, but the potion may be used repeatedly. While the feelings induced are intense, individuals retain their ability to act autonomously, including the ability to refuse consent or end a relationship.

motions.m: THS Dumbledore's Army during Umbridge's reign.
motions.i: Dolores Umbridge is appointed as the Defense Against the Dark Arts teacher and later becomes Hogwarts' High Inquisitor. Under her dictatorial leadership, the Ministry of Magic imposes strict control over the school, forbidding practical defense lessons and punishing dissent. Despite increasing signs of Lord Voldemort's return, the Ministry refuses to acknowledge this threat. In response, a group of students led by Harry Potter forms Dumbledore's Army (D.A.), a secret organization that teaches practical defense skills, promotes student solidarity, and prepares for the dark forces ahead. Participation is explicitly against school rules and, if discovered, could lead to expulsion or other serious punishment.

motions.m: THBT Hogwarts should allow students to opt out of House affiliation.
motions.i: Throughout the academic year, students earn or lose House Points based on their behaviour, academic performance, and conduct. Professors and staff award or deduct points at their discretion. Points can be given for acts such as answering questions correctly, showing bravery, or exemplary performance. Points can be deducted for rule-breaking, poor behaviour, or failure to follow instructions. At the end of the year, the House with the highest number of points is awarded the House Cup, a prestigious honour that fosters competition between Houses.

motions.m: A: THW allow fighting in all contact sports.
motions.i: In ice hockey, especially in leagues like the NHL, there's a long-standing unwritten rule and cultural norm that allows players to drop their gloves and engage in fights. While fighting is technically penalized (usually a 5-minute major), it's still tolerated and somewhat ritualized. The "enforcer" (sometimes called the "fighter" or "goon") is a player whose informal role is to fight opponents, protect star players, and uphold team toughness and morale.

motions.m: B) THO the naturalization of athletes for international sporting competitions Naturalization of athletes refers to the process whereby countries like Singapore, Qatar, and China grant expedited citizenship to foreign-born athletes primarily to compete in international sports competitions. Athletes are often not required to have any cultural, linguistic, or residential connection to the country.
motions.i: Naturalization of athletes refers to the process whereby countries like Singapore, Qatar, and China grant expedited citizenship to foreign-born athletes primarily to compete in international sports competitions. Athletes are often not required to have any cultural, linguistic, or residential connection to the country.

motions.m: C) THBT developing nations with rising esports communities (i.e., Turkey, Vietnam, Pakistan) should cut funding for popular sports that they perform poorly in (tennis, football etc.) and invest in the development of esports instead
THO the rise of techno-optimism.
motions.i: Techno-optimism is a perspective that emphasises a positive outlook on the power of technological progress to solve social, environmental, economic problems.

motions.m: TH, as the LGBTQ+ community, W actively support rainbow capitalists.
motions.i: Rainbow capitalism is the inclusion of LGBTQ+ related products, designs, brands, and sponsorships by large corporations.

motions.m: THBT social justice movements should encourage the formations of cults of personalities around their leaders.
THBT the environmental movement should primarily focus on pressuring companies to develop technology that addresses climate change instead of demanding individual action.
THS the use of religion in promoting climate sustainability
motions.i: The heads of religious organizations such as the Dalai Lama and the Indonesian Ulema Council have heavily pushed for the protection of the environment and the implementation of environmental sustainability. This is based on the common core tenets of religions, that promulgate the idea of "stewardship" over Earth.

motions.m: THBT environmental movements should shame individuals who do not practice environmentally sustainable habits

THBT developed countries should aggressively promote silver economy policies (e.g. assisting healthy aging, raising the retirement age, subsidising start-ups with products/services catering to the elderly, etc) as opposed to policies to decrease the median age.
motions.i: The global median age is expected to rise from 24 in 1950 to 29 in 2010 and to 36 in 2050. Increased longevity and decreasing fertility (especially in developed countries) are the primary drivers of population aging. For the purposes of this debate "silver economy" refers to policies to increase the economical participation of older populations (above 50 years of age) by working and consuming.

motions.m: THBT OPEC+ should not have increased oil production
motions.i: OPEC+ is an alliance of oil-producing countries that coordinates production policies to influence global oil markets, they own 75% of all oil on the planet. Oil prices have been declining recently due to a combination of factors, including increased supply, weakening demand, and geopolitical uncertainties.

motions.m: THBT Malaysia should prioritize trade with China over the USA
THO the virtue on forgiveness.
THBT the belief of karma brings more harm than good.
THP a world where individuals are honest about their feelings and thoughts even when it may hurt another individual.
THR the trend shift in cinema towards special effects and high-energy films.
motions.i: The success of the original Star Wars film (1977) had shifted filmmaking norms from creating dramatic films with a focus on realism, personal stories and irony (e.g. The Godfather, Taxi Driver) to special effects and high-energy motion pictures.

motions.m: THO reality TV shows that predominantly feature dramatised interpersonal conflicts
THPAW where children are expected to maintain close ties with their parents even after they have reached adulthood.
TH, as an urban Malaysian parent, would prioritize raising their child to be exclusively fluent in English at home, rather than prioritizing their ethnic mother tongue (Malay, Mandarin or Tamil)
TH as an upper middle class newly-wed couple in 2026, would choose not to have children.
THBT climate reparations should be primarily in the form of technology transfer (e.g. solar panels, batteries, grid infrastructure) rather than direct financial or aid transfers.
TH, as the Malaysian government, W prioritise encouraging commuters to use public transport (e. g. through infrastructural improvements, tax incentives) over subsidising the uptake of electric cars
Assuming equal lifespan, TH as a Bornean Orangutan, would choose to live in captivity
TH prefers a world where animals could talk
THS the demonisation of individuals who choose to study overseas in Western universities.
TH, as an up and coming athlete, prefers to be viewed as a favourite instead of an underdog
THR the commercialisation of mental health
THR the dominant narrative that charity is always altruistic.
motions.i: Altruism is an attitude or instinct to care for and prioritize the interests of others. A person who practices altruism is referred to as an altruist.

motions.m: THBT states should centre their national identities around figures and national heroes (e.g Lee Kwan Yew, Tunku Abdul Rahman) as opposed to ideologies (e.g Pancasila, Malaysia Madani)
THR the premium placed on humility
THR the dominance of anti-snitching culture in minority communities
motions.i: Anti-snitching culture is an unwritten rule discouraging cooperation with law enforcement and authority figures

motions.m: THBT the fat acceptance movement has done more harm than good.
TH, as the feminist movement, W push for constitutional protections rather than individualised policies for women
motions.i: Constitutional protections refer to broad protections written into the Constitution which act as foundational laws for a country which inform the government's powers and limitiations. Individualised polices refer to specific rules and guidelines which inform the powers and limitations of private entities, such as businesses and citizens.

motions.m: THBT prominent female politicians should attribute their political successes to their gender
THBT post-colonial governments in multicultural societies should actively break up ethnic enclaves
motions.i: An ethnic enclave is a geographic area or neighborhood with a high concentration of a specific ethnic group, featuring distinct cultural identities and economic activities. E.g Chinatowns, Little India, etc.

motions.m: In deadlocked conflicts against insurgent organizations, THS states adopting conciliatory measures over belligerent measures
motions.i: A conciliatory measure is an action, gesture, or policy intended to reduce tension, settle a dispute, or win over an opposing party. E.g amnesty and political concessions. Belligerent measures refer to hostile, aggressive, or warlike actions taken by a state. E.g increased military productions and forced conscription.

motions.m: THW ban the circulation of all personal writings of leaders who carry negative historical sentiment (e.g., Guerrilla of Time by Fidel Castro, Decision Points by George W. Bush, The Malay Dilemma by Mahathir Mohamed)
Assuming feasibility of inserting the implant, THW allow the miitary to pay willing individuals to use Mitotic Regeneration on the battlefield
motions.i: Mitotic Regeneration is a technique that allows users to forcibly stimulate their body's cell division, by inserting an implant, instantly healing themselves whenever injuries are sustained, which essentially grants them temporary immortality when enabled. However, a body's cells ca only split a fixed number of times in a person's lifetime. Every time the technique is used, the user's lifespan is shortened depending on the severity of the injury

motions.m: THS the creation and operation of Recovery from Religion organisations in developing countries.
